Coverage rule:

- SUPERSEDED_NOTED = generic or conflicting legacy representation removed from mainline and traced to canonical replacement.

======================================================================
4. EXECUTIVE PRODUCT DEFINITION
======================================================================
Shadow Empire is a cross-platform Creator Operating System that converts creator/founder intent into governed AI-presenter video production through a sovereign orchestration layer, a media-factory production spine, and an adaptive local/hybrid/cloud execution model.
It is simultaneously:
- a creator operating system
- a founder control plane
- a builder-grade orchestration constitution
- a Claude handoff artifact for future implementation without architectural drift

======================================================================
5. MISSION, AGENDA, AND STRATEGIC INTENT
======================================================================
Mission: Build a self-running AI creator studio that works from Windows/macOS operator surfaces while safely escalating heavy or premium work to cloud or specialized nodes.
Agenda: registry-first truth, patch-only dossier state, directorial governance, creator-mode acceleration, builder readability, smooth Claude continuation, zero-hallucination contracts.

======================================================================
6. PROBLEM DEFINITION AND OPPORTUNITY SPACE
======================================================================
Problem: creator workflows are fragmented across research, scripting, voice, avatar, editing, publishing, analytics, and optimization systems; the orchestration layer is usually under-governed and under-observable.
Opportunity: Shadow Empire turns those fragments into one governed machine with routing, budget legality, hardware safety, creator modes, dashboard control, and feedback/evolution loops.

======================================================================
7. PRODUCT CONTEXT AND JOURNEY
======================================================================
Canonical creator path:
Creator Intent -> Kubera Cost Gate -> Yama Policy Gate -> Krishna Intent Sanction -> Ganesha Neural Index -> Aruna Flow Builder -> Director Selection -> Skill Chain -> Vayu Resource Gate -> Execution Workers -> Governance / Tribunal -> Output / Publish / Feedback

======================================================================
8. PRODUCT VISION AND OPERATING PHILOSOPHY
======================================================================
- registry truth outranks prose
- patch-only dossier truth outranks mutable blobs
- neural routing outranks ad-hoc orchestration
- Vein handoff deltas outrank prose handoffs
- creator modes accelerate lawful execution by pruning unnecessary overhead
- Vayu is an active hardware negotiator, not passive telemetry
- product accessibility comes from adaptive acceleration, not from weakening the system

======================================================================
9. SCOPE, BOUNDARIES, AND NON-NEGOTIABLE DIRECTION
======================================================================
In scope: creator OS, founder/creator modes, dashboard/control plane, director/skill runtime, hybrid switching, provider integration, AI presenter pipeline, publish-learning loop, deployment blueprint.
Out of scope in this PRD: inventing unsupported exact values; bypassing Kubera/Yama/Vayu; replacing decision packets with guesswork.

======================================================================
10. FOUNDER MODE / CREATOR MODE / RUNTIME MODE LOGIC
======================================================================
--- Founder Mode vs Creator Mode Specification ---
| Dimension       | Founder Mode                                                  | Creator Mode                                 |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| purpose         | governance, override, cost control, release authority         | content creation, iteration, analytics usage |
| default screens | Central Command, Observatory, Settings/Registry, Founder Mode | Channels, Pipelines, Analytics, Library      |
| override power  | yes, scoped by policy and audit                               | no premium/legal overrides                   |
| route defaults  | tribunal/gov-safe when risk present                           | fast/standard creator-optimized routes       |
| cost controls   | can inspect and approve premium                               | cannot bypass Kubera                         |
| analytics depth | full RCA, cost, incident, route trace                         | creator KPIs + recommendations               |
| review controls | approval queue, veto, release channels                        | submit for review, consume feedback          |
| debug / RCA     | full access                                                   | limited surface                              |

--- User / Operator / Founder / Builder / AI Reader Model ---
- Layman overview reader: uses Executive Product Definition, creator model, dashboard overview, deployment posture.
- Creator/operator reader: uses Creator Dimension, Creator Mode, screen pack, workflow maps.
- Founder/admin reader: uses Founder Mode, budget/policy matrices, release blueprint, decision packet blocking semantics.
- Builder/engineer reader: uses registries, module rows, schemas, route catalog, environment/deployment matrices.
- Claude/AI reader: uses Final Claude Handoff Layer, Builder Acceptance Pack, decision packet workflow.

--- Founder vs Creator Cost Permission Matrix ---
| Mode     | Can View Estimate | Can Request Token | Can Approve Token | Can View Cost Ledger    | Can Override Cost Gate | Can See Provider Usage | Can Adjust Thresholds | Notes       |
| -------- | ----------------- | ----------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------------- | --------------------- | ----------- |
| Founder  | Yes               | Yes               | Yes               | Yes                     | Yes                    | Yes                    | Yes                   | full        |
| Creator  | Yes               | No                | No                | Scoped / estimated only | No                     | Limited                | No                    | self view   |
| Builder  | Yes               | No                | No                | Yes                     | No                     | Yes                    | Yes in dev/staging    | system view |
| Operator | Limited           | No                | No                | Limited                 | No                     | No                     | No                    | ops view    |

--- Founder Mode and Creator Mode End-to-End Product/System Flows ---
48.1 Founder Mode
Purpose: command-center mode for architecture control, premium approval, override, release decisions, and incident response.

| Capability | Founder Mode | Notes |
| ---------- | ------------ | ----- |
| view_all | Yes | full dossier, telemetry, route trace, cost ledger |
| route_override | Yes | may force route under policy legality |
| queue_control | Yes | hold, resume, reroute, DLQ replay |
| governance_override | limited by Yama non-overridables | cannot bypass policy illegality or consent rules |
| premium_approval | Yes | may approve HYBRID_PREMIUM, CLOUD_PREMIUM, ENTERPRISE |
| threshold_adjustment | Yes | bounded and audited |
| registry_hot_reload | Yes with validator pass | build-time and ops-time tool |
| release/promotion | Yes | owns build-vs-release gate |

Founder workflow:
creator/founder command -> Crew Composer -> route preview -> budget estimate -> governance preview -> approve / override / reject -> execution -> Mission Control / RCA -> release / rollback decision.

48.2 Creator Mode
Purpose: safe daily production mode for content operations without dangerous architecture mutation.

| Capability | Creator Mode | Notes |
| ---------- | ------------ | ----- |
| manage_channels | Yes | channel/pipeline/schedule/persona scope only |
| request_token | Conditional | may request, cannot self-approve premium beyond budget mode rules |
| override_cost_gate | No | Kubera remains hard gate |
| override_policy | No | Yama remains hard gate |
| content_control | Yes | scripts, variants, schedules, packaging |
| queue_control | limited | own jobs only |
| analytics_view | Yes | creator-facing KPIs, performance, recommendations |
| provider_usage_view | limited | summarized view only |

Creator workflow:
intent -> creator_mode route default -> preview cost/resource impact -> execute permitted route -> review deliverables -> publish / schedule -> analytics feedback -> optimization suggestions.

48.3 Founder vs Creator route defaults
| Area | Founder Default | Creator Default |
| ---- | --------------- | --------------- |
| build / registry work | tribunal / admin route | not exposed |
| content production | standard/hybrid route with optional premium approval | fast/standard route based on creator mode |
| incident recovery | direct RCA + replay tools | view incident summary, limited rerun |
| deployment | visible | hidden |
| threshold tuning | visible | hidden |

48.4 Fast-track creator handoff rule
If `creator_mode_id` is present and `mode_status=verified`, Ganesha skips open-ended search and loads the mode’s fixed `route_id`. Only Yama, Kubera, and Vayu remain hard guards.

11. PLATFORM TARGETS AND EXECUTION MODES
======================================================================
--- Platform Targets and Execution Modes ---
| Mode            | Primary OS / Environment     | Main Use                                             | Heavy Work Posture           | Notes                                     |
| --------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| local           | Windows/macOS                | control plane, creator work, light inference         | Vayu-gated local workers     | Default operator surface                  |
| hybrid          | Windows/macOS + cloud        | mixed creator + premium/runtime acceleration         | selective offload            | Mainline recommended posture              |
| cloud           | cloud/serverless + workers   | heavy media production, async provider orchestration | cloud/native                 | Production spine                          |
| untrusted_local | restricted local environment | limited preview / safe read modes                    | no sensitive heavy execution | Reduced secrets + reduced mutation rights |

--- Windows/macOS Customer Device Retune ---
Windows/macOS customer-device retune restored from DNA:
- The job is to make the runtime sovereign on the hardware customers actually own.
- Windows and macOS are the customer-device mainline.
- Linux-first or VDI-specific assumptions are preserved only as optional backend / acceleration-node doctrine unless explicitly mainline-supported elsewhere.
- Cross-platform viability must be judged against average creator hardware, not only server-grade infrastructure.

--- GitHub, Local AI, Web AI, and Hybrid Portability Model ---
| Portability Surface | Required Truth | Local AI | Web / Hosted AI | GitHub Repo | Notes |
| ------------------- | -------------- | -------- | --------------- | ----------- | ----- |
| Registry layer | plain-text YAML/JSON + schema validation | Yes | Yes | Yes | core portability spine |
| .md skill army | markdown + manifests + tests | Yes | Yes | Yes | no platform-specific code embedded |
| Kernel runtime | Python with OS adapter boundaries | Yes | Partial | Yes | hosted AI may invoke through API shell instead of local daemon |
| Dashboard / control plane | API-first DTO contracts | Yes | Yes | Yes | browser-safe plus local shell wrappers |
| Media runtime | local / hybrid / cloud depending provider and hardware | Partial | Yes | Yes | heavy media may offload |
| Plugin host | bounded manifests + permissions | Yes | Partial | Yes | browser-automation plugins vary by environment |
| Micro-agent host | runtime-managed bounded helpers | Yes | Yes | Yes | limited by runtime capabilities |
| Mission Control / Chronos | DAG exporter + telemetry APIs | Yes | Yes | Yes | must not assume one deployment shape |

Repository governance:
- `main` = stable registry truth.
- `develop` = next build wave.
- `skills/*` branches allowed only for generated packs and validator-backed diffs.
- pull request checks must run schema validation, dependency cycle checks, skill contract validation, and build-blocker matrix checks.

--- Hardware Class and OS Compatibility Matrix ---
This closes the gap between abstract hardware classes and the actual user agenda.

| Hardware Profile | Canonical Label | OS | Safe Modes | Offload Requirement | Directors Safe | Skills Safe | Heavy Modules | Builder Verdict |
| ---------------- | --------------- | -- | ---------- | ------------------- | -------------- | ----------- | ------------- | --------------- |
| Windows laptop / 8 GB RAM / standard CPU / 256 GB SSD | WIN_STD_8G | Windows 10/11 | local degraded, hybrid, cloud | avatar render, premium TTS, heavy visual generation, large vector jobs, batch analytics | yes (decision plane) | yes for text/intel/distribution/system skills; heavy media only via offload | AV render, Wav2Lip, large FFmpeg, image-heavy packages require offload | supported only with aggressive unload, cache discipline, and offload-first heavy media |
| Windows laptop / 16 GB RAM / stronger CPU / optional entry GPU | WIN_MID_16G | Windows 10/11 | local, hybrid, cloud | premium avatar or long renders may still offload | yes | most skills; medium media local, heavy media hybrid | final long-form render, premium avatar, multi-variant swarms | mainstream creator target |
| Windows workstation / 32 GB+ / stronger CPU/GPU / larger SSD | WIN_PRO_32G | Windows 10/11 | local, hybrid, cloud | offload optional for premium-quality deadlines | yes | yes across core + many expansion paths | enterprise-scale or high concurrency still hybrid/cloud | high-end creator/founder station |
| macOS Intel / 16 GB+ | MAC_INTEL_16G | macOS Intel | local, hybrid, cloud | GPU-heavy paths may prefer hybrid/cloud | yes | text, orchestration, moderate media | some local GPU paths vary by tooling | supported with adapter caveats |
| Apple Silicon / M4 class | MAC_AS_M4 | macOS Apple Silicon | local, hybrid, cloud | premium avatar / provider-dependent steps may still use cloud; local Metal-friendly acceleration preferred | yes | broad coverage, strong local reasoning/media support where libraries exist | provider-bound avatar and large render clusters still hybrid/cloud | fully supported target class |
| untrusted local browser / borrowed machine | UNTRUSTED_LOCAL | any | untrusted_local only | all premium, mutation, secret-bearing, and heavy write flows blocked | view/control only | read-only or constrained helper flows | nearly all heavy or secret-bearing modules blocked | safe degraded inspection mode |

OS adapter rules:
- Windows: Job Objects, priority classes, WMI / Performance Counters / DXGI where supported.
- macOS: IOKit / Metal-facing telemetry bindings or validated adapters.
- cross-platform law: routing, dossier, governance, thresholds, and Vayu semantics remain identical; only adapters differ.

======================================================================
13. NEURAL / VEINS / WORKER ROUTER ARCHITECTURE
======================================================================
--- Empire Neural Model ---
Purpose: make routing, activation, and bounded execution explicit instead of implicit.

Neural layers:
N0 Intent Layer -> creator/founder command intake
N1 Governance-legal Layer -> Kubera + Yama sanctioning
N2 Sanction / Arbitration Layer -> Krishna / Vishnu / tribunal locks
N3 Neural Index Layer -> Ganesha classifies intent, matches vectors, filters phase, applies Three-Blade select, binds tools/providers
N4 Flow Builder Layer -> Aruna shapes chain and activation envelopes
N5 Director Activation Layer -> council/domain directors approve or redirect
N6 Skill Activation Layer -> skill contracts load from registry / .md schema / hot cache
N7 Peripheral Execution Layer -> plugins, micro-agents, workers
N8 Vein Commit Layer -> patch engine persists lawful state deltas
N9 Feedback Layer -> publish, analytics, optimization, historical incidents

Neural path maps:
- Intent -> director activation: Creator Intent -> Kubera -> Yama -> Krishna -> Ganesha -> Aruna -> council/director
- Directors -> skills: director verdict -> skill chain -> skill loader -> execution workers
- Skills -> plugins: skill tool policy -> plugin registry -> sandboxed binding
- Skills -> micro-agents: skill spawn policy -> helper class -> TTL-bounded execution
- Workers -> dossier writes: worker output -> Vein handoff envelope -> patch engine -> shard write -> audit

--- Veins Module and Vein Handoff Register ---
Veins are the state-bearing circulation system of Shadow Empire.
They are not generic memory. They are the lawful route by which data, artifacts, approvals, and audit deltas move between layers.

Canonical Vein handoff contract from source:
{
  "from_node": "Valmiki",
  "to_node": "Saraswati",
  "reason": "narrative_approved",
  "fields_read": ["artifacts.research", "artifacts.outline"],
  "fields_written": ["artifacts.script", "governance.strategy_score"],
  "next_skill_chain": ["M-041", "M-042", "M-043"],
  "approval_required": true
}

Vein ownership and mutation law:
- Krishna may place arbitration locks only on covered namespaces, never global full-system lock.
- Directors pass dossier deltas, not raw prose.
- Durable truth writes go through patch engine, not ad-hoc mutation.
- Micro-agents and plugins have bounded shard rights only.
- Artifact lineage writes are separate from approval/governance verdict writes.

| Vein Register | Purpose                                          | Primary Writers      | Primary Readers              | Notes                                       |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| meta.db       | identity, mission, mode, creator/founder context | Kernel               | All layers                   | creator_mode_id and route context live here |
| routing.db    | route decisions, lane selection, fallbacks       | Ganesha, Aruna       | Directors, dashboard         | route trace root                            |
| state.db      | workflow and actor state                         | orchestrator         | dashboard, workers           | state machine source                        |
| hybrid.db     | budget tokens, mode switches, Vayu legality      | Kubera, Vayu         | dashboard, kernel            | cost + hardware truth                       |
| governance.db | policy verdicts, approvals, tribunal results     | Yama, tribunal       | dashboard, routes            | veto root                                   |
| artifacts_*   | research/script/voice/avatar/video outputs       | execution subsystems | QA, publish, dashboard       | versioned artifacts                         |
| analytics.db  | retention, CTR, sentiment, shares, views         | analytics engine     | optimizer, creator dashboard | feedback loop                               |
| audit.db      | incidents, RCA, decision lineage                 | Chitragupta          | founder/builder              | append-only                                 |

Vein Decision Packet closure rule:
- If a per-state mutation point lacks exact numeric timeout or retry, the gap moves to State Decision Packet Register.
- If a per-screen dossier diff field is not source-final, it moves to Dashboard Decision Packet Register.

--- Worker Router and Neural Dispatch Model ---
Worker Router is a named neural subsystem in v8.
Purpose: convert route legality + workload class + Vayu posture + provider policy into worker assignment.

| Task Class                   | Priority | Default Lane  | Worker Class                        | Checkpoint Owner   | DLQ Owner            | Notes                         |
| ---------------------------- | -------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| interactive_creator_light    | P1       | Fast          | local-control worker                | local orchestrator | operator diagnostics | UI-driven creator tasks       |
| interactive_founder_override | P0       | Tribunal      | control-plane privileged worker     | Krishna/Vishnu     | founder diagnostics  | highest governance priority   |
| script_generation            | P1       | Standard      | local/hybrid script worker          | script engine      | content ops          | premium allowed after Kubera  |
| voice_generation             | P1       | Standard      | voice worker / cloud voice provider | voice engine       | audio ops            | chunk-safe route              |
| avatar_generation            | P2       | Standard      | cloud avatar worker                 | avatar engine      | visual ops           | async callback/poller         |
| render_final                 | P2       | Standard      | render worker / Fargate             | render engine      | render ops           | never heavy-final in Lambda   |
| analytics_pull               | P3       | Fast          | analytics worker                    | analytics engine   | growth ops           | retry friendly                |
| plugin_ingest                | P2       | Standard      | plugin worker                       | plugin host        | plugin ops           | sandbox required              |
| micro_agent_helper           | P2       | Fast/Standard | micro-agent worker                  | parent skill       | parent subsystem     | TTL-bound                     |
| batch_optimization           | P4       | Fast/Standard | background optimizer                | optimizer          | growth ops           | throttled under Vayu pressure |

Worker-router decision algorithm:
1. Read route envelope
2. Verify budget legality (Kubera)
3. Verify policy legality (Yama)
4. Resolve lane + route + allowed providers (Ganesha)
5. Build chain + required worker classes (Aruna)
6. Ask Vayu whether local execution is safe by workload class
7. If unsafe, offload to cloud/specialized worker according to route fallback table
8. Emit worker task envelope + checkpoint ownership + audit seed
9. On failure, route retry/DLQ according to task class matrix

--- Empire Neural Model Patch ---
The Neural Model is restored as a first-class integrated system rather than implicit route prose.

53.1 Neural layers
1. Intent Cortex
   - normalizes raw creator/founder input into task envelope
2. Kernel Spine
   - Kubera -> Yama -> Krishna/Vishnu -> Ganesha -> Aruna
3. Director Council Layer
   - decision/arbitration/jurisdiction/approval
4. Skill Activation Layer
   - family/archetype skill chains
5. Peripheral Extension Layer
   - plugins and micro-agents
6. Worker Execution Layer
   - local/hybrid/cloud worker classes
7. Dossier / Vein Return Layer
   - append-only audit + sharded artifact truth
8. Control Plane Reflection Layer
   - dashboard, Mission Control, Chronos, RCA

53.2 Canonical neural paths
- creator intent -> intent envelope -> Ganesha domain route -> director selection -> skill chain -> worker -> dossier patch -> dashboard reflection
- founder override -> override packet -> Yama legality -> Krishna arbitration -> route override -> audit append
- analytics feedback -> analytics write-back -> optimization director -> skill mutation request -> registry-backed future route adjustment

53.3 Neural path registry
| Path | Source | Sink | Mandatory Envelope |
| ---- | ------ | ---- | ------------------ |
| N-01 | creator intent | Ganesha | task_envelope |
| N-02 | Ganesha | director | route_decision_envelope |
| N-03 | director | skill chain | approval packet + dossier delta intent |
| N-04 | skill | plugin / micro-agent | bounded subtask envelope |
| N-05 | skill / worker | dossier | dossier_delta |
| N-06 | dossier | dashboard | dashboard DTO |
| N-07 | incident | Chitragupta / founder | incident packet |

--- Veins Module and CONTENT_DOSSIER Flow Register ---
Veins are the formal handoff bloodstream between thinking, execution, and state.

54.1 Vein classes
- discovery_vein
- research_vein
- narrative_vein
- production_vein
- distribution_vein
- analytics_vein
- governance_vein

54.2 Vein ownership by stage
| Vein | Primary Owners | Read Shards | Write Shards |
| ---- | -------------- | ----------- | ------------ |
| discovery_vein | Narada / research skills | metadata, routing, analytics | artifacts/research, routing |
| research_vein | Vyasa / research council | artifacts/research, metadata, analytics | artifacts/research/current |
| narrative_vein | Valmiki / script stack | research, metadata, creator prefs | artifacts/script, governance |
| production_vein | Saraswati / Maya / Indra / Agni | script, assets, hybrid, resources | voice, avatar, video, audit |
| distribution_vein | Garuda / Kubera / Yama | video, metadata, policy, budget | metadata bundle, publish status |
| analytics_vein | Oracle / Archivist | publish stats, route, cost | analytics, optimization artifacts |
| governance_vein | Yama / Ravana / Bheeshma / Krishna | any needed shards under legality | governance verdicts, audit |

54.3 Mutation points by workflow stage
- research complete -> write `/artifacts/research/current`
- script approved -> write `/artifacts/script/current` + `/governance/script_score`
- voice synthesized -> write `/artifacts/voice/versioned[]`
- avatar rendered -> write `/artifacts/avatar/versioned[]`
- render complete -> write `/artifacts/video/versioned[]`
- publish complete -> write `/state/publish` + `/analytics/input`
- analytics pull complete -> write `/analytics/current` + `/artifacts/optimization[]`

54.4 Actor-type read/write matrix
| Actor Type | Read Scope | Write Scope |
| ---------- | ---------- | ----------- |
| director | declared jurisdictions only | governance, routing, approved deltas |
| skill | manifest-declared input shards | declared output shards only |
| plugin | manifest-declared temporary or bounded output | declared plugin output only |
| micro-agent | parent-granted shards only | temp or delegated output only |
| dashboard | DTO and read models only except privileged actions | commands through API only |

54.5 Canonical vein handoff schema
```json
{
  "handoff_id": "uuid",
  "vein": "narrative_vein",
  "from_node": "VALMIKI",
  "to_node": "SARASWATI",
  "reason": "script_approved_for_voice",
  "route_id": "ROUTE-STANDARD-001",
  "read_shards": ["artifacts/research", "artifacts/script"],
  "write_shards": ["artifacts/voice", "governance"],
  "fields_read": ["/artifacts/script/current", "/metadata/channel_profile"],
  "fields_written": ["/artifacts/voice/pending", "/governance/voice_request"],
  "approval_required": true,
  "next_skill_chain": ["AU-VOI-014", "AU-VOI-015"]
}
```

--- Worker Router Deterministic Runtime Contract ---
55.1 Queue classes
- interactive_control
- creator_standard
- heavy_media
- analytics_batch
- incident_replay
- forensic_research
- tribunal_review

55.2 Worker classes
- cpu_text_worker
- cpu_io_worker
- local_gpu_worker
- render_fargate_worker
- cloud_function_worker
- browser_automation_worker
- analytics_batch_worker
- incident_replay_worker

55.3 Deterministic router flow
1. parse route envelope
2. expand dependency set from registry
3. compute feasible candidates (provider + exec tier + worker class)
4. remove policy-illegal candidates
5. remove budget-illegal candidates
6. remove environment-illegal candidates
7. rank remaining candidates by quality floor, deadline, then minimum cost
8. apply switch hysteresis
9. reserve budget token if required
10. assign worker queue and checkpoint policy
11. emit route_decision_envelope and cost_event_envelope

55.4 Lane arbitration
- Fast Lane: low-risk, cached, low-resource, no premium or heavy GPU by default.
- Standard Lane: normal creator production.
- Tribunal Lane: expensive, conflicted, externally visible, or policy-sensitive.

55.5 DLQ and resume ownership
| Failure Class | Primary Owner | Resume Path |
| ------------- | ------------- | ----------- |
| render timeout | Vayu + render engine | checkpoint resume or reroute |
| callback failure | provider adapter + Chitragupta | replay or founder review |
| policy veto | Yama / governance | remodify path only |
| budget exhaustion | Kubera | founder approval or downgrade |
| queue saturation | Ganesha + Vayu | defer, downgrade telemetry, reroute |

--- Master Module and Component Contract Expansion ---
This section restores full-system module meaning so the PRD does not remain only a cost/execution patch.

| Module Family | Strategic Role | Core Subcomponents | Primary Interfaces | Inputs | Outputs | Ownership | Deployment Significance | QA / Failure Surfaces |
| ------------- | -------------- | ------------------ | ------------------ | ------ | ------- | --------- | ----------------------- | --------------------- |
| Kernel Spine | legality, routing, orchestration, resource gating, audit | Kubera, Yama, Krishna, Vishnu, Ganesha, Aruna, Vayu, Chitragupta | task_envelope, route_decision_envelope, budget_token, dossier_patch | intent, mode, budget, policy, telemetry | lawful route, gating verdicts, incident events | Constitutional layer | mandatory for every mode | budget illegality, policy veto, stale lease, unsafe hardware, backpressure misrouting |
| CONTENT_DOSSIER / Veins | sharded durable truth and handoff bloodstream | shard schemas, patch engine, saga validator, ownership registry, audit appenders | dossier_delta, handoff_envelope, artifact_version, audit_event | every stage output | versioned state, append-only audit | Chitragupta + stage owners | mandatory for all runtime and UI state | shard collision, ambiguous ownership, mixed-path mutation, blind overwrite |
| Skill OS Runtime | execution layer for 218 core skills + 25 expansion | family manifests, 8 archetype templates, skill factory, validator, loader, resolver, hot cache | .md skill contract, manifest index, route map, dependency map | route call + dossier subset + provider policy | structured output contract + next node hint | skill factory + owner directors | enables local AI, web AI, and GitHub portability | missing input schema, cycle dependency, undeclared tools, invalid fallback |
| Director Runtime | decision, arbitration, approval, escalation, quality governance | 30 director contracts, council maps, jurisdiction matrix, shadow pairs, override scopes | director_call, approval packet, debate packet, escalation packet | skill outputs, governance scores, context, dossier state | approvals, vetoes, remodify instructions, route changes | 6 councils | command layer across all modes | decision ambiguity, conflicting ownership, missing rationale, unsafe override |
| Plugin Host | bounded extension surface | plugin registry, permission matrix, sandbox, enable/disable/update/remove, event hooks | plugin manifest, plugin IO contract, plugin event schema | plugin call from skill or dashboard | bounded result, audit, telemetry | plugin governor + owning directors | extends repo without core drift | auth leak, unsafe tool spread, version mismatch, UI surfacing drift |
| Micro-Agent Host | short-lived bounded helpers under director/skill control | spawn controller, TTL monitor, rights matrix, kill escalation, parent binding | micro_agent_task, checkpoint envelope, kill event | parent request + limited shard rights | structured helper output or escalate/fail | parent skill/director | improves bounded concurrency and decomposition | runaway spawn, rights bleed, orphan state, stale helper result |
| Media Factory | concrete production spine | script_engine, voice_engine, emotion_engine, avatar_engine, qa_engine, render_engine, assethunter, deliver_engine, publish adapters, analytics writers | media task contracts, asset manifest, render manifest, publish payload | topic, research, script, voice, avatar, assets | deliverable media + metadata + analytics artifacts | media runtime + directors | primary product value path | QA failure, provider TTL, heavy render contention, missing assets |
| Dashboard / Control Plane | operator command surface and machine visibility | Mission Control, Creator Dashboard Ops, Founder God Mode, Crew Composer, Prompt Engineer utility, cost surfaces, queue views, dossier diff, route trace | REST/JSON DTOs, dashboards, alert feeds, route trace, deep-link params | registry, telemetry, dossier, analytics, queues | commands, approvals, overrides, views, export state | Krishna + Aruna + founder roles | makes local/hybrid/cloud operation usable | widget drift, permission leak, stale ETA, unbound alert paths |
| Deployment / Packaging | runtime portability and promotion | local shell, web shell, manifests, environment configs, release channels, rollback packs, CI rules | env files, secret bindings, manifests, release artifact manifest | registries, packaged workers, dashboard, media runtime | runnable local/hybrid/cloud distributions | build system | determines mass-market viability | missing env contract, broken secrets posture, non-portable shell choice |

--- Delegation and Responsibility Model ---
| Actor          | Decides                | Routes               | Validates                    | Executes                       | Audits            | Overrides            | Owns State                            |
| -------------- | ---------------------- | -------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| Kubera         | budget legality        | no                   | spend legality               | no heavy work                  | budget log        | no creator override  | hybrid.db                             |
| Yama           | policy legality        | no                   | policy legality              | no heavy work                  | governance audit  | hard veto            | governance.db                         |
| Krishna/Vishnu | arbitration / sanction | indirect via Ganesha | cross-domain sanity          | no heavy media                 | incident lineage  | founder-scoped       | meta/routing/state via locks          |
| Ganesha        | no                     | yes                  | route legality context       | no                             | route trace       | no spend override    | routing.db                            |
| Aruna          | flow build             | chain assembly       | context completeness         | no                             | flow trace        | no                   | routing/state                         |
| Directors      | domain judgment        | indirect             | domain quality/approval      | no heavy work                  | domain decisions  | scoped per policy    | declared namespaces                   |
| Skills         | no                     | no                   | task-level output validation | bounded execution              | skill trace       | none beyond contract | declared shards only                  |
| Workers        | no                     | no                   | no                           | heavy compute / external calls | execution metrics | none                 | artifact shards only via patch engine |
| Chitragupta    | no                     | no                   | incident/RCA integrity       | no                             | yes               | no                   | audit.db                              |

--- Contract, Policy, and Governance Requirements ---
- Prompt–Registry Interlock: signed registry instance outranks prompt prose.
- Registry truth law: nothing exists at runtime unless anchored in registry/contract.
- Dossier truth law: durable state only through patch engine.
- Kubera spend law: premium routes require signed budget token; creator cannot bypass.
- Yama policy law: non-compliant actions are vetoed before routing.
- Director law: directors decide, do not perform heavy compute.
- Skill contract law: skills execute bounded contracts; they do not self-authorize premium/legal bypass.
- Hybrid switching law: local/hybrid/cloud switching obeys budget, policy, quality, deadline, hardware, and route legality.
- Vayu lifecycle: profile -> admit / defer / offload -> checkpoint -> release.
- Akasha namespace law: no cross-domain bleed by default.
- Chronos weighted DAG law: every node declares resource_weight and critical-path meaning.
- Asset admission law: no media path without asset_manifest or synthetic_asset_waiver.

15. COMPONENT AND SUBSYSTEM BREAKDOWN
======================================================================
--- Component and Subsystem Breakdown via v8 estates ---
The following mainline estates and subsystem packs remain preserved:
- Director estate
- Skill estate
- Plugin system contract

- Dashboard screen contract pack
- Interface and event schema register
- Route catalog and approval matrix
- Environment matrix and feature matrix
- Deployment and production blueprint

--- Plugin System Full Lifecycle and Registry Contract ---
51.1 Plugin categories
- ingestion
- browser automation
- media tooling
- cloud/provider adapters
- repository automation
- analytics utilities
- QA / policy
- UI utilities

51.2 Canonical plugin manifest
```yaml
plugin_id: PLG-YTDLP-001
name: yt_dlp_bridge
category: ingestion
version: 1.0.0
permissions:
  network: true
  file_read: true
  file_write: limited
  browser_control: false
  premium_provider_access: false
allowed_exec_tiers: [local, hybrid]
input_contract_ref: schemas/plugins/yt_dlp_input.json
output_contract_ref: schemas/plugins/yt_dlp_output.json
route_bindings: [ROUTE-CM-030]
skill_bindings: [YT-FOR-090]
screen_surfaces: [/providers, /routes]
failure_codes: [ERROR_PLUGIN_001, ERROR_PLUGIN_002]
```

51.3 Lifecycle
install -> validate -> register -> enable -> healthcheck -> observe -> update / disable -> retire.

51.4 Failure isolation rules
- plugin failure may not crash kernel lane.
- plugin failures emit error glossary codes and incident links.
- plugin auth failures must degrade to legal fallback route where available.
- plugin version mismatch blocks enablement until validator passes.

--- Micro-Agent System Full Taxonomy and Lifecycle ---
52.1 Allowed micro-agent classes
| Class | Purpose | Parent Type | Typical TTL | Writes Allowed |
| ----- | ------- | ----------- | ----------- | -------------- |
| transcript_extractor | bounded transcript parsing | skill | short | artifacts/research temp only |
| trend_scout | bounded market scan | skill/director | short | artifacts/research temp only |
| dto_builder | dashboard projection shaping | dashboard or control plane | short | none outside view cache |
| policy_auditor | preflight policy mirror check | governance | short | governance/temp only |
| asset_probe | candidate asset lookup | assethunter skill | short | artifacts/assets temp only |
| budget_probe | premium legality preview | Kubera/route estimator | short | hybrid/temp only |
| callback_verifier | webhook integrity check | provider adapter | short | audit only |
| qa_sampler | bounded scoring helper | QA gate | short | governance/temp only |
| persona_packager | packaging helper | creator pipeline | medium | metadata temp only |
| replay_preparer | incident replay scoper | Chitragupta | medium | audit/replay temp only |

52.2 Rights model
- memory rights: scoped read of parent-granted shards only.
- tool rights: only tools explicitly inherited from parent contract.
- patch rights: no cross-namespace writes; only temp or delegated target writes.
- TTL expiry forces kill and audit append.
- spawn forbidden in untrusted_local for premium or secret-bearing flows.

52.3 Spawn policy
- parent must declare `may_spawn_micro_agents`.
- spawn envelope includes parent_id, granted_shards, granted_tools, ttl_sec, kill_condition.
- orphaned micro-agent results are rejected if parent no longer owns route.

Closed critical-path skill examples:
SKILL-001 TOPIC_DISCOVERY_ENGINE | Input: niche, trend_data, audience_profile | Output: ranked_topic_list | Owner: Narada | Approval: Chanakya | Route_Map: research_intake -> viability_review | Tier: local/hybrid/cloud | Worker: light_cpu | Retry: bounded 2x on source failure | Acceptance: ranked output with source provenance
SKILL-002 TOPIC_VALIDATOR | Input: ranked_topic_list | Output: viable_topic_set with demand/competition/longevity scores | Owner: Chanakya | Veto: Krishna | Execution_Critical_YN: TRUE
SKILL-007 STORY_ARCHITECT | Input: approved topic + persona + style | Output: story_blueprint | Owner: Valmiki | Acceptance: hook + narrative flow + emotional arc
HOOK_GENERATOR / ARGUMENT_BUILDER / STORY_STRUCTURE | under Script Intelligence family | route-bound to script critical path
voice_style_selection / audio_loudness_normalization | owner Saraswati | output voice config / normalized audio manifest
mrbeast_edit_formula / final_render_pipeline | owner Agni | output edit plan / final render package
youtube_upload / metadata/tag/pinned_comment generation | owner Garuda | output publish package
ctr_analysis / strategy_adjustment | owner Oracle/Archivist | output optimization artifacts
pipeline_trigger / alert_system | owner Aruna/Chitragupta | output stage transition + alert event

Patch law:
No critical-path skill may remain packetized for input contract, output contract, retry policy, recovery policy, tool policy, observability hooks, or acceptance test if that skill affects route legality or release readiness.
Where exact skill rows are not source-complete, keep skill row and append BDP-SKILL-CONTRACT-<ID>.

- therefore only execution-critical and demonstrative rows are closed here
- remaining estate-wide population requires skill factory + registry generation path, not hallucinated manual fill

| DP-SKILL-M-017-TOOL_POLICY            | M-017 | tool_policy            | Source matrix gives owner, purpose, upstream, downstream, gate, escalation, trigger, but not a fully final row-level contract field for this item. | No           | Depends on skill criticality | Use family/archetype default contract and validator gate until row finalized. | Generate skill_contracts/M-017.md + validator test row for tool_policy.            |
| DP-SKILL-M-017-OBSERVABILITY_HOOKS    | M-017 | observability_hooks    | Source matrix gives owner, purpose, upstream, downstream, gate, escalation, trigger, but not a fully final row-level contract field for this item. | No           | Depends on skill criticality | Use family/archetype default contract and validator gate until row finalized. | Generate skill_contracts/M-017.md + validator test row for observability_hooks.    |
| DP-SKILL-M-017-ACCEPTANCE_TEST        | M-017 | acceptance_test        | Source matrix gives owner, purpose, upstream, downstream, gate, escalation, trigger, but not a fully final row-level contract field for this item. | No           | Depends on skill criticality | Use family/archetype default contract and validator gate until row finalized. | Generate skill_contracts/M-017.md + validator test row for acceptance_test.        |
| DP-SKILL-M-018-INPUT_CONTRACT_SCHEMA  | M-018 | input_contract_schema  | Source matrix gives owner, purpose, upstream, downstream, gate, escalation, trigger, but not a fully final row-level contract field for this item. | No           | Depends on skill criticality | Use family/archetype default contract and validator gate until row finalized. | Generate skill_contracts/M-018.md + validator test row for input_contract_schema.  |

======================================================================
20. REPOSITORY / .MD SKILL ARMY RUNTIME ARCHITECTURE
======================================================================
--- .md Skill File Workflow Specification ---
Canonical .md skill file structure derived from skill repository architecture in source corpus:
skills/<family>/<capability>/<skill_id>_<slug>.md

Required blocks:
1. YAML header
2. Purpose / when-to-use
3. Inputs required
4. Step-by-step execution
5. Output format
6. Tool integrations / bindings
7. Policy / escalation block
8. Examples / tests block
9. Versioning metadata

Mandatory header fields:
- skill_id
- name
- family
- archetype
- owner_director
- supervising_director
- escalation_director
- runtime_modes
- tool_policy
- patch_policy
- approval_gate
- ttl
- max_concurrency
- version
- registry_sync_status

Lifecycle:
factory manifest -> generated .md -> validator -> registry sync -> hot cache refresh -> runtime activation
Manual edit restrictions:
- No manual edit of generated contract fields without validator pass
- Manual examples/tests allowed in declared block only
- Registry remains source of runtime truth; .md is the human/LLM-facing playbook shadow of the runtime contract

--- Markdown Skill Repository Architecture and Runtime ---
The final product outcome remains a lightweight .md skill-repository-based Agentic Army. This section converts that from implied posture into a concrete repository/runtime contract.

45.1 Canonical repository tree
```text
shadow-empire/

      strategy.yaml

      hardware_groups.yaml

    strategy/

```

45.2 Canonical .md skill file schema
```md
# SHADOW EMPIRE SKILL MODULE
SKILL_ID: SC-SCR-041
SKILL_NAME: Hook Engineering Engine
FAMILY: Script Intelligence
ARCHETYPE: Creative
OWNER_DIRECTOR: Valmiki
SUPERVISING_DIRECTOR: Krishna
ESCALATION_DIRECTOR: Bheeshma
PRIORITY_BAND: P1
RESOURCE_WEIGHT: 1
ALLOWED_EXEC_TIERS: [local, hybrid, cloud]
PREFERRED_EXEC_TIER: local
WORKER_CLASS: cpu_text_worker
TOKEN_REQUIRED: false
ZERO_COST_FALLBACK_SKILL: SC-SCR-001

## PURPOSE
Generate first 8-12 second hook variants aligned to creator persona, platform, and retention policy.

## INPUT_CONTRACT
- /artifacts/research/current
- /metadata/channel_profile
- /governance/creative_policy

## OUTPUT_CONTRACT
- /artifacts/script/hook_variants[]
- /governance/hook_score

## EXECUTION_STEPS
1. load dossier shards
2. validate prerequisites
3. execute deterministic/generative logic
4. run immune checks
5. emit dossier delta

## TOOL_POLICY
allowed_tools:
  - local_llm
  - prompt_template_engine
forbidden_tools:
  - premium_voice
  - browser_automation

## GOVERNANCE
approval_gate: hook_quality_gate
veto_power: false
failure_modes:
  - missing research shard
  - policy mismatch
  - low novelty score
recovery_policy:
  - retry_once_with_alternate_prompt
  - escalate_to_director_if_second_failure

## VEIN_HANDOFF
next_nodes:
  - SC-SCR-042
  - SC-SCR-043
handoff_mode: sequential

## TESTS
- contract_test: tests/skills/test_sc_scr_041.yaml
- golden_examples: tests/skills/golden/sc_scr_041/*.json
```

45.3 Loader contract
- loader discovers skills only from `skill_registry.yaml` and verified manifest paths.
- no filename scanning without registry membership.
- loader validates front-matter, contract block, route map, tool policy, and tests before activation.
- hot reload allowed only through signed registry diff + validator pass.
- local AI runtime may preload only mode-relevant packs; web AI runtime may stream packs lazily by route.

45.4 Versioning and packaging
- every skill file carries `skill_version`, `manifest_version`, `contract_hash`.
- repo packaging targets:
  - `starter_local_pack`
  - `hybrid_creator_pack`
  - `premium_avatar_pack`
  - `forensic_research_pack`
- GitHub portability requirement: skills are pure text contracts; runtime-specific code lives outside skill files.

45.5 Claude handoff rule for skill generation
Claude generates skill files only after:
1. family manifests exist
2. archetype templates exist
3. route registry exists
4. provider policy exists
5. validator exists
6. test harness template exists

--- 2026 Claude Ecosystem / Zero-Infrastructure Runtime Truth ---
Source-backed 2026 Claude ecosystem / zero-infrastructure runtime truth restored:
- Runtime environment explicitly preserved in source: Claude Web, Claude Code CLI, Claude Cowork, Local Skill Repository.
- Infrastructure model explicitly preserved in source: Zero Infrastructure.
- System scale explicitly preserved in source: 30 Sovereign Directors, 218 Skill Modules, 112 Claude Agents, 146 Claude Skills, 9000+ Plugin Ecosystem.
- Architectural meaning restored: directors think/decide/route; skills are markdown skill modules; agents perform micro-tasks and tool coordination; plugins form a large but governed extension surface.

--- Plugin System Contract ---
| Plugin ID | Name                       | Category           | Purpose                                     | Host               | Route Binding                             | Config Contract           | Fallback                     | UI Surface                      |
| --------- | -------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| PLG-001   | yt-dlp                     | media ingest       | video/audio extraction                      | plugin worker      | route-bound via ingest skills             | local plugin config       | manual URL ingest            | screened in Library/Diagnostics |
| PLG-002   | Selenium / Playwright      | browser automation | upload / comment / browser action           | plugin worker      | founder-controlled publish/comment routes | session/cookie secret set | manual founder action        | Founder Mode / Pipelines        |
| PLG-003   | Stable-Diffusion-WebUI-API | visual generation  | Maya 8K render dependency in source example | specialized worker | visual-production routes                  | API binding               | fallback to local/alt render | Creator/Workspace               |
| PLG-004   | FFmpeg bridge              | render assembly    | media merge/encode/crop                     | render worker      | render routes                             | binary/service binding    | defer render                 | Diagnostics / Pipelines         |

Plugin lifecycle matrix:
| Lifecycle Step | Owner            | Law                                                |
| -------------- | ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| install        | Founder/Builder  | registry write + sandbox validation required       |
| enable         | Founder/Operator | permission + route binding + healthcheck must pass |
| disable        | Founder/Operator | route fallback must exist or action blocked        |
| update         | Builder          | version pin + regression validation required       |
| remove         | Founder/Builder  | dependency impact check + route cleanup required   |

--- Micro-Agent System Contract ---
Source-backed role: micro-agents are bounded helper sub-nodes under directors/skills, not sovereign free agents.
| Micro-Agent Class    | Parent                     | Purpose                        | Memory Rights              | Tool Rights            | TTL             | Kill Condition     | Audit Visibility       |
| -------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------ | -------------------------- | ---------------------- | --------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------- |
| Transcript Extractor | Research / ingest skills   | extract transcript / subtitles | read-only research shards  | yt-dlp/ffmpeg          | task-bounded    | on success/failure | Diagnostics + audit.db |
| Trend Scout          | Narada / research skills   | pattern/trend scan             | read analytics/research    | web/search tools       | task-bounded    | quota/policy/veto  | audit.db               |
| Widget DTO Builder   | Dashboard services         | screen DTO shaping             | read-only dashboard shards | none beyond DTO mapper | request-bounded | response emitted   | dashboard traces       |
| Policy Auditor       | Yama/Shakuni-linked skills | preflight check                | read policy/governance     | policy engine only     | request-bounded | hard veto or pass  | audit.db               |

Rules:
- Spawn only when parent skill/director contract allows it.
- No independent premium spend rights.
- No unbounded shard writes.
- Kill on TTL expiry, parent completion, veto, or containment failure.
- All micro-agent actions produce audit lineage with parent reference.

--- Micro-Agent Decision Packet Register ---
| Packet ID          | Owner             | Field               | Why source is not final                                                                 | Blocks Build | Blocks Release               | Fallback                                          | Follow-up Artifact                     |
| ------------------ | ----------------- | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------ | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| DP-MA-SPAWN-MATRIX | Micro-Agent Layer | spawn_policy_matrix | Corpus supports bounded helper sub-nodes but not exhaustive class-by-class spawn table. | No           | No                           | Use parent skill allowlist                        | Produce micro_agent_spawn_matrix.yaml. |
| DP-MA-SHARD-RIGHTS | Micro-Agent Layer | shard_access_table  | Exact shard rights by class are not fully row-finalized.                                | No           | Yes for unrestricted release | read-only except parent-approved ephemeral writes | Produce micro_agent_shard_rights.yaml. |

--- Provider Credential, OAuth, Callback, Rotation, and Secret Scope Model ---
| Provider | Credential Type | Auth Model | Secret Scope | Local Storage Posture | Cloud Storage Posture | Rotation Model | Callback / OAuth Truth | Build or Release Blocking |
| -------- | --------------- | ---------- | ------------ | --------------------- | --------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------- | ------------------------- |
| OpenRouter | API key | bearer key | route-limited | OS keychain / encrypted local vault | secrets manager | manual + schedule | no callback; usage polling only | build blocker if missing for premium LLM paths |
| Anthropic direct | API key | bearer key | provider-scoped | local vault | secrets manager | manual + schedule | no callback | release blocker only if direct path enabled |
| Google Gemini / AI Studio | API key or OAuth depending surface | bearer or OAuth | provider-scoped | local vault | secrets manager | schedule + revoke | callback not required; webhook only if external automation added | release blocker if chosen surface requires OAuth and env not finalized |
| ElevenLabs | API key | bearer key | voice provider scope | local vault | secrets manager | schedule + manual revoke | callback optional; polling allowed | build blocker for premium voice route |
| HeyGen | API key + callback secret | bearer + webhook/callback verification | avatar provider scope | local vault for dev | secrets manager for prod | schedule + manual revoke | callback verification required for unattended release | unattended release blocker if callback contract unresolved |
| YouTube Data API | OAuth client + refresh token | OAuth2 | channel-owner scope | encrypted local token store | secrets manager / KMS-backed store | refresh + revoke | exact callback URI env-specific | unattended publish blocker if callback URI / refresh lifecycle unresolved |
| YouTube Analytics API | OAuth2 | shared with channel auth | analytics scope | encrypted local token store | secrets manager | refresh + revoke | no additional callback beyond OAuth | release blocker only for autonomous analytics write-back |
| GitHub | PAT or GitHub App | token / app | repo-scoped | encrypted local token store | secrets manager | manual or app rotation | no callback mandatory | build blocker if repo automation enabled |
| Playwright / browser automation | account cookies/session tokens if needed | browser/session | automation scope | isolated browser profile | isolated automation store | short-lived / session reset | callback not required | release blocker if login automation is part of unattended path |

50.1 Decision packets still required
- exact OAuth callback URIs by environment (dev/local/hybrid/cloud) -> Provider Decision Packet PVD-001.
- HeyGen callback verification secret propagation -> PVD-002.
- enterprise spend approval integration with external billing system -> PVD-003.

Provider decision packet template:
```md
Packet ID: PVD-001
Field: youtube_oauth_callback_uri
Owning subsystem: Provider/Auth
Why unresolved: environment-specific deployment origin not frozen in source corpus
Missing evidence: final domain list for local desktop shell, web shell, cloud hosted shell
Legal placeholder: CALLBACK_URI_TEMPLATE
Builder/founder decision required: choose desktop custom-scheme vs localhost callback vs hosted callback per environment
Follow-up artifact: oauth_matrix.yaml
Blocks build: no
Blocks release: yes for unattended publish
Fallback until finalized: manual token seed in founder mode only
```

--- Task Distribution and Worker Allocation Matrix ---
See Worker Router section for algorithm. This matrix binds task class to queue/worker/ownership.
| Queue Class      | Primary Tasks                                       | Worker Pool                     | Preemption                            | Checkpoint Owner    | DLQ Owner            |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------- |
| Q-P0-GOV         | Founder override, legal veto, tribunal              | control-plane privileged        | never preempted by lower class        | Krishna/Vishnu      | Founder Diagnostics  |
| Q-P1-INTERACTIVE | script, creator commands, dashboard-triggered tasks | local/hybrid interactive        | preempts batch                        | parent subsystem    | Operator Diagnostics |
| Q-P2-MEDIA       | voice, avatar, render                               | cloud/specialized media workers | preemptible only by P0/P1             | media subsystem     | Media Ops            |
| Q-P3-BACKGROUND  | analytics pull, optimization, indexing              | background workers              | throttled heavily under Vayu pressure | optimizer/analytics | Growth Ops           |

| ROUTE-STANDARD-001  | default creator production        | local_light_worker;cloud_lambda_light | local,hybrid,cloud | LAPTOP_STD      | No             | Yes                    | Ganesha + domain director                     | 2                   |

--- Worker Topology Map ---
| Worker Class       | Runs In      | Purpose                                              | Queue Class      | Scaling Posture                  | Offline Capable | Secrets Required |
| ------------------ | ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------- | -------------------------------- | --------------- | ---------------- |
| local_light_worker | local        | CPU-light orchestration / DTO / research / scripting | Q-P1-INTERACTIVE | no autoscale; host constrained   | Yes             | No               |
| local_gpu_worker   | local        | voice/image/QA when Vayu permits                     | Q-P2-MEDIA       | host constrained; Vayu hard gate | No              | No               |
| cloud_lambda_light | cloud        | short async events, analytics, routing helpers       | Q-P1/Q-P3        | serverless scale                 | No              | No               |
| cloud_fargate_gpu  | cloud        | final render / media heavy workloads                 | Q-P2-MEDIA       | service autoscale                | No              | No               |
| special_accel_node | hybrid/cloud | GPU-heavy avatar, Wav2Lip, visual compute            | Q-P2-MEDIA       | manual or metric-based scale     | No              | No               |

--- Hybrid Switch Policies ---
| Family / Route Class | Switch-Up Conditions                                   | Switch-Down Conditions                      | Hysteresis Window         | Retry in Local Before Offload                  | Provider Switch Penalty |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| Research / scripting | quality_floor unmet locally or local model unavailable | budget warning active + cache hit available | 1 completed local attempt | 1 retry local before offload                   | medium                  |
| Voice generation     | voice quality floor or latency floor unmet             | premium cap warning / XTTS success          | 2 jobs                    | 1 local chunk fallback                         | high                    |
| Avatar generation    | cloud route required or local avatar path unavailable  | local draft avatar available                | 1 job                     | no repeated offload within same failure window | high                    |
| Render final         | Vayu blocks local heavy render or deadline too tight   | deadline relaxed + local safe               | 1 job                     | no local reattempt after thermal hard stop     | high                    |
| Analytics batch      | quota or schedule rules suggest cloud batch            | local safe and no urgency                   | 1 cycle                   | defer before offload                           | low                     |

--- Untrusted Local Policy Matrix ---
| Actor        | Secrets Allowed                                    | Mutation Rights                                   | Premium Allowed | Tool Rights | UI Visibility                    |
| ------------ | -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------- | ----------- | -------------------------------- |
| Directors    | read-only except safe local planning               | meta/routing read; no sensitive governance writes | No              | No          | founder hidden / creator limited |
| Skills       | bounded read + safe local write only where allowed | safe shards only                                  | No              | No          | no privileged tools              |
| Plugins      | high-risk plugins blocked                          | read-only or disabled                             | No              | No          | browser automation blocked       |
| Micro-Agents | bounded read only                                  | parent-approved ephemeral writes only             | No              | No          | hidden unless diagnostics        |
| Dashboard    | view-only critical telemetry                       | no dangerous mutation                             | N/A             | N/A         | layman-safe / creator-safe only  |

--- Dependency Environment Matrix ---
| Dependency                  | Allowed Environments | Fallback                        | Primary Use                |
| --------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| Gemini / long-context model | local,hybrid,cloud   | cached research / alt local LLM | premium research synthesis |
| ElevenLabs                  | hybrid,cloud         | XTTS-v2                         | premium voice              |

| FFmpeg                      | local,hybrid,cloud   | defer or reduced local render   | render path                |

Navigation tree:

- Every screen must define empty, loading, error, and partial-data states.
- Every action must declare confirmation and audit write requirements.
- Alert-to-root navigation must land on incident / route / dossier diff / worker details depending on origin.

--- Dashboard API Contracts ---
```md
/dashboard_api_contracts:
  GET /cost/ledger:
    returns: [job_id, route_id, stage_id, provider, est_cost, actual_cost, token_id, selected_cost_tier, selected_exec_tier, retry_count, timestamp]
  GET /budget/tokens:
    returns: [token_id, scope, owner_mode, issuer, max_spend, state, provider_allowlist, expiry_ts]
  POST /budget/tokens/request:
    payload: [scope, route_id, provider_allowlist, max_spend, justification]
  POST /budget/tokens/{id}/approve:
    payload: [approver, decision, notes]
  POST /cost/estimate:
    payload: [creator_mode_id, route_id, quality_floor, deadline, candidate_constraints]
  GET /providers/usage:
    returns: [provider, billing_unit, quota_usage, est_spend, retries, callback_failures, fallback_count]
```

49.3 Mandatory widget states
- every widget declares: empty_state, loading_state, error_state, refresh_sec, role_guard, drilldown_target.
- alert-to-root law: every alert must deep-link to exactly one RCA starting point (`incident_id`, `route_id`, `dossier_id`, or `token_id`).

```md
POST /api/v1/budget-tokens/request
POST /api/v1/routes/{route_id}/estimate
POST /api/v1/overrides/route
POST /api/v1/queues/{queue_id}/replay
GET  /api/v1/mission-control/dag
GET  /api/v1/providers/{provider}/health
GET  /api/v1/skills/{skill_id}/contract
```

--- Observability, Mission Control, RCA, and Incident Navigation Model ---
56.1 Telemetry model
- logs: structured events by component
- metrics: queue depth, provider health, route latency, budget burn, render timings, callback failures
- traces: route_id / dossier_id / incident_id correlated path
- alerts: severity-classed alerts with deep links

56.2 Click-to-root law
Every major alert must deep-link to one root object:
- dossier diff
- route trace
- incident timeline
- provider health record
- budget token

56.3 Incident taxonomy
| Severity | Meaning | Example |
| -------- | ------- | ------- |
| info | non-blocking signal | low telemetry lag |
| warning | degraded but recoverable | quota nearing cap |
| high | production-impacting, recoverable | callback failure, queue saturation |
| critical | build/release or active production blocker | policy illegality, shard corruption, unsafe hardware interrupt |

56.4 STAR-style RCA packet
- situation
- task
- action
- result
- error_code
- owner
- impact_scope
- root_cause
- remediation
- prevention_rule
