26. DELEGATION AND RESPONSIBILITY MODEL
======================================================================
| Actor          | Decides                | Routes               | Validates                    | Executes                       | Audits            | Overrides            | Owns State                            |
| -------------- | ---------------------- | -------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| Kubera         | budget legality        | no                   | spend legality               | no heavy work                  | budget log        | no creator override  | hybrid.db                             |
| Yama           | policy legality        | no                   | policy legality              | no heavy work                  | governance audit  | hard veto            | governance.db                         |
| Krishna/Vishnu | arbitration / sanction | indirect via Ganesha | cross-domain sanity          | no heavy media                 | incident lineage  | founder-scoped       | meta/routing/state via locks          |
| Ganesha        | no                     | yes                  | route legality context       | no                             | route trace       | no spend override    | routing.db                            |
| Aruna          | flow build             | chain assembly       | context completeness         | no                             | flow trace        | no                   | routing/state                         |
| Directors      | domain judgment        | indirect             | domain quality/approval      | no heavy work                  | domain decisions  | scoped per policy    | declared namespaces                   |
| Skills         | no                     | no                   | task-level output validation | bounded execution              | skill trace       | none beyond contract | declared shards only                  |
| Workers        | no                     | no                   | no                           | heavy compute / external calls | execution metrics | none                 | artifact shards only via patch engine |
| Chitragupta    | no                     | no                   | incident/RCA integrity       | no                             | yes               | no                   | audit.db                              |

======================================================================
27. CONTRACTS, POLICIES, AND GOVERNANCE RULES
======================================================================
--- Contracts, Policy, and Governance Requirements ---
- Prompt–Registry Interlock: signed registry instance outranks prompt prose.
- Registry truth law: nothing exists at runtime unless anchored in registry/contract.
- Dossier truth law: durable state only through patch engine.
- Kubera spend law: premium routes require signed budget token; creator cannot bypass.
- Yama policy law: non-compliant actions are vetoed before routing.
- Director law: directors decide, do not perform heavy compute.
- Skill contract law: skills execute bounded contracts; they do not self-authorize premium/legal bypass.
- Hybrid switching law: local/hybrid/cloud switching obeys budget, policy, quality, deadline, hardware, and route legality.
- Vayu lifecycle: profile -> admit / defer / offload -> checkpoint -> release.
- Akasha namespace law: no cross-domain bleed by default.
- Chronos weighted DAG law: every node declares resource_weight and critical-path meaning.
- Asset admission law: no media path without asset_manifest or synthetic_asset_waiver.

--- Cost Tier Taxonomy ---
System-wide cost tier enum. Every director, skill, route, provider, worker choice, and dashboard cost surface binds to one of these tiers.

| Tier            | Meaning                                                         | Budget Token Required | Quality / Deadline Assumption               | Fallback / Downgrade Path         |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| ZERO_LOCAL      | No external premium spend; local-only or already cached         | No                    | Best-effort / degraded acceptable           | Use free/local fallback           |
| LOW_LOCAL       | Low-cost local or low-cost API usage; strict spend minimization | No                    | Quality floor moderate, deadline moderate   | Downgrade to ZERO_LOCAL           |
| HYBRID_STANDARD | Mixed local + cloud standard provider path                      | Conditional           | Normal quality floor and standard deadlines | Downgrade to LOW_LOCAL or reroute |
| HYBRID_PREMIUM  | Mixed execution with premium provider for critical stage only   | Yes                   | Higher quality floor and tighter deadline   | Fallback to HYBRID_STANDARD       |
| CLOUD_PREMIUM   | Cloud-heavy premium execution                                   | Yes                   | High quality and throughput targets         | Fallback to HYBRID_PREMIUM        |
| ENTERPRISE      | High-authority or multi-provider high-cost execution            | Yes + founder-scoped  | Maximum reliability/compliance targets      | Fallback to CLOUD_PREMIUM or hold |

Kubera governs legality of tier escalation. Founder Mode may request or approve premium tiers according to the Mode Cost Permissions Matrix. Creator Mode cannot override Kubera or Yama.

--- Cost Optimisation Matrix ---
| Dimension                | Rule                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Objective                | Select minimum-cost candidate meeting quality_floor and deadline                                                        |
| Inputs                   | quality_floor, deadline, budget_remaining, policy_verdict, hardware_capability, route lane, provider health, cache hits |
| Primary constraint order | policy legality -> budget legality -> environment legality -> quality floor -> deadline floor -> cost ranking           |
| Candidate ranking        | lowest acceptable cost first, then lower switch penalty, then lower retry-cost risk, then lower callback risk           |
| Escalation rule          | if no acceptable candidate, create cost escalation or founder review instead of silent premium burn                     |

--- Cost Policy ---
```yaml
cost_policy:
  objective: min_cost_meeting_quality_and_deadline
  legal_tiers: [ZERO_LOCAL, LOW_LOCAL, HYBRID_STANDARD, HYBRID_PREMIUM, CLOUD_PREMIUM, ENTERPRISE]
  hard_stops:
    - policy_verdict != PASS
    - budget_token_required_but_missing
    - selected_env_forbidden_for_actor
  router_order:
    - compute_feasible_candidates
    - remove_illegal_candidates
    - apply_cache_reuse_bonus
    - rank_by_cost_then_quality_then_deadline
    - emit_estimate_and_selected_tier
  escalation:
    no_candidate: founder_or_tribunal_review
    premium_cap_hit: fallback_or_hold
```

--- Budget Token Schema ---
```json
{
  "token_id": "string",
  "scope": "route|provider|job|namespace",
  "owner_mode": "founder|creator|builder|operator",
  "issuer": "Kubera|Founder",
  "provider_allowlist": ["string"],
  "max_spend": "number",
  "currency": "string",
  "job_id": "string",
  "route_id": "string",
  "state": "REQUESTED|APPROVED|ISSUED|RESERVED|CONSUMED|REFUNDED|EXPIRED|REVOKED",
  "expiry_ts": "datetime",
  "notes": "string"
}
```

--- Budget Token State Machine ---
| State     | Meaning                            | Next                 | Notes                            |
| --------- | ---------------------------------- | -------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| REQUESTED | route or stage needs premium spend | APPROVED or REJECTED | Kubera/founder decides           |
| APPROVED  | budget authority grants spend      | ISSUED               | token materialized and logged    |
| ISSUED    | token available for reservation    | RESERVED or EXPIRED  | bound to provider/job            |
| RESERVED  | candidate provider selected        | CONSUMED or REFUNDED | holds spend window               |
| CONSUMED  | provider spend occurred            | CLOSED               | actual cost logged               |
| REFUNDED  | reservation released without spend | CLOSED               | cost ledger adjusts              |
| EXPIRED   | ttl exceeded                       | CLOSED               | reroute/founder review may occur |

======================================================================
28. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
======================================================================
- topic → research → script → emotion → voice → avatar → QA → render → package → publish → analytics → optimize
- creator modes and founder modes modify route selection and dashboard surfaces
- dashboard supports route trace, dossier diff, worker status, RCA, cost ledger, approvals, and channel/pipeline/persona management
- plugin and micro-agent systems operate only as bounded peripheral execution systems
- provider integrations support publish, analytics, voice, avatar, and optional research/automation integrations

======================================================================
29. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
======================================================================
- Windows/macOS operator-first
- local/hybrid/cloud portability
- patch-safe, audit-safe, route-traceable
- no hidden mutation paths
- heavy work isolated from control plane
- builder-readable and Claude-readable
- layman overview still navigable through layered sections and HTML paths

======================================================================
30. RUNTIME AND OPERATIONAL REQUIREMENTS
======================================================================
--- Runtime and Operational Requirements ---
- local runtime handles creator/control surfaces and safe light execution
- hybrid runtime is default recommendation
- cloud runtime hosts heavy media spine and async provider flows
- untrusted_local removes sensitive mutation or premium execution rights
- Vayu mandatory for local GPU/thermal-sensitive work
- worker-router mandatory for lawful queue assignment

--- Cost Optimisation & Deployment Tiers ---
This section cross-links the two missing hard systems: cost tiering (Zero→Premium) and execution tiering (local→hybrid→cloud→untrusted_local).

Cross-map summary:
| Runtime Object | Cost Tier Binding              | Execution Tier Binding         | Primary Owner                       | Dashboard Surface             |
| -------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Directors      | Director Cost Authority Matrix | Director Execution Matrix      | Founder/Kubera + director councils  | Founder Mode / Settings       |
| Skills         | Skill Cost Profiles            | Skill Execution Matrix         | owner_director + runtime validators | Builder / diagnostics         |
| Routes         | Route Cost Matrix              | Route Worker Matrix            | Ganesha / Aruna / route owners      | Central Command / Pipelines   |
| Providers      | Provider Cost Registry         | Provider Execution Policy      | Provider adapters + Kubera          | Provider Usage                |
| Workers        | Worker Router Cost Policy      | Worker Topology Map            | Worker Router + Vayu                | Diagnostics / Observatory     |
| Modes          | Mode Cost Permissions Matrix   | Environment / Feature matrices | Founder/Creator governance          | Founder/Creator mode surfaces |

Build law: the system is invalid if cost-tier and execution-tier truth are absent from any of the above runtime objects.

======================================================================
31. DEPENDENCY / INTEGRATION / CREDENTIAL MODEL
======================================================================
--- Dependency and Integration Model ---
Provider / plugin / worker dependencies are formalized in separate matrices below. Core integration law:
- route legality before provider selection
- provider selection before worker assignment
- worker assignment before Vein write rights
- artifact generation before publish/analytics loops

--- Provider / API / Secrets Matrix ---
| Provider                          | Purpose                          | Auth Type                      | Secret / Env Blueprint                    | Storage Posture                   | Fallback                                        | Open Items                                          |
| --------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| Google AI Studio / Gemini 1.5 Pro | Long-context planning / research | API Key                        | GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_API_KEY                  | local/cloud                       | Fallback to local LLM / cached research         | Quota/limits need DP                                |
| ElevenLabs                        | Premium voice synthesis          | API Key                        | ELEVEN_LABS_KEY                           | cloud/secrets manager             | Fallback to XTTS-v2                             | Chunk 4800 chars against 5000 limit                 |
| HeyGen                            | Avatar rendering                 | API Key + callback/poller      | HEYGEN_API_KEY                            | cloud/secrets manager             | Fallback to local avatar path / decision packet | Async render 20–35 min; callback/poller packetized  |
| YouTube Data API                  | Upload / metadata / channel ops  | OAuth 2.0                      | YOUTUBE_CLIENT_ID / YOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRET | local secure store + cloud secret | Manual upload / founder mode                    | Exact callback URI and refresh lifecycle require DP |
| YouTube Analytics API             | Post-publish analytics           | OAuth 2.0                      | YOUTUBE_ANALYTICS_CLIENT_ID / SECRET      | local secure store + cloud secret | Deferred analytics polling                      | Quota and refresh token lifecycle require DP        |
| yt-dlp                            | Video/audio ingestion            | No external auth by default    | N/A                                       | local                             | Manual URL ingest                               | Proxy requirement may apply for anti-ban posture    |
| Selenium / Playwright             | Browser automation               | Session / cookie / credentials | BROWSER_AUTOMATION_SECRET_SET             | local/cloud secure vault          | Manual founder/operator action                  | Permission and callback surfacing require DP        |
| XTTS-v2                           | Local TTS fallback               | Local model                    | N/A                                       | local                             | Use premium voice route                         | Vayu hardware gate applies                          |
| Wav2Lip                           | Lip sync / QA                    | Local model/service            | N/A                                       | local/specialized node            | Cloud QA fallback                               | GPU required                                        |
| FFmpeg                            | Render assembly                  | Local/service binary           | N/A                                       | local/cloud worker                | Defer render                                    | Never in Lambda for heavy final renders             |

--- Provider Decision Packet Register ---
| Packet ID           | Owner         | Field                         | Why source is not final                                                        | Blocks Build | Blocks Release             | Fallback                          | Follow-up Artifact                         |
| ------------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------ | -------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| DP-PROV-YT-CALLBACK | YouTube OAuth | callback_uri                  | Exact callback URI is deployment-environment specific and not fixed in corpus. | No           | Yes for release            | Manual founder token provisioning | Produce oauth_matrix.yaml per environment. |
| DP-PROV-YT-QUOTA    | YouTube APIs  | quota_thresholds              | Corpus cites quota posture but not final environment-specific thresholds.      | No           | No                         | Use safe conservative quota guard | Produce provider_quota_matrix.csv.         |
| DP-PROV-HG-CALLBACK | HeyGen        | callback/webhook verification | Corpus uses poller/callback patterns but not final verification payload.       | No           | Yes for unattended release | Use secure poller path            | Produce heygen_callback_contract.md.       |

--- Interface & Event Schema Register ---
| Schema                  | Producer -> Consumer                   | Core Shape                                                                               |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| route_decision_envelope | Kubera/Yama/Ganesha -> Aruna/Directors | {route_id,lane,allowed_providers,budget_token,policy_verdict,creator_mode_id}            |
| vein_handoff_envelope   | director/skill/worker -> patch engine  | {from_node,to_node,reason,fields_read,fields_written,next_skill_chain,approval_required} |
| worker_task_envelope    | router -> worker                       | {task_id,task_class,route_id,worker_class,input_refs,checkpoint_owner,dlq_owner}         |
| approval_request        | stage -> founder/tribunal/dashboard    | {approval_id,stage,owner,reason,risk_class,required_action,expiry}                       |
| artifact_metadata       | media subsystems -> artifacts shard    | {artifact_id,type,version,source_refs,quality_scores,state}                              |
| publish_request         | render/package -> deployment engine    | {video_id,channel_id,metadata_id,thumbnail_id,schedule_mode,oauth_profile}               |
| analytics_ingest        | platform -> analytics engine           | {video_id,retention,ctr,sentiment,shares,views,watch_time,ingest_ts}                     |
| optimization_writeback  | analytics -> optimizer / route advisor | {video_id,signals,recommendations,skill_impacts,mode_impacts}                            |
| dashboard_dto_generic   | API -> screen                          | {screen_id,widgets,tables,filters,actions,alerts,route_trace_refs}                       |
| plugin_event            | plugin host -> audit/dashboard         | {plugin_id,event_type,status,error_code,route_id,task_id}                                |

--- Cost Ledger Schema ---
```json
{
  "job_id": "string",
  "route_id": "string",
  "stage_id": "string",
  "provider": "string",
  "units": "number",
  "unit_type": "tokens|chars|minutes|requests|compute_seconds",
  "est_cost": "number",
  "actual_cost": "number",
  "token_id": "string",
  "retry_count": "integer",
  "selected_exec_tier": "local|hybrid|cloud|untrusted_local",
  "selected_cost_tier": "ZERO_LOCAL|LOW_LOCAL|HYBRID_STANDARD|HYBRID_PREMIUM|CLOUD_PREMIUM|ENTERPRISE",
  "timestamp": "datetime"
}
```

--- Cost Event Envelope ---
```md
cost_event_envelope:
  producer: Worker Router | Provider Adapter | Render Worker | Analytics Worker
  fields:
    - job_id
    - route_id
    - stage_id
    - provider
    - est_cost
    - actual_cost
    - selected_exec_tier
    - selected_cost_tier
    - token_id
    - retry_count
    - cache_hit
    - budget_warning_level
```

--- Route Decision Envelope Contract ---
```md
route_decision_envelope:
  job_id: string
  creator_mode_id: string
  founder_mode: boolean
  route_id: string
  lane: Fast|Standard|Tribunal
  selected_cost_tier: ZERO_LOCAL|LOW_LOCAL|HYBRID_STANDARD|HYBRID_PREMIUM|CLOUD_PREMIUM|ENTERPRISE
  selected_exec_tier: local|hybrid|cloud|untrusted_local
  candidate_set: [provider,worker,exec_tier]
  quality_floor: string
  deadline: string
  budget_token_required: boolean
  budget_token_id: string|null
  estimated_cost_min: string
  estimated_cost_max: string
  fallback_chain: string
  mode_override_owner: string|null
```

--- Cost Estimate Contract ---
```md
cost_estimate:
  estimate_id: string
  route_id: string
  stage_scope: full_job|stage_only
  feasible_candidates: list
  cheapest_candidate: provider+worker+exec_tier
  estimated_cost_min: string
  estimated_cost_max: string
  selected_cost_tier: string
  selected_exec_tier: string
  premium_required: boolean
  quality_floor: string
  deadline_floor: string
  estimate_confidence: low|medium|high
```

======================================================================
32. PARAMETER / THRESHOLD / CONTROL SURFACE SUMMARY
======================================================================
--- Parameter / Threshold / Control Surface Register ---
| Parameter / Control | Value / Logic                                                     | Notes                                               |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| Vayu GPU threshold  | VRAM hard marker / temp guard                                     | 78% VRAM marker in UI; thermal gating source-backed |
| HeyGen render       | 20–35 min async window                                            | callback/poller path                                |
| ElevenLabs chunking | 4800 chars against 5000 limit                                     | chunk-safe voice route                              |
| Wav2Lip             | 40ms/frame, confidence target around >=0.92                       | lip sync gate                                       |
| Realism             | SSIM >= 0.85 vs reference                                         | tribunal gate                                       |
| Voice quality       | MOS-SIM >=4.2, MOS-N >=4.0                                        | voice acceptance                                    |
| Weighted success    | retention 0.35, shares 0.25, sentiment 0.20, CTR 0.15, views 0.05 | optimization loop                                   |

--- Cost Thresholds ---
| Parameter                  | Value / Rule                                         |
| -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| max_premium_spend_per_job  | route-specific cap from Route Cost Matrix            |
| max_spend_per_day          | founder-controlled budget namespace cap              |
| max_spend_per_route        | route-specific ceiling                               |
| cost_hysteresis_window     | see Hybrid Switch Policies                           |
| provider_switch_penalty    | see Hybrid Switch Policies                           |
| retry_cost_cap             | 1 for premium-heavy routes unless founder override   |
| premium_fallback_delay     | 5m default before re-requesting premium after denial |
| budget_warning_threshold   | 80% of route/day cap                                 |
| budget_hard_stop_threshold | 100% of route/day cap                                |

--- QA Cost Retry Policy ---
| Cost Tier                   | Premium Retry Cap | Fallback to Lower Cost Allowed            | Escalation                                       | Fallback Path               |
| --------------------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------- |
| LOW_LOCAL / HYBRID_STANDARD | 2                 | Yes                                       | creator/operator path may continue after warning | route fallback              |
| HYBRID_PREMIUM              | 1                 | Yes                                       | founder notice if cap reached                    | premium->standard downgrade |
| CLOUD_PREMIUM / ENTERPRISE  | 1                 | No without founder/founder-approved token | tribunal/founder review                          | hold or reroute             |

--- Cost Cache Policy ---
| Artifact         | Cache Key                                  | TTL                                | Invalidation                 | Cost Objective                      |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| voice chunks     | script_hash + persona + emotion profile    | 7d or until persona change         | persona/version change       | avoid repeated premium TTS spend    |
| avatar renders   | script_hash + avatar profile + voice asset | 14d or until avatar/profile update | avatar/profile mutation      | avoid repeated premium render spend |
| research results | topic_hash + source manifest               | 24h to 7d                          | source freshness shift       | reduce API spend                    |
| analytics pulls  | video_id + interval                        | 1h to 24h                          | force refresh or major event | avoid needless quota burn           |

--- Cost KPI Specification ---
| KPI                    | Definition                                 | Dashboard Surface            |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------- |
| cost_per_video         | sum actual_cost per published video        | /cost-ledger, /analytics     |
| cost_per_minute        | actual_cost / video_minutes                | /cost-ledger                 |
| premium_ratio          | premium_spend / total_spend                | /provider-usage              |
| cost_vs_retention_gain | retention delta / additional spend         | /analytics + /cost-estimator |
| provider_retry_burn    | retry cost attributed to provider failures | /provider-usage, diagnostics |

======================================================================
33. WORKFLOW AND LIFECYCLE MODEL
======================================================================
--- Workflow and State Machine Model ---
Canonical happy path + branch-aware model:
intent -> legality -> routing -> flow build -> director verdict -> skill chain -> worker assignment -> Vein writes -> governance/QA -> package -> publish -> analytics -> optimization

| State                  | Entry Trigger                 | Owner             | Primary Next Legal State | Failure / Branch State        | Primary Vein Write |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------- | ------------------ |
| INTENT_RECEIVED        | creator/founder input         | kernel intake     | LEGALITY_CHECK           | REJECTED                      | meta.db            |
| LEGALITY_CHECK         | Kubera/Yama preflight         | kernel            | ROUTE_RESOLVED           | REJECTED                      | hybrid/governance  |
| ROUTE_RESOLVED         | Ganesha/Aruna output          | router            | DIRECTOR_ACTIVE          | REROUTE_PENDING               | routing.db         |
| DIRECTOR_ACTIVE        | director sanction/approval    | director          | SKILL_CHAIN_ACTIVE       | TRIBUNAL_REVIEW               | routing/governance |
| SKILL_CHAIN_ACTIVE     | skills loaded/working         | skill host        | WORKER_EXECUTION         | REMODIFY / RETRY              | state.db           |
| WORKER_EXECUTION       | bounded heavy execution       | worker            | QA_GOVERNANCE            | RETRY / DLQ                   | artifact shards    |
| QA_GOVERNANCE          | quality/policy validation     | tribunal/QA       | PACKAGE_READY            | REJECT / REMODIFY             | governance.db      |
| PACKAGE_READY          | final package built           | package/render    | PUBLISHING               | RERENDER                      | artifacts_video.db |
| PUBLISHING             | platform deployment           | deployment engine | PUBLISHED                | PUBLISH_RETRY / MANUAL_REVIEW | deploy state       |
| PUBLISHED              | video live / async processing | deployment engine | ANALYTICS_INGEST         | ROLLBACK_REVIEW               | publish state      |
| ANALYTICS_INGEST       | signals pulled                | analytics engine  | OPTIMIZATION_WRITEBACK   | ANALYTICS_RETRY               | analytics.db       |
| OPTIMIZATION_WRITEBACK | recommendations persisted     | optimizer         | CLOSED                   | REVIEW_REQUIRED               | analytics/audit    |

--- State Decision Packet Register ---
| Packet ID           | Owner         | Field                       | Why source is not final                                                   | Blocks Build | Blocks Release | Fallback                                   | Follow-up Artifact                      |
| ------------------- | ------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------- | ------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------- |
| DP-STATE-TIMEOUTS   | State Machine | per_state_timeout_values    | Corpus defines states and branches, but not every numeric timeout.        | No           | No             | Use subsystem default timeouts             | Produce state_machine_timeouts.yaml.    |
| DP-STATE-RETRY      | State Machine | per_state_retry_ceilings    | Retry behavior exists, but not all per-state ceilings are numeric-final.  | No           | No             | Use capped conservative retry by subsystem | Produce state_retry_matrix.csv.         |
| DP-STATE-VISIBILITY | State Machine | dashboard_visibility_matrix | Core dashboard-state linkage is defined, but not every state-screen cell. | No           | No             | Surface default route/status/RCA widgets   | Produce dashboard_state_visibility.csv. |

--- Route Catalog and Approval Matrix ---
| Route ID           | Purpose                           | Lane              | Mode Compatibility                            | Owner                             | Approval/Veto                         | Use Case                              | Fallback                                      |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------- | ----------------- | --------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| ROUTE-FAST-001     | low-risk fast path                | fast              | all modes except premium-only media           | Ganesha                           | no premium by default                 | low-risk content ops                  | ROUTE-STANDARD-001                            |
| ROUTE-STANDARD-001 | default creator production        | standard          | creator/founder                               | Ganesha + domain directors        | QA/policy gates                       | normal creator pipeline               | ROUTE-TRIBUNAL-001 or route-specific fallback |
| ROUTE-TRIBUNAL-001 | policy / premium / conflict route | tribunal          | founder, governance, conflicted creator tasks | Krishna/Vishnu + tribunal         | approval/veto heavy                   | premium/media/policy-conflicted tasks | route-specific fallback or reject             |
| ROUTE-CM-030       | youtube_forensic_deepdig          | standard/tribunal | CM-030                                        | Ganesha + research/forensic chain | depends on mode and provider legality | quad-stream deep research             | ROUTE-FAST-001 for transcript-only fallback   |

--- Route Decision Packet Register ---
| Packet ID             | Owner         | Field                       | Why source is not final                                                     | Blocks Build | Blocks Release                  | Fallback                        | Follow-up Artifact                  |
| --------------------- | ------------- | --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| DP-ROUTE-OWNER-MATRIX | Route Catalog | per_route_owner_matrix      | Corpus is strong on route laws, but not every row owner/veto pair is final. | No           | No                              | Use lane owner defaults         | Produce route_owner_matrix.csv.     |
| DP-ROUTE-VETO-MATRIX  | Route Catalog | per_route_veto_matrix       | Not every route has final veto row bound in source.                         | No           | Yes for release-governed routes | Apply Yama/tribunal defaults    | Produce route_veto_matrix.csv.      |
| DP-ROUTE-PROVIDER     | Route Catalog | route_to_provider_selection | Exact provider order by route remains partly variable by environment.       | No           | No                              | Use provider_registry fallbacks | Produce route_provider_matrix.yaml. |

======================================================================
34. HARDWARE COMPATIBILITY AND DEGRADED-MODE RULES
======================================================================
--- Environment Matrix and Feature Matrix ---
| Environment     | Target                        | Feature Bias                                      | Restrictions                     | Secrets Posture       |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| local           | Windows/macOS operator laptop | control plane, creator workflows, light inference | heavy render may degrade/offload | local secure store    |
| hybrid          | local + cloud                 | mainline full product                             | preferred posture                | mixed local/cloud     |
| cloud           | serverless/workers            | media spine, async providers, publish             | full heavy execution             | cloud secrets manager |
| untrusted_local | restricted local              | viewer-safe / limited ops                         | no premium or sensitive mutation | minimal secrets only  |

| Feature                  | Role Visibility         | Env Availability   | Dependency                   | Governance                            | Maturity                        |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| Creator Studio dashboard | creator/founder/builder | local,hybrid,cloud | none                         | founder mode adds privileged controls | mature                          |
| Premium voice route      | founder/creator         | hybrid,cloud       | ElevenLabs                   | Kubera + Yama + route legality        | mature                          |
| Avatar render            | creator/founder         | hybrid,cloud       | HeyGen or local fallback     | Vayu/quality gates                    | mature                          |
| Plugin host              | founder/builder         | local,hybrid,cloud | plugin registry              | sandbox + permission                  | population-pending in some rows |
| Micro-agent helper layer | builder/runtime         | all                | parent skill/director rights | TTL + audit                           | contract-defined                |

--- Hardware Class and OS Compatibility Matrix ---
This closes the gap between abstract hardware classes and the actual user agenda.

| Hardware Profile | Canonical Label | OS | Safe Modes | Offload Requirement | Directors Safe | Skills Safe | Heavy Modules | Builder Verdict |
| ---------------- | --------------- | -- | ---------- | ------------------- | -------------- | ----------- | ------------- | --------------- |
| Windows laptop / 8 GB RAM / standard CPU / 256 GB SSD | WIN_STD_8G | Windows 10/11 | local degraded, hybrid, cloud | avatar render, premium TTS, heavy visual generation, large vector jobs, batch analytics | yes (decision plane) | yes for text/intel/distribution/system skills; heavy media only via offload | AV render, Wav2Lip, large FFmpeg, image-heavy packages require offload | supported only with aggressive unload, cache discipline, and offload-first heavy media |
| Windows laptop / 16 GB RAM / stronger CPU / optional entry GPU | WIN_MID_16G | Windows 10/11 | local, hybrid, cloud | premium avatar or long renders may still offload | yes | most skills; medium media local, heavy media hybrid | final long-form render, premium avatar, multi-variant swarms | mainstream creator target |
| Windows workstation / 32 GB+ / stronger CPU/GPU / larger SSD | WIN_PRO_32G | Windows 10/11 | local, hybrid, cloud | offload optional for premium-quality deadlines | yes | yes across core + many expansion paths | enterprise-scale or high concurrency still hybrid/cloud | high-end creator/founder station |
| macOS Intel / 16 GB+ | MAC_INTEL_16G | macOS Intel | local, hybrid, cloud | GPU-heavy paths may prefer hybrid/cloud | yes | text, orchestration, moderate media | some local GPU paths vary by tooling | supported with adapter caveats |
| Apple Silicon / M4 class | MAC_AS_M4 | macOS Apple Silicon | local, hybrid, cloud | premium avatar / provider-dependent steps may still use cloud; local Metal-friendly acceleration preferred | yes | broad coverage, strong local reasoning/media support where libraries exist | provider-bound avatar and large render clusters still hybrid/cloud | fully supported target class |
| untrusted local browser / borrowed machine | UNTRUSTED_LOCAL | any | untrusted_local only | all premium, mutation, secret-bearing, and heavy write flows blocked | view/control only | read-only or constrained helper flows | nearly all heavy or secret-bearing modules blocked | safe degraded inspection mode |

OS adapter rules:
- Windows: Job Objects, priority classes, WMI / Performance Counters / DXGI where supported.
- macOS: IOKit / Metal-facing telemetry bindings or validated adapters.
- cross-platform law: routing, dossier, governance, thresholds, and Vayu semantics remain identical; only adapters differ.

--- Windows/macOS Customer Device Retune ---
Windows/macOS customer-device retune restored from DNA:
- The job is to make the runtime sovereign on the hardware customers actually own.
- Windows and macOS are the customer-device mainline.
- Linux-first or VDI-specific assumptions are preserved only as optional backend / acceleration-node doctrine unless explicitly mainline-supported elsewhere.
- Cross-platform viability must be judged against average creator hardware, not only server-grade infrastructure.

======================================================================
35. IMPLEMENTATION SEQUENCING AND BUILD ORDER IS LAW
======================================================================
--- Build Phase Plan with Exit Criteria ---
| Phase                   | Scope                                                           | Outputs                                        | Acceptance Criteria                      | No-Go Condition                                  |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| P1 Constitution         | registries, schema, dossier shards                              | all registries + schema validators + shard map | registry/schema validation pass          | No runtime without constitution                  |
| P2 Validators/Factories | skill factory, patch engine, route cache                        | working validators + contract generators       | factory smoke tests + patch engine tests | No kernel before validators                      |
| P3 Kernel               | Kubera, Yama, Ganesha, Aruna, Vayu, Krishna/Vishnu, Chitragupta | live legal/routing stack                       | budget/policy/route/Vayu tests pass      | No media runtime before kernel                   |
| P4 Neural/Knowledge     | Akasha, Neural index, worker router, Chronos                    | route-aware activation + telemetry             | lane routing + dispatch tests            | No creator modes before neural layer             |
| P5 Media Factory        | script, voice, avatar, QA, render, publish                      | topic-to-video spine                           | integration tests across media path      | No production release before media spine works   |
| P6 Control Plane        | dashboard, RCA, founder/creator modes                           | screen contracts + APIs + overlays             | UI/API contract tests                    | No founder/operator handoff before control plane |
| P7 Operations           | deployment blueprint, release channels, recovery                | environment matrices + release/rollback docs   | release gate + rollback drill            | No public release without ops pack               |

--- Source-backed Build Order Is Law ---
Source-backed Build Order Is Law restored:
1. System constitution and registries first:
   CONTENT_DOSSIER schema, director / skill / provider / policy / route / mode registries, registry instance.
2. Factories and validators second:
   skill factory, contract validator, dependency resolver, dossier patch engine, route cache, registry integrity validator.
3. Kernel third:
   Kubera, Yama, Ganesha, Aruna, Vayu, Krishna orchestration bus, Chitragupta state/memory.
4. Media runtime fourth:
   script engine, voice engine, emotion engine, avatar engine, asset hunter / Indra, render kernel, QA + tribunal.
5. Publish loop fifth:
   YouTube deployment engine, analytics learning engine, optimization feedback writers.
6. Control plane sixth:
   dashboard APIs, telemetry stream, MissionControl / dag_state exporter, deployment manifests for local / cloud / hybrid.
Hard rule preserved from source: do not start with UI, workers, or random skills before constitution, registries, validators, and kernel truth exist.

--- Build / Release Gate Matrix Patch ---
| Gap                                            | Blocks Build | Blocks Release                      |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| Missing Cost Tier Taxonomy                     | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Cost Optimisation Matrix               | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Provider Cost Registry                 | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Route Cost Matrix                      | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Skill Cost Profiles                    | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Director Execution Matrix              | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Skill Execution Matrix                 | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Route Worker Matrix                    | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Hybrid Switch Policies                 | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Dashboard Cost Screens                 | Yes          | Yes                                 |
| Missing Provider OAuth / callback final values | No           | Yes for unattended release          |
| Missing route owner / veto exact rows          | No           | Yes for governance-critical release |

======================================================================
36. DEPLOYMENT AND PRODUCTION POSTURE
======================================================================
--- Deployment and Production Blueprint ---
Topology:
Client / Creator Studio UI -> API Gateway / control services -> Kernel -> queues -> Step Functions / orchestrator -> provider APIs (voice/avatar) -> render workers / FFmpeg -> object storage + CDN -> publish APIs -> analytics -> optimizer

Production blueprint elements:
| Area                 | Decision / Posture                                                     | Notes                                       |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| desktop vs web shell | Decision packet unless product release scope fixes one                 | founder/builder decision affects packaging  |
| release channels     | internal builder -> founder beta -> creator beta -> production         | channel promotion checklist required        |
| rollback             | artifact manifest + registry version rollback + state migration policy | must test before public release             |
| observability        | route trace + worker status + RCA + cost ledger + queue wait           | Mission Control truth                       |
| incident ops         | severity matrix + ownership + recovery playbooks                       | builder/founder/operator split              |
| storage/CDN          | artifact storage with versioning; CDN delivery for publish/download    | exact vendor choice may vary by environment |

--- Packaging Decision Matrix ---
| Packaging Choice | Pros                                          | Cons                                           | Recommended Release Channel           | Architectural Fit                                 |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| desktop_shell    | best local-native control experience          | higher update/installer burden                 | founder/internal builder tiers first  | good for local/hybrid operator-first posture      |
| web_shell        | simpler deployment and cloud-control surfaces | weaker local-native integration unless bridged | creator/founder browser-first rollout | good for cloud-heavy posture                      |
| hybrid_shell     | desktop wrapper + web control panes           | highest implementation complexity              | later enterprise tier                 | best match for local/hybrid/cloud unified product |

--- Deployment Artifact Pack, Packaging, and CI/CD / Promotion Architecture ---
57.1 Required deployment artifacts
- environment manifests (`env/*.yaml`)
- shell packaging manifest (`packaging_decision_matrix.md`)
- registry snapshot (`registries/*.yaml|json`)
- dashboard route map (`dashboard_routes.yaml`)
- plugin manifests (`plugins/manifests/*.yaml`)
- route catalogs (`routes/*.yaml`)
- provider auth matrix (`oauth_matrix.yaml`)
- callback contracts (`heygen_callback_contract.md` and provider callback pack)
- build blocker matrix (`build_release_gates.yaml`)
- error glossary (`error_glossary.json`)
- audit schema pack (`schemas/audit/*.json`)

57.2 Packaging decision matrix
| Shell Choice | Best For | Builder Decision |
| ------------ | -------- | ---------------- |
| local desktop shell | creator/founder local operations on Windows/macOS | good for packaged command center, local vault, OS adapters |
| browser-first web shell | hosted control plane | best for team ops and cloud-heavy mode |
| hybrid shell | local command center + hosted APIs | best fit for Shadow Empire mainline |

57.3 CI/CD rules
- PR must pass schema validation, registry integrity, route cycle checks, skill contract validation, build blocker matrix.
- release channels: `dev`, `staging`, `production`.
- promotion requires zero P0 blockers and zero unresolved release-blocking decision packets.
- rollback requires manifest hash and registry snapshot restore.

--- Avatar Engine PRD v3.0.0 Conformance and Integration Matrix ---
This section ensures the Avatar Engine PRD is deployed within Shadow Empire context instead of being treated as an isolated project.

| Avatar PRD Capability | Shadow Empire Mainline Mapping | Deploy Decision |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------ | --------------- |
| 5 phases / 19 modules production spine | Media Factory law + Avatar/Voice/Emotion/Render/Publish layers | KEEP mainline |
| Step Functions .waitForTaskToken long render orchestration | non-blocking long-running media job rule | KEEP where cloud avatar path is used |
| ElevenLabs chunking and async generation | voice engine + premium provider path | KEEP mainline |
| HeyGen avatar submit / poll / TTL handling | avatar engine + provider callback decision packets | KEEP mainline |
| Wav2Lip / SadTalker / Wav2Vec realism stack | local/hybrid premium quality path | KEEP mainline as expansion-capable media layer |
| FFmpeg Fargate render chain | cloud render worker / hybrid render fallback | KEEP mainline |
| QA gate (lip sync >= 0.75, retries <= 3) | governance + realism gate | KEEP mainline |
| YouTube deployment and analytics learning | publish loop v1 | KEEP mainline |
| source-material requirements for avatar/voice | persona / creator asset onboarding | KEEP mainline |
| training-factory gaps (avatar factory / voice factory) | future defensibility / model ownership expansion | KEEP as post-mainline expansion, not removed |

58.1 Avatar/voice model-factory expansion kept in scope
The corpus explicitly flags that generation pipeline alone is insufficient for long-term moat. Therefore Shadow Empire keeps these post-mainline subsystems in the architecture:
- Avatar Training Factory
- Voice Training Factory
- Voice Model Registry
- Avatar Registry
- Prosody Analyzer
- Speaker Verification
These are expansion-path modules, not deleted scope.

======================================================================
37. VALIDATION, QA, RELIABILITY, AND RECOVERY EXPECTATIONS
======================================================================
--- Validation, QA, and Reliability Expectations ---
- quality gates by stage
- realism tribunal for avatar path
- lip sync, voice, render, publish, analytics validators
- no-open-packet release gate for release-blocking decision packets
- deterministic RCA for incidents
- builder acceptance tests by module family live in Builder Acceptance Pack

--- Error Glossary and Recovery Matrix ---
| Error Code         | Severity | Subsystem    | Condition                                          | Automatic Response            | Audit              | Recovery               |
| ------------------ | -------- | ------------ | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------- |
| ERR-BUDGET-TOKEN   | High     | Kubera       | premium route requested without valid budget token | route block + founder notice  | RCA + cost ledger  | issue token or reroute |
| ERR-POLICY-VETO    | High     | Yama         | policy/legal violation                             | hard reject / remodify        | governance audit   | fix content/route      |
| ERR-VAYU-GATE      | Medium   | Vayu         | unsafe local hardware posture                      | defer/offload/degrade         | resource audit     | change route or wait   |
| ERR-PLUGIN-SANDBOX | Medium   | Plugin Host  | plugin violates sandbox/permission                 | disable plugin + reroute      | plugin audit       | fix plugin config      |
| ERR-DLQ-MEDIA      | High     | Media Worker | media task exhausted retries                       | DLQ + manual/auto remediation | worker + route RCA | replay after fix       |

--- Observability, Mission Control, RCA, and Incident Navigation Model ---
56.1 Telemetry model
- logs: structured events by component
- metrics: queue depth, provider health, route latency, budget burn, render timings, callback failures
- traces: route_id / dossier_id / incident_id correlated path
- alerts: severity-classed alerts with deep links

56.2 Click-to-root law
Every major alert must deep-link to one root object:
- dossier diff
- route trace
- incident timeline
- provider health record
- budget token

56.3 Incident taxonomy
| Severity | Meaning | Example |
| -------- | ------- | ------- |
| info | non-blocking signal | low telemetry lag |
| warning | degraded but recoverable | quota nearing cap |
| high | production-impacting, recoverable | callback failure, queue saturation |
| critical | build/release or active production blocker | policy illegality, shard corruption, unsafe hardware interrupt |

56.4 STAR-style RCA packet
- situation
- task
- action
- result
- error_code
- owner
- impact_scope
- root_cause
- remediation
- prevention_rule

======================================================================
38. RISK, CONSTRAINT, AND FAILURE-SURFACE MAPPING
======================================================================
--- Risk, Constraint, and Failure-Surface Mapping ---
- packetized unresolved rows that still need founder/builder decisions
- provider auth/callback variability across environments
- performance and thermal constraints on local devices
- governance/legal drift if founder overrides are not bounded
- dashboard complexity without strict role guards
- route and worker misalignment without worker-router matrix enforcement

--- What Blocks Build vs What Blocks Release Matrix ---
| Gap                                        | Blocks Build | Blocks Release             | Notes                                   |
| ------------------------------------------ | ------------ | -------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| Missing shadow pair beyond Krishna/Vishnu  | No           | Yes for HA release tier    | Decision packet exists                  |
| Missing per-screen API signature           | Partial      | No                         | Stub contract allowed for build         |
| Missing provider callback URI exact value  | No           | Yes for unattended release | Decision packet exists                  |
| Missing per-state timeout numeric          | No           | No                         | Subsystem defaults allowed during build |
| Missing route owner/veto matrix exact rows | No           | Yes for governed release   | Decision packet exists                  |

--- Build Blockers vs Release Blockers Matrix and Final No-Re-Derivation Handoff ---
| Item | Blocks Build | Blocks Release | Notes |
| ---- | ------------ | -------------- | ----- |
| missing cost tier taxonomy | Yes | Yes | from v9 patch law |
| missing execution tier matrices | Yes | Yes | from v9 patch law |
| missing .md skill repo schema / loader contract | Yes | Yes | cannot generate or load Agentic Army safely |
| missing dashboard screen contract pack | Yes | Yes | control plane not builder-safe |
| missing provider callback / OAuth environment packet | No | Yes | unattended release blocker |
| missing exact enterprise billing bridge | No | Yes for enterprise | non-mainline consumer/founder builds may proceed |
| unresolved route owner / veto | Yes | Yes | governance ambiguity |
| unresolved M-090 collision | Yes | Yes | registry corruption risk |
| missing Windows/macOS adapter truth | Yes | Yes | violates cross-platform mainline constitution |
| missing Neural / Veins and Worker Router contracts | Yes | Yes | machine still requires re-derivation |

60.1 Final no-re-derivation handoff law
Claude or any builder should not have to rediscover:
- where state lives
- who owns a namespace
- which route is legal
- when premium is allowed
- how a skill file is structured
- how dashboard actions map to APIs
- how worker routing chooses candidates
- how founder and creator modes differ
- how Windows/macOS/local/hybrid/cloud modes differ
- what generic IDs are forbidden

60.2 Builder decision packets still unavoidable
Only exact environment-specific callback URIs, enterprise billing bridge details, and external domain names remain as decision packets. Everything else in v10 is carried as mainline machine truth.

60.3 Final builder position
v10 supersedes v9 as the production master PRD because it preserves the cost/execution patch while restoring the missing machine layers: repository runtime, Neural/Veins model, control plane, portability, hardware classes, provider/OAuth truth, plugin/micro-agent lifecycle, worker-router contract, avatar-v3 integration, and data hygiene.

| Screen_ID | Path         | Screen                     | Roles                    | Key_Table_Columns                                              | Filters                | API_Endpoints                                                  | Deep_Link_Contracts                  | DTO_Schema               | Role_Guards                    | State_Model                 | Mutation_Rules                              |

| SCR-002   | /observatory | Sarvantharyami Observatory | Founder|Builder          | actor,metric,value,window,status,drill_id                      | metric,window,severity | GET /skills/health; GET /directors/accuracy; GET /cost/heatmap | heatmap cell -> profile -> incident  | observability_matrix_dto | builder/founder only           | observability_state_machine | telemetry degraded -> partial state         |
| SCR-003   | /workspace   | Sovereign Workspace        | Founder|Builder          | crew_id,mode,route,scope,validator_status                      | mode,route             | GET /modes; GET /registry/routes; POST /workspace/compose      | save crew -> validation result       | workspace_compose_dto    | founder/builder write          | workspace_state_machine     | invalid compose -> blocked                  |
| SCR-004   | /diagnostics | Diagnostics                | Builder|Operator         | incident_id,severity,owner,worker_class,queue,age,route        | severity,queue,owner   | GET /incidents; GET /workers; GET /queues                      | alert -> root cause -> replay        | incident_detail_dto      | builder/operator action        | diagnostics_state_machine   | replay blocked if policy denies             |

48. PROVIDER / OAUTH / CALLBACK / SECRET LIFECYCLE CLOSURE
======================================================================
This section preserves the v11 provider matrix and closes the auth/callback/secret lifecycle gaps required for unattended release. Exact deployed secret values remain intentionally absent; only contract shapes are defined.

| Provider                          | Auth_Type                      | Secret_Env_Blueprint                      | Storage_Model                              | Rotation_Model                               | Scope_Model           | Auth_Failure_Handling                         | Callback_Contract                                                          | Open_Release_Blocker                             | Blocks_Build | Blocks_Release |
| --------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------ | -------------- |
| Google AI Studio / Gemini 1.5 Pro | API Key                        | GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_API_KEY                  | local secure store or cloud secret manager | rotate on provider schedule or policy breach | request scope only    | 429/backoff + budget-aware reroute            | N/A                                                                        | Quota/limits remain env-specific decision packet | No           | No             |
| ElevenLabs                        | API Key                        | ELEVEN_LABS_KEY                           | cloud secret manager / secure local vault  | rotate on cadence + revoke on compromise     | voice synthesis only  | chunk-aware retry with cost cap               | N/A                                                                        | No release blocker after chunk policy            | No           | No             |
| HeyGen                            | API Key + callback/poller      | HEYGEN_API_KEY                            | cloud secret manager                       | rotate on cadence + revoke on compromise     | avatar render only    | poller fallback if callback unavailable       | https://{env_host}/callbacks/heygen/render-status + signature verification | Exact verification payload remains packetized    | No           | Yes            |
| YouTube Data API                  | OAuth 2.0                      | YOUTUBE_CLIENT_ID / YOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRET | local secure store + cloud secret manager  | refresh-token renewal + revoke on auth reset | upload/metadata scope | oauth refresh retry + founder manual fallback | https://{env_host}/auth/youtube/callback                                   | Exact deployed URI remains env-bound             | No           | Yes            |
| YouTube Analytics API             | OAuth 2.0                      | YOUTUBE_ANALYTICS_CLIENT_ID / SECRET      | local secure store + cloud secret manager  | refresh-token renewal + revoke on auth reset | analytics scope       | quota-aware polling backoff                   | https://{env_host}/auth/youtube-analytics/callback                         | Exact deployed URI remains env-bound             | No           | Yes            |
| yt-dlp                            | No external auth by default    | N/A                                       | local                                      | N/A                                          | ingest only           | anti-ban backoff + manual fallback            | N/A                                                                        | Proxy/cookie policy depends on environment       | No           | No             |
| Selenium / Playwright             | Session / cookie / credentials | BROWSER_AUTOMATION_SECRET_SET             | local/cloud secure vault                   | cookie/session refresh per runbook           | browser automation    | permission/auth fail -> founder manual path   | env-specific browser callback if used                                      | Exact callback only if browser OAuth flow used   | No           | Partial        |
| XTTS-v2                           | Local model                    | N/A                                       | local                                      | N/A                                          | local tts             | Vayu-gated retry                              | N/A                                                                        | No release blocker                               | No           | No             |
| Wav2Lip                           | Local model/service            | N/A                                       | local/specialized                          | N/A                                          | lip sync/qa           | gpu-availability fallback                     | N/A                                                                        | No release blocker                               | No           | No             |
| FFmpeg                            | Local/service binary           | N/A                                       | local/cloud worker                         | N/A                                          | render assembly       | worker retry / reroute                        | N/A                                                                        | No release blocker                               | No           | No             |


Environment-specific callback URI contract shapes:
```md
YouTube callback URI pattern: https://{env_host}/auth/youtube/callback
YouTube Analytics callback URI pattern: https://{env_host}/auth/youtube-analytics/callback
HeyGen callback URI pattern: https://{env_host}/callbacks/heygen/render-status
Verification rule: callback signature / shared secret / request-id correlation must pass before Vein write or job-state mutation.
```

======================================================================
51. WORKFLOW STATE MACHINE DEEPENING — NUMERICS, RETRIES, ROLLBACK, AND REPLAY
======================================================================
Operational closure for workflow states, retries, rollback, replay, and UI visibility is appended here to strengthen the v11 lifecycle model.

| State             | Timeout | Retry_Ceiling | Dashboard_Visibility             | Rework_or_Retry_Path                   | Rollback_Path                    | Manual_Override_Rule                      |
| ----------------- | ------- | ------------- | -------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| creator_draft     | 10m     | 1             | /workspace,/pipelines            | save draft / validate draft            | cancel to workspace              | founder/builder override allowed          |
| route_selected    | 2m      | 1             | /command,/workspace              | reroute to standard or tribunal        | hold route                       | mode override owner may intervene         |
| provider_reserved | 5m      | 1             | /cost-estimator,/budget-tokens   | provider switch if legal               | refund token and reroute         | cost gate applies                         |
| voice_pending     | 15m     | 1 premium     | /pipelines,/library              | fallback to XTTS-v2                    | rework script/voice settings     | founder premium approval may be requested |
| avatar_pending    | 45m     | 1 premium     | /pipelines,/library,/diagnostics | poller fallback or reduced avatar path | rollback to still-image path     | callback contract required                |
| render_pending    | 60m     | 1 heavy       | /pipelines,/diagnostics,/library | reduced render path                    | rollback to pre-render artifacts | Vayu / worker telemetry may force degrade |
| publish_pending   | 30m     | 2 auth-safe   | /pipelines,/channels             | manual founder publish                 | rollback to scheduled draft      | oauth refresh path applies                |
| analytics_pending | 24h     | 2 low-cost    | /analytics,/observatory          | defer analytics batch                  | none; retain publish state       | quota-aware backoff                       |
| incident_replay   | 1h      | 1             | /diagnostics                     | replay from checkpoint                 | rollback to last clean state     | builder/operator only                     |

State laws added in v12:
- pause/resume allowed only for long-running provider or render states with checkpoint owner defined.
- publish rollback preserves artifact lineage and revokes only the publish envelope, not prior creative artifacts.
- incident replay always writes a new replay envelope and cannot mutate historical audit rows.

| Hardware_Profile                           | Safe_Modules                                                    | Offload_Required_Modules                                   | Degraded_Behavior                                                                             | Environment_Caveats                                      |
| ------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| Windows 8GB / CPU / 256GB SSD              | research, scripting, governance, local dashboard read paths     | avatar/render heavy routes, browser automation heavy flows | force ZERO_LOCAL/LOW_LOCAL, route premium work to hybrid/cloud, disable local heavy GPU tasks | web hosted control surfaces partial; plugin host limited |
| Windows 16GB / stronger CPU / optional GPU | most local text + some local media QA                           | full avatar/render still offload-preferred                 | allow hybrid standard and selective local GPU paths                                           | browser automation allowed with guardrails               |
| Windows 32GB+ / stronger CPU/GPU           | nearly all except premium provider dependencies                 | still offload cloud-only provider work                     | local render draft + hybrid/cloud premium paths                                               | good local + GitHub builder posture                      |
| macOS Intel                                | research, scripting, governance, dashboards                     | heavy local media slower / plugin variance                 | hybrid preferred for media runtime                                                            | browser/plugin compatibility needs per-plugin check      |
| Apple Silicon / M4                         | research, scripting, governance, strong local QA and some media | provider-only premium paths remain remote                  | local+hybrid strong; offload provider-bound workloads                                         | best local creator class in corpus                       |
| untrusted_local                            | view-only or bounded local generation                           | premium/authenticated mutation surfaces blocked            | read-only secrets, no premium provider writes, no registry mutation                           | layman-safe / creator-safe only                          |

======================================================================
55. OBSERVABILITY / RCA / RECOVERY HARDENING SUPPLEMENT
======================================================================
| Severity | Meaning                                         | Primary_Surfaces                    | Automatic_Remediation_Eligible | Telemetry_Contract              | Escalation                        |
| -------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| INFO     | non-blocking telemetry                          | /observatory,/diagnostics           | no                             | trace + dashboard event only    | none                              |
| WARN     | degraded but continuing                         | /command,/observatory,/diagnostics  | conditional                    | incident stub + route trace     | builder/operator                  |
| ERROR    | stage failed or reroute required                | /diagnostics,/pipelines,/command    | yes                            | incident row + replay envelope  | builder/operator/founder by scope |
| CRITICAL | policy, spend, release, or data-integrity break | /founder-mode,/diagnostics,/command | yes hard stop                  | full RCA + founder notification | founder/tribunal                  |

Trace/span model:
- trace_id ties route envelope, worker task envelope, vein handoff envelope, cost event envelope, and incident replay envelope.
- span classes: route_select, provider_call, worker_exec, vein_write, dashboard_mutation, approval_action.
- runbook linkage rule: every ERROR or CRITICAL glossary code must map to an RCA template and recovery action.
