59. V13 ADDITIVE GAP-CLOSURE PATCH LAW
======================================================================
This v13 pass is additive only.
- No valid source-backed text from v12 has been deleted.
- No prior matrix has been shortened or collapsed.
- Existing wording remains baseline truth.
- The purpose of v13 is to close the remaining agenda-critical gaps identified after v12: Self-Healing, Self-Evolving, Agent Estate closure, workflow runtime closure, dependency graph closure, provider/auth release closure, and remaining portability / parameter / entity gaps.
- Where literal values remain not source-final, they remain explicitly classified in blocker ledgers instead of being silently guessed.


======================================================================
60. SELF-HEALING LAYER — FIRST-CLASS OPERATIONAL SUBSYSTEM
======================================================================
v12 preserved recovery, RCA, diagnostics, and packet-ledger truth, but it did not elevate self-healing into its own governed subsystem. v13 closes that gap.

Self-Healing mission:
- detect runtime failure early
- apply bounded, auditable, safe auto-remediation
- preserve artifacts and state integrity
- escalate only when action crosses founder, legal, destructive, or premium boundaries

Bound runtime primitives sourced from project truth:
- Failure Detector
- Recovery Engine
- Adaptive Re-Router
- Task Queue Manager
- Resource Allocator
- Performance Monitor

SELF_HEALING_LAYER_REGISTER_v13

Self-Healing is now a first-class operational subsystem. It does not replace existing recovery, RCA, or diagnostics sections in v12; it binds them into one governed layer with explicit auto-actions, safe-mode limits, and founder/HITL boundaries.

| Healing_ID | Subsystem                | Detection_Trigger                                                                            | Eligible_Auto_Action                                                                   | Ineligible_Actions                                     | HITL_Boundary                                                    | Mode_Scope                         | Audit_Logging                              | Dashboard_Visibility             | Dependencies                                                | Acceptance_Criteria                                             |
| ---------- | ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| SH-001     | Failure Detector         | module failure, task crash, bad output signature, callback failure, queue dead-letter growth | raise incident class + isolate failing component                                       | no schema mutation; no premium escalation without gate | No for safe isolate, Yes for destructive replay or vendor switch | local,hybrid,cloud,untrusted_local | all healing events -> audit.db + RCA       | Mission Control -> RCA deep link | Failure Detector / Performance Monitor / Task Queue Manager | incident class assigned + route resumed or safely held          |
| SH-002     | Recovery Engine          | confirmed failure after detector threshold                                                   | checkpoint resume, state replay, safe rollback, restart bounded stage                  | no cross-domain data mutation                          | Yes if rollback crosses approval boundary                        | local,hybrid,cloud                 | recovery action + outcome + packet linkage | Diagnostics -> Recovery Timeline | state.db / Veins / Workflow Register                        | job either restored or held with explicit blocker               |
| SH-003     | Adaptive Re-Router       | provider outage, worker unhealthy, route veto, cost hard stop, Vayu thermal stop             | reroute to approved fallback chain                                                     | no forbidden route or premium overrun                  | Yes if route changes creator/founder approval class              | hybrid,cloud,local_degraded        | route mutation + budget diff               | Central Command / Routes         | Worker Router / Route Register / Kubera                     | fallback route accepted + SLA preserved or downgrade declared   |
| SH-004     | Queue Remediator         | queue saturation, stuck task age, repeated retry burst                                       | pause intake, reclassify priority, drain queue, move to DLQ if ceiling hit             | no silent drop of jobs                                 | No for bounded pause; Yes for bulk purge                         | local,hybrid,cloud                 | queue audit row + saturation metrics       | Queue Ops                        | Task Queue Manager / Resource Allocator                     | queue stabilized within threshold window                        |
| SH-005     | Worker Recycler          | worker memory leak, GPU reset need, plugin host corruption                                   | recycle worker, warm standby switch, cache purge where safe                            | no delete of user artifacts                            | Yes if active premium render would be interrupted                | local,hybrid,cloud                 | worker lifecycle event                     | Worker Health                    | Worker Topology / Vayu / Provider policy                    | worker replaced and active jobs reattached                      |
| SH-006     | Provider Failover Healer | provider healthcheck fail, callback timeout, auth refresh failure                            | switch to approved provider fallback, enter poller mode, degrade quality tier if legal | no unapproved provider or callback spoofing            | Yes if fallback crosses release/payment boundary                 | hybrid,cloud                       | provider failover event                    | Provider Usage / Cost Ledger     | Provider policy / Route matrix / Release blockers           | service continues on approved fallback or explicit block raised |
| SH-007     | Safe-Mode Degrader       | low-resource stress, Vayu hard stop, untrusted_local restriction, budget hard stop           | enter safe mode, disable premium/media-heavy lanes, keep governance + audit alive      | no hidden disablement of critical alerts               | No                                                               | local,untrusted_local,hybrid       | safe-mode entry/exit events                | Central Command + Banner         | Hardware matrix / Cost thresholds                           | system remains operable in degraded mode                        |
| SH-008     | Founder Escalation Gate  | healing action crosses approval, policy, legal, enterprise spend, or destructive boundary    | freeze job and request human decision packet                                           | no autonomous crossing of founder-only boundary        | Always                                                           | all                                | escalation + response SLA                  | Founder Queue / Alerts           | Yama / Kubera / Krishna                                     | human decision captured with closure code                       |


======================================================================
61. SELF-EVOLVING LAYER — GOVERNED MUTATION AND LEARNING SUBSYSTEM
======================================================================
v12 preserved optimization loops, analytics feedback, and route/threshold evolution hints, but not a first-class self-evolving layer. v13 closes that gap.

Self-Evolving mission:
- transform analytics and pattern signals into governed mutation proposals
- gate all self-modification through evaluation, rollback, freeze, and budget law
- prevent drift, corruption, silent regressions, and unbounded spend

SELF_EVOLVING_LAYER_REGISTER_v13

Self-Evolving is now a governed runtime layer rather than a promise-only optimization loop. It binds mutation proposals, evaluation gates, rollback, budget law, and anti-drift governance to the existing analytics/evolution logic in v12.

| Evolution_ID | Subsystem                          | Trigger                                                              | Eligible_Action                                          | Ineligible_Action                                    | Approval_Boundary                                                      | Mode_Scope         | Audit_Output               | Dashboard_Surface             | Dependencies                                  | Acceptance_Criteria                                 |
| ------------ | ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| SE-001       | Mutation Proposal Gate             | analytics drift, fatigue score, threshold breach, route inefficiency | propose skill/route/prompt/threshold change              | no silent mainline mutation                          | No automatic mainline merge without evaluation gate                    | all                | mutation proposal envelope | Optimization / Evolution      | Analytics loop / Pattern Recognition / Narada | proposal fully scoped with rollback and owner       |
| SE-002       | Evaluation Gate                    | new proposal created                                                 | run champion-challenger test or offline validator suite  | no production cutover on untested mutation           | Founder/tribunal for high-impact policy/cost changes                   | local,hybrid,cloud | evaluation scorecard       | Experiment panel              | Mutation Engine / QA                          | evidence produced for accept/reject                 |
| SE-003       | Skill Evolution Engine             | validated mutation in skill behavior                                 | rev new .md skill or modifier pack                       | no overwrite of canonical skill without version bump | Human if policy/cost/hardware class changes                            | local,hybrid,cloud | skill evolution log        | Skill Registry / GitHub       | Skill repo architecture                       | versioned artifact emitted                          |
| SE-004       | Route Evolution Engine             | route quality-cost mismatch or provider drift                        | adjust route preference, fallback chain, or exec bias    | no unapproved provider class introduction            | Founder/Kubera/Yama for payment/policy shifts                          | hybrid,cloud       | route evolution ledger     | Routes / Cost screens         | Route matrices / Worker router                | route diff + rollback plan recorded                 |
| SE-005       | Threshold Evolution Engine         | stable evidence that thresholds are too lax/strict                   | propose new timeout, retry, alert, or quality thresholds | no threshold mutation without bounded range          | Human for safety or legal thresholds                                   | all                | parameter change audit     | Diagnostics / Parameter panel | Parameter register                            | new threshold validated and revertable              |
| SE-006       | Model/Provider Selection Evolution | provider health, cost, quality, or hardware drift                    | re-rank provider selection or model family               | no secret/provider scope breach                      | Human for enterprise/payment/provider onboarding                       | hybrid,cloud       | selection change audit     | Provider Usage                | Provider registry                             | selection change preserves policy and budget law    |
| SE-007       | Rollback / Freeze Controller       | bad mutation, regression, cost spike, legal or quality breach        | rollback to prior version, freeze mutations in scope     | no continued auto-evolution in failing scope         | No for automatic rollback within tested boundary; Yes for broad freeze | all                | rollback/freeze record     | Founder / Diagnostics         | release/blocker ledger                        | regression contained and canonical version restored |

DIRECTOR_RUNTIME_FINALIZATION_NOTES_v13

The v12 supplement already carries row-level reads, writes, lockable namespaces, dossier lock authority, delegation policy, shadow pair, override scope, allowed modes, HITL triggers, degradation rules, degradation mode, secrets scope, dependency scope, worker interaction, route ownership, dashboard visibility, cost authority, execution tier, acceptance notes, failure surfaces, recovery expectations, and audit visibility. v13 preserves that table as the authoritative row register and binds unresolved items to the release/blocker ledger rather than leaving them implicit.

64. SKILL ESTATE FINALIZATION — AUTHORITATIVE IN-MAINLINE RUNTIME REGISTER
======================================================================

66. WHOLE-MACHINE WORKFLOW RUNTIME REGISTER
======================================================================
v12 had strong workflow language but still lacked one unified runtime workflow register. v13 closes that gap with a whole-machine register that binds owner, skill chain, route, timeout, retry, rollback, replay, audit, and dashboard reflection.

WHOLE_MACHINE_WORKFLOW_RUNTIME_REGISTER_v13

This register closes the missing whole-machine runtime workflow layer. It binds stage, owner, route, timeout, retry, rollback, replay, audit, and dashboard reflection in one machine-safe table.

======================================================================
P1-2 — INSERT DIRECTLY AFTER: Whole-Machine Workflow Runtime Register
======================================================================

SECTION: ARUNA_ATOMIC_WORK_DECOMPOSITION_LAW

Define indivisible work-unit law:
- Every major workflow stage may decompose into atomic work units.
- Each work unit inherits route, policy, audit, retry, and reassembly rules.
- Reassembly must preserve dossier lineage and artifact manifest continuity.

SECTION: WHOLE_MACHINE_WORK_UNIT_REGISTER

Columns:
Work_Unit_ID
Parent_Workflow_ID
Parent_Route_ID
Task_Node_Type
Subtask_Node_Type
Owning_Director
Owning_Skill
Queue_Class
Worker_Class
Split_Rule
Reassembly_Rule
Dependency_Depth
Retry_Inheritance
Audit_Write
Failure_Surface
Escalation_Rule

Representative rows:
WU-RES-01 topic research batch
WU-SCR-01 hook generation
WU-VOI-CHUNK-01 TTS chunk render
WU-AVA-POLL-01 avatar polling interval
WU-REN-SEG-01 render segment
WU-PUB-01 upload transaction
WU-ANA-01 analytics fetch

SECTION: WORK_UNIT_REASSEMBLY_AND_CHAIN_INHERITANCE_RULES

- No child unit may bypass parent policy gates.
- All child failures must map to parent route RCA.
- Chunkable render and TTS paths must emit durable checkpoint manifests.


======================================================================
67. WHOLE-MACHINE DEPENDENCY GRAPH
======================================================================
v12 had many strong partial registers but no full cross-layer dependency graph. v13 closes that gap by binding directors, skills, workers, providers, plugins, UI/control plane, hardware, self-healing, self-evolving, and release dependencies into one matrix.

WHOLE_MACHINE_DEPENDENCY_GRAPH_v13

This appendix closes the missing whole-machine dependency graph. It supplements the existing route, provider, worker, and hardware matrices with one cross-layer dependency register.

| Source_Component      | Dependency_Type | Target_Component                     | Mandatory_or_Optional          | Runtime_Impact                     | Failure_Impact              | Fallback_Available             | Mode_Specificity        | Hardware_Specificity       | Observability_Hook               | Recovery_Path                     |
| --------------------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------- | -------------------------- | -------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| Creator Command       | runtime         | intent_router                        | Mandatory                      | no command intake                  | system idle                 | No                             | all                     | none                       | central command logs             | clarification or reject           |
| intent_router         | governance      | Yama policy gate                     | Mandatory                      | illegal intent may pass            | policy breach               | No                             | all                     | none                       | policy verdict audit             | block or founder review           |
| neural_router         | runtime         | Director estate                      | Mandatory                      | no route/owner selection           | orchestration gap           | No                             | all                     | none                       | route decision envelope          | fallback route or hold            |
| Director estate       | execution       | Skill estate                         | Mandatory                      | no task execution                  | pipeline stall              | Partial via alternative family | all                     | none                       | director->skill invocation trace | reroute or manual review          |
| Skill estate          | execution       | Worker Router                        | Mandatory                      | no worker/provider selection       | task unexecuted             | Partial                        | all                     | worker class specific      | worker assignment logs           | reroute or hold                   |
| Worker Router         | provider        | Provider registry                    | Mandatory                      | no premium/hosted execution        | feature degradation         | Yes if local fallback exists   | hybrid,cloud            | provider/hardware specific | provider usage logs              | failover or downgrade             |
| Worker Router         | worker          | Worker topology map                  | Mandatory                      | no execution node selection        | job queue stall             | Partial                        | all                     | hardware class dependent   | worker lifecycle metrics         | requeue or recycle                |
| Skill estate          | artifact        | Veins / dossier shards               | Mandatory                      | state not persisted                | audit/state loss            | No                             | all                     | none                       | vein mutation logs               | replay or recover from checkpoint |
| Dashboard screen pack | data            | DTO schemas / API contracts          | Mandatory                      | UI ambiguity                       | operator confusion          | No                             | all                     | none                       | screen/api audit                 | supplemented contracts            |
| Dashboard mutations   | audit           | audit.db / change ledger             | Mandatory                      | untraceable operator action        | governance risk             | No                             | all                     | none                       | audit writes                     | block mutation if unavailable     |
| Provider callbacks    | auth            | secret / auth lifecycle              | Mandatory for unattended flows | publish/render may hang            | release blocker             | Partial via poller             | hybrid,cloud            | provider env specific      | callback/auth logs               | poller fallback or block release  |
| Self-Healing Layer    | signal          | Failure Detector                     | Mandatory                      | no automatic incident detection    | slow/manual recovery        | No                             | all                     | resource dependent         | incident metrics                 | manual escalation                 |
| Self-Healing Layer    | action          | Recovery Engine                      | Mandatory                      | no replay/recovery                 | job loss                    | Partial                        | all                     | state/checkpoint dependent | recovery audit                   | manual replay                     |
| Self-Healing Layer    | reroute         | Adaptive Re-Router                   | Mandatory                      | stuck on bad route/provider        | extended outage             | Yes if human reroute           | all                     | route/hardware specific    | route failover logs              | reroute or degrade                |
| Self-Healing Layer    | resource        | Performance Monitor                  | Mandatory                      | no saturation/thermal detection    | hardware damage or SLA miss | No                             | local,hybrid            | hardware specific          | telemetry/RCA                    | safe mode                         |
| Task Queue Manager    | resource        | Resource Allocator                   | Mandatory                      | queue starvation                   | throughput collapse         | No                             | all                     | worker/hardware specific   | queue metrics                    | throttle or reclassify            |
| Resource Allocator    | monitoring      | Performance Monitor                  | Mandatory                      | bad capacity assignment            | resource burn               | No                             | all                     | hardware specific          | allocator metrics                | rebalance                         |
| Self-Evolving Layer   | input           | analytics feedback loop              | Mandatory                      | no learning proposals              | static system               | No                             | all                     | none                       | mutation proposal log            | manual optimization only          |
| Self-Evolving Layer   | policy          | evaluation + rollback gates          | Mandatory                      | unsafe mutation                    | regression risk             | No                             | all                     | none                       | evaluation audit                 | freeze or rollback                |
| Agent estate          | linkage         | directors + skills                   | Mandatory                      | ecosystem posture unbound          | handoff ambiguity           | Partial                        | local,web,github        | runtime surface dependent  | agent invocation logs            | manual remap                      |
| Plugin estate         | compatibility   | OS/runtime portability matrices      | Mandatory                      | plugin misfire on unsupported env  | runtime instability         | Partial                        | local,hybrid,web-hosted | OS/runtime specific        | plugin health hooks              | disable plugin                    |
| Media runtime         | hardware        | Windows/macOS/Apple Silicon profiles | Mandatory                      | wrong local execution expectations | user-facing failure         | Partial                        | local,hybrid            | hardware specific          | hardware banner + telemetry      | offload or degrade                |
| Build Order Is Law    | deployment      | release blocker ledger               | Mandatory                      | promote with open blockers         | unsafe release              | No                             | build,release           | none                       | release manifest                 | block promotion                   |

71. PORTABILITY CLOSURE SUPPLEMENT
======================================================================
v12 improved portability, but some surfaces remained Partial. v13 states the exact hosted/local/web/plugin/media caveats, degrade rules, and dashboard reflections.

PORTABILITY_CLOSURE_SUPPLEMENT_v13

This appendix closes the remaining partial portability surfaces by stating exact caveats, adaptation boundaries, and required degradation behavior.

| Surface                         | Current_State        | Exact_Caveat                                                                | Closure_or_Degrade_Rule                                                       | Dashboard_Reflection      |
| ------------------------------- | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| Kernel runtime on Web/Hosted AI | PARTIAL              | no unrestricted shell, limited local filesystem, hosted session constraints | web-safe routing, hosted-safe registry reads, no privileged local plugin host | screen + route banner     |
| Media runtime on Local AI       | PARTIAL              | avatar/render heavy paths may require offload or reduced fidelity           | voice/avatar/render fallback chains + Vayu safe mode                          | hardware/profile banner   |
| Plugin host on Web/Hosted AI    | PARTIAL              | browser/sandbox and secret scope limits                                     | disable unsupported plugin classes, use hosted-safe adapters only             | plugin compatibility view |
| GitHub runtime                  | COVERED_WITH_CAVEATS | repo automation depends on auth, branch policy, hosted runner availability  | manual founder approval for destructive repo actions                          | repo dashboard + CI       |
| Untrusted local                 | COVERED_WITH_LIMITS  | premium, secret, and mutation rights constrained                            | read-only and safe-mode paths only                                            | safety banner             |

======================================================================

SECTION: COMPONENT_LEVEL_HARDWARE_AND_PORTABILITY_BINDING_MATRIX

Columns:
Subsystem
Supported_Hardware_Class
Blocked_Hardware_Class
Offload_Required_YN
Local_Degraded_Behavior
Hybrid_Behavior
Cloud_Assisted_Behavior
Apple_Silicon_Notes
Windows_8GB_Notes
Windows_16GB_Notes
Windows_32GB_Notes
Local_AI_Notes
Web_AI_Notes
GitHub_Runtime_Notes

Representative bindings:
Kernel core / dossier / governance: supported on Windows 8GB, 16GB, 32GB, macOS Intel, Apple Silicon
Heavy avatar/render paths: offload required on 8GB standard CPU class; partial local on 16GB + small GPU; fuller local/hybrid on 32GB+ or Apple Silicon with capability match
Plugin host on hosted/web remains PARTIAL where local process control or GPU coupling is required
Media runtime on Local AI remains PARTIAL for strongest realism stack without GPU resources
Skill loader/vector loading optimized for 8-16GB machines via ~7% active load target

SECTION: HEAVY_MODULE_OFFLOAD_AND_DEGRADED_MODE_MAP
SECTION: LOCAL_WEB_HYBRID_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_TABLE

73. MODEL-FACTORY EXPANSION MODULE OPERATIONALIZATION
======================================================================
v12 preserved Avatar Training Factory, Voice Training Factory, Voice Model Registry, Avatar Registry, Prosody Analyzer, and Speaker Verification as expansion-path modules. v13 operationalizes them with inputs, outputs, dependencies, governance, tests, and recovery.

MODEL_FACTORY_OPERATIONALIZATION_SUPPLEMENT_v13

This appendix turns the preserved expansion-path factories and registries into operational modules with dependencies, gates, acceptance, and recovery.

| Module                  | Purpose                             | Inputs                                        | Outputs                                  | Dependencies                  | Allowed_Modes      | Training_or_Inference           | Governance_Gates                     | Acceptance_Tests                      | Observability_Hooks           | Failure_Surfaces             | Recovery_Path                         |
| ----------------------- | ----------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| Avatar Training Factory | training pipeline for avatar models | consented image/video dataset, profile config | trained avatar artifact + registry entry | storage, compute, consent, QA | cloud/hybrid       | training only, not inference    | founder approval + policy/legal gate | factory acceptance suite + realism QA | Factory dashboard + telemetry | data policy breach / drift   | abort + rollback to prior avatar      |
| Voice Training Factory  | training pipeline for voice models  | consented voice dataset, persona config       | trained voice artifact + registry entry  | storage, compute, consent, QA | cloud/hybrid       | training only, not inference    | founder approval + rights check      | speaker/prosody acceptance            | Factory dashboard + telemetry | consent or drift fail        | rollback to prior voice model         |
| Voice Model Registry    | catalog of approved voice models    | factory outputs + metadata                    | versioned registry row                   | factory, runtime, publish     | local,hybrid,cloud | inference-ready selection layer | builder/founder guarded              | registry validator                    | Registry views                | bad metadata / stale version | freeze selection until fixed          |
| Avatar Registry         | catalog of approved avatar models   | factory outputs + metadata                    | versioned registry row                   | factory, runtime, render      | local,hybrid,cloud | render-ready selection layer    | builder/founder guarded              | registry validator                    | Registry views                | stale or broken avatar       | fallback to previous avatar           |
| Prosody Analyzer        | voice rhythm/prosody scoring        | audio asset + target persona                  | prosody score + defect report            | voice runtime, QA             | local,hybrid,cloud | inference/QA                    | no policy gate beyond safe use       | QA acceptance threshold               | QA + diagnostics              | false score / model mismatch | manual review                         |
| Speaker Verification    | speaker identity consistency check  | voice sample + reference profile              | verification score + pass/fail           | voice runtime, QA             | local,hybrid,cloud | inference/QA                    | policy/consent aware                 | identity threshold validator          | QA + diagnostics              | false reject/accept          | manual review or prior model fallback |

Promotion law for v13:
- no unattended release when callback/auth packets remain OPEN
- no enterprise promotion when spend-threshold packet remains OPEN
- no critical-path route release when skill/director ambiguity remains execution-critical
- all unresolved items must remain explicit, auditable, and linked to a required artifact or owner

VERSION_RECONCILIATION_AND_CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_NOTE_v14

Purpose:
Preserve all inherited wording from earlier additive passes without letting builders misread historical labels as current artifact identity.

Canonical authority rules:
- The canonical master artifact after this pass is Detailed_PRD_MASTERPIECE_v14.txt / .html.
- All retained v10/v11/v12 wording inside the preserved baseline remains historical carry-forward context unless a v14 reconciliation section explicitly declares it authoritative.
- Current authoritative release gating lives in FINAL_RELEASE_GATING_MATRIX_v14 and RELEASE_BLOCKER_LEDGER_v14.
- Current authoritative runtime closure layers for newly patched defects live in the v14 supplements appended in this pass.
- Historical labels remain visible by design to satisfy the zero-deletion law; they are not to be treated as current artifact identity when they conflict with v14 authority tables.

| Artifact_Name                    | Visible_Label                                     | Actual_Version_Context                 | Reason_Present                               | Canonical_or_Historical   | Builder_Instruction                                                                                                                                      |

92. SKILL EXECUTION CRITICAL CLOSURE SUPPLEMENT
======================================================================
SKILL_EXECUTION_CRITICAL_CLOSURE_SUPPLEMENT_v14

This supplement appends release-focused closure criteria for execution-critical skills. It preserves the estate-level v13 runtime truth and isolates the remaining contract ambiguity that can still block critical-path release.

Critical-path closure rules:
- Any skill on a critical publish/render/healing route must have final input/output/retry/recovery/test truth before that route is fully releasable.
- Packet linkage is acceptable for auditability, but not sufficient for unattended or critical-path release if the linked field is execution-critical.
- Release blocking is route-specific, not estate-wide by default.

93. PROVIDER AUTH RELEASE CLOSURE SUPPLEMENT
======================================================================
PROVIDER_AUTH_RELEASE_CLOSURE_SUPPLEMENT_v14

This supplement preserves all open provider/auth packets and makes their release inheritance explicit.

Auth closure rules:
- No unattended release for any provider flow whose callback/auth lifecycle is OPEN and required by that route.
- Poller fallback is a valid temporary workaround only if it is explicitly approved for the affected provider flow.
- Secret values remain intentionally absent; only contract shapes, storage models, scope models, and expiry/rotation handling are defined.

| Provider_or_Area         | Open_Contract                                                    | Current_Status | Blocks_Unattended_Release | Blocks_Enterprise_Promotion             | Temporary_Workaround                                  | Required_Artifact                          | Required_Owner  |
| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | --------------- |
| YouTube Data API / OAuth | callback URI mapping by environment                              | OPEN           | Yes                       | Yes                                     | manual founder upload / attended publish              | oauth_matrix.yaml + callback contract      | Founder+Builder |
| YouTube Analytics OAuth  | refresh lifecycle and callback mapping                           | OPEN           | No                        | Yes                                     | manual analytics pull / founder-authenticated session | oauth_matrix.yaml + refresh lifecycle spec | Founder+Builder |
| HeyGen callback          | callback verification / signature or approved poller equivalence | OPEN           | Yes                       | Yes                                     | poller-only mode                                      | heygen_callback_contract.md                | Builder         |
| HeyGen auth              | token scope / expiry / retry-backoff                             | PARTIAL        | Conditional               | Yes on unattended render                | guarded render with manual supervision                | provider auth lifecycle supplement         | Builder         |
| ElevenLabs auth          | rotation/expiry model                                            | PARTIAL        | No                        | No if attended or local fallback exists | fallback to XTTS/local voice                          | provider auth lifecycle supplement         | Builder         |
| Enterprise spend bridge  | threshold approval + billing posture                             | OPEN           | No                        | Yes for enterprise                      | restrict to non-enterprise                            | enterprise_budget_policy.yaml              | Founder+Kubera  |


UNATTENDED_RELEASE_ELIGIBILITY_TABLE_v14

| Area                              | Eligible_YN      | Reason                                            | Required_Closure_First                                     |
| --------------------------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| Publish via YouTube callback path | No               | callback mapping and refresh lifecycle still open | close YouTube callback/auth contract                       |
| Avatar render callback path       | No               | HeyGen callback verification still open           | close HeyGen callback contract or approve poller-only mode |
| Analytics unattended refresh      | No               | analytics token refresh lifecycle not fully final | close analytics OAuth refresh contract                     |
| Voice generation                  | Conditional      | depends on provider/auth + route fallback         | close provider auth if no local fallback                   |
| Local-only degraded runtime       | Yes with caveats | can operate without hosted callbacks              | respect degraded-mode and manual upload constraints        |


ENTERPRISE_PROMOTION_ELIGIBILITY_TABLE_v14

| Promotion_Area                   | Eligible_YN | Reason                                          | Required_Closure_First                           |
| -------------------------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| Enterprise premium routes        | No          | enterprise spend thresholds still open          | approve enterprise budget policy                 |
| Enterprise unattended publishing | No          | callback/auth + spend thresholds still open     | close provider/auth and enterprise spend packets |
| Non-enterprise hybrid operation  | Conditional | depends on route/provider-specific open packets | close only affected route/provider blockers      |

98. SELF-TROUBLESHOOTING ENGINE
======================================================================
SELF_TROUBLESHOOTING_ENGINE_v14

Mission:
Turn detected failures into exact, navigable, operator-safe troubleshooting flows with direct links from alert to root cause, related runbooks, and allowed recovery actions.

Engine rules:
- Every failure class must map to a primary target area and a deep-link destination.
- The engine distinguishes what can be auto-fixed, what requires operator action, and what requires founder approval.
- Troubleshooting output must link to Self-Healing actions, release blockers, and User Manual sections.

| Failure_Class                       | Detection_Source                                       | Primary_Target_Area                    | Deep_Link_Target                            | Diagnostic_Panel                | Suggested_Actions                                        | Automated_First_Response_or_Manual_Steps              | Escalation_Path                                    | Related_Runbook   | Related_Manual_Section |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| Callback/Auth Failure               | provider health / callback timeout / auth refresh fail | Provider Usage + Publish/Avatar panels | Diagnostics > Provider RCA > callback trace | callback verifier / auth panel  | check callback route, refresh lifecycle, fallback poller | poller switch, token refresh, hold affected route     | Founder if release-blocking or premium impact      | provider runbook  | HELP-PROVIDERS         |
| Queue Saturation                    | queue depth + pressure threshold                       | Central Command + Queue Ops            | Diagnostics > Queue > worker lane           | queue metrics / worker topology | inspect queue class, stuck jobs, saturation source       | pause intake, reprioritize, DLQ spill, worker recycle | Builder/Founder on persistent saturation           | queue runbook     | HELP-CENTRAL-COMMAND   |
| Render Failure                      | render exit / GPU/Vayu stop / provider fail            | Render panel + Diagnostics             | Diagnostics > Render RCA                    | render timeline / worker logs   | inspect Vayu stop, asset validity, provider health       | checkpoint resume, reroute, safe-mode degrade         | Founder if premium reroute or destructive rollback | render runbook    | HELP-DIAGNOSTICS       |
| Policy/Governance Rejection         | Yama / tribunal fail                                   | Founder Queue + QA                     | Diagnostics > Verdict Trace                 | policy/verdict panel            | review failing rule and artifact                         | remodify, reject, or founder override where legal     | Founder only for override                          | policy runbook    | HELP-HOME              |
| UI Mutation Error                   | API schema fail / permission denied / stale data       | screen-local banner + Diagnostics      | Diagnostics > UI/API trace                  | screen dto/payload panel        | inspect payload, role guard, stale data version          | retry, refresh, downgrade to read-only                | Builder if schema drift                            | ui runbook        | HELP-CENTRAL-COMMAND   |
| Analytics Drift / Evolution Regress | analytics anomaly / quality regression                 | Optimization + Diagnostics             | Diagnostics > Evolution RCA                 | mutation trace / KPI diff       | review proposal, compare champion-challenger             | rollback, freeze mutations, revert parameter          | Founder/Builder when learning budget or policy hit | evolution runbook | HELP-CREATOR           |

100. DNA BEST PRACTICES AND IMPLEMENTATION DISCIPLINE
======================================================================
DNA_BEST_PRACTICES_AND_IMPLEMENTATION_DISCIPLINE_v14

These are operational best-practice notes shaped by the strongest finalized DNA materials in the project, especially the universal Director DNA and universal Skill DNA patterns.

| DNA_Family                | Purpose                              | Implementation_Best_Practices                                                   | Failure_Avoidance_Practices                              | Observability_Practices                                 | Dashboard_Control_Implications                               | Security_Practices                              | Portability_Practices                           | Anti_Drift_Notes                                              |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Director DNA              | director decisions and arbitration   | keep reads/writes/lock authority explicit per row                               | never allow hidden cross-domain mutation                 | log every override, veto, reroute, and approval         | surface decision, reason, and blocker state in dashboard     | least-privilege namespace writes                | bind modes/hardware/cost to row truth           | do not let historical aliases replace canonical director row  |
| Skill DNA                 | atomic execution units               | single-responsibility skill contract with deterministic input/output boundaries | no skill without retry/recovery/failure notes            | trace skill invocation, fallback, and validator results | show route + worker + acceptance linkage                     | tool policy + patch policy must be bounded      | respect local/web/GitHub portability notes      | do not let examples stand in for estate-wide closure          |
| Agent DNA                 | ecosystem/agentic runtime            | explicit parent system, permissions, tool rights, and review triggers           | no hidden tool escalation                                | audit every agent action that mutates state or code     | show linked directors/skills and runtime boundary            | web/local/GitHub boundaries explicit            | keep counts and estate posture source-faithful  | do not fake a row roster the source does not provide          |
| Worker DNA                | execution workers and queues         | deterministic queue class + worker class + topology mapping                     | no silent worker recycle or DLQ purge                    | worker lifecycle and saturation visible in diagnostics  | surface queue pressure and recycle events                    | least-privilege secret scope per worker         | hardware-aware Vayu rules mandatory             | avoid mixing local/cloud semantics without explicit route law |
| Route Controller DNA      | route ownership and fallback         | owner/veto/fallback/quality/deadline all explicit                               | no route without legal downgrade path                    | route decision envelopes auditable                      | dashboard must show chosen route and fallback chain          | Kubera/Yama/Founder gate respected              | mode and hardware specificity explicit          | do not call route covered if only named once                  |
| Self-Healing Crew DNA     | auto-recovery subsystem              | bounded auto-actions only                                                       | never cross founder-only destructive boundaries silently | every healing action writes RCA-linked audit row        | mission control must show failing node + next safe step      | safe-mode and hardware-aware behavior mandatory | local/hybrid/cloud healing differences explicit | do not normalize away incident-class differences              |
| Self-Evolving Crew DNA    | governed mutation subsystem          | proposal->evaluation->rollback/freeze always explicit                           | no auto-mainline mutation without gate                   | mutation audit and KPI diff mandatory                   | evolution surfaces visible in optimization screens           | budget and policy limits explicit               | hardware-aware learning posture explicit        | anti-drift and anti-corruption rules mandatory                |
| Troubleshooting Crew DNA  | diagnostics and operator guidance    | map alert to exact failing area and runbook                                     | do not hide failure class or ambiguity                   | every suggested action should be auditable              | dashboard deep-link must point to root and supporting panels | permission-aware troubleshooting                | support novice and expert guidance              | never leave operator with prose-only advice                   |
| Dashboard/Manual Crew DNA | help and control plane               | contextual help at screen/widget/form level                                     | do not bury release blockers or warnings                 | audit UI mutations and reversals                        | manual/help linked to screens and troubleshooting            | role-aware help visibility                      | offline/help-cache posture explicit             | avoid generic docs divorced from control plane                |
| Factory/Training Crew DNA | voice/avatar training and registries | training/inference split explicit                                               | no training without policy/consent gate                  | registry writes and model admissions auditable          | factory screens show acceptance and failure surfaces         | resource profile + deployment path explicit     | portable only where allowed                     | do not treat factory modules as generic future ideas          |

101. MODULE AND CREW DNA EXAMPLE LIBRARY
======================================================================
MODULE_AND_CREW_DNA_EXAMPLE_LIBRARY_v14

Example DNA templates to guide future module and crew creation without inventing unrelated architecture.

| Example_Name                      | Module_or_Crew_Class  | Constitutional_Role                              | Inputs                                                  | Outputs                                  | Allowed_Actions                              | Forbidden_Actions                                   | Dependencies                             | Governance_Rules                                    | Failure_Surfaces                          | Observability_Hooks                  | Acceptance_Standard                            |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- |
| Director DNA Example              | Director              | constitutional arbiter + owner of bounded domain | creator/founder intent, dossier fields, route envelopes | decision, approval, veto, reroute, notes | domain-bounded read/write/lock only          | cross-domain mutation without law, secret overreach | owning rows, routes, modes, thresholds   | founder/policy/cost gates                           | ambiguous shadow/domain, illegal override | decision trace + blocker link        | must be row-bound and audit-visible            |
| Skill DNA Example                 | Skill                 | atomic execution brain                           | input contract + upstream artifacts                     | output contract + validator/test links   | allowed tools/patch rules only               | unbounded mutation, hidden retries                  | route/worker/fallback, TTL, concurrency  | approval/veto hooks when relevant                   | missing test or retry/recovery ambiguity  | invocation trace + acceptance result | must be factory-buildable and repo-loadable    |
| Agent DNA Example                 | Agent                 | bounded orchestration helper                     | linked director/skill scope                             | agent action or proposal                 | allowed tools and permissions only           | unapproved state/code mutation                      | local/web/GitHub + runtime boundary      | human review triggers by class                      | estate ambiguity or permission drift      | agent action logs                    | must map to parent system and portability      |
| Micro-Agent DNA Example           | Micro-Agent           | short-lived helper                               | parent scope + task envelope                            | bounded assist output                    | TTL-limited, parent-bound tools only         | cross-parent state bleed                            | mode + hardware limits                   | escalate on blocked tool or bad state               | spawn drift / stale scope                 | spawn/kill logs                      | must stay request-bounded                      |
| Plugin DNA Example                | Plugin                | external capability adapter                      | plugin input contract + environment                     | plugin output/event                      | sandbox, secret, compatibility scope         | unsupported runtime or unsafe host access           | OS/runtime compatibility + fallback      | policy and approval rules                           | host/runtime mismatch                     | plugin health + failure events       | must declare install/update/rollback semantics |
| Worker DNA Example                | Worker                | execution carrier                                | task envelope + route class                             | artifact or state update                 | worker secret scope + resource limits        | unsafe recycle/purge or unauthorized access         | env/hardware topology + Vayu class       | queue + founder gates where destructive             | queue saturation / hardware stress        | worker metrics + lifecycle logs      | must be mapped to topology and route matrix    |
| Self-Healing Crew DNA Example     | Self-Healing crew     | detect and recover bounded failures              | incident envelope + telemetry                           | recovery action / escalation             | safe auto-actions only                       | destructive auto-heal beyond approval boundary      | mode/hardware-aware healing              | founder threshold on destructive or premium actions | recovery loop / false heal                | RCA + incident logs                  | must link to troubleshooting and dashboard     |
| Self-Evolving Crew DNA Example    | Self-Evolving crew    | governed adaptation                              | analytics + mutation proposal                           | proposal / accepted change / rollback    | proposal-evaluate-freeze-rollback only       | silent mutation to mainline                         | budget + hardware-aware learning posture | founder/Kubera/Yama gates as needed                 | regression / drift / budget spike         | mutation diff + KPI logs             | must never bypass rollback and audit           |
| Troubleshooting Crew DNA Example  | Troubleshooting crew  | pinpoint and guide repair                        | alerts, incidents, blocker states                       | diagnostic path + suggested actions      | read-only diagnostics unless approved action | speculative destructive repair                      | screen/runbook/manual portability        | role-aware escalation                               | misclassification / missing link          | deep-link + runbook telemetry        | must map every major failure class             |
| Dashboard/Manual Crew DNA Example | Dashboard/manual crew | help + command surfaces                          | screen context + role + blocker state                   | help block / command / audit write       | only allowed commands by role                | unguarded destructive actions                       | local/web help availability              | founder vs creator help posture                     | stale docs / wrong guard                  | screen analytics + help usage        | must include contextual help and safety notes  |
| Factory/Training Crew DNA Example | Factory/training crew | training and registry governance                 | factory inputs + consent + resource profile             | trained artifact + registry entry        | strict policy/consent gates                  | training without data policy or acceptance          | hybrid/cloud posture explicit            | founder/policy approvals                            | bad dataset / drift / failed acceptance   | factory metrics + registry audit     | must separate training from inference cleanly  |

======================================================================
75. V15 ADDITIVE STRUCTURAL-GAP REPAIR LAW
======================================================================
This v15 pass is additive only.
- All v14 text remains intact and preserved.
- No inherited wording is deleted even where historical drift exists.
- v15 only appends canonical authority, sovereign namespace law, director/skill/route finality, forensic troubleshooting depth, self-evolving depth control, plugin hot-swap governance, machine-safe numeric normalization, underinjected module closure, and deeper avatar/lip-sync/editing parameterization.
- Where source-final closure is not provable, v15 keeps those items explicit as blocker packets or needs-confirmation packets.

Canonical authority rules:
- The canonical master artifact after this pass is Detailed_PRD_MASTERPIECE_v14.txt / .html.
- All retained v10/v11/v12 wording inside the preserved baseline remains historical carry-forward context unless a v14 reconciliation section explicitly declares it authoritative.
- Current authoritative release gating lives in FINAL_RELEASE_GATING_MATRIX_v14 and RELEASE_BLOCKER_LEDGER_v14.
- Current authoritative runtime closure layers for newly patched defects live in the v14 supplements appended in this pass.
- Historical labels remain visible by design to satisfy the zero-deletion law; they are not to be treated as current artifact identity when they conflict with v14 authority tables.

ROW_AND_SUBROW_CANONICALIZATION_LAYER_v15

Purpose:

Row law:

- Sub-row canonicalization never deletes the historical row; it attaches normalized field truth
- Execution-critical ambiguity must be surfaced at row or sub-row level, never hidden in prose only

| plugin row       | PLG-{class}-{nnn}       | plugin identity                                                 | PLG-NS-001 + parent subdomain     | lifecycle, hot-swap rules, env compatibility              | plugin manifest                           |
| screen row       | SCR-{surface}-{nnn}     | dashboard screen identity                                       | DBG-NS-001 + parent subdomain     | screen DTO/API/state/guard logic                          | screen contract pack                      |
| worker row       | WRK-{class}-{nnn}       | worker identity                                                 | WRK-NS-001 + route/skill bindings | execution topology, Vayu class, fallback                  | worker topology map                       |
| sub-row          | {ROW_ID}::field::{name} | field-level canonicalization for malformed or packetized fields | inherits parent row               | machine-safe field normalization without deleting history | field supplement / quality reconciliation |

| DIR-GOV-001 Yama          | release/policy veto must stay explicit          | Yes                         | all governed release routes | release gating matrix           | Founder           | Open only where linked blockers remain open |
| DIR-MED-* media directors | provider/callback/provider fallback sensitivity | Yes on premium media routes | avatar/voice/render routes  | provider auth closure artifacts | Builder/Founder   | Conditional until provider blockers close   |

79. CRITICAL-PATH SKILL CLOSURE AND ROUTE FINALITY

SKILL_EXECUTION_CRITICAL_CLOSURE_SUPPLEMENT_v14

This supplement appends release-focused closure criteria for execution-critical skills. It preserves the estate-level v13 runtime truth and isolates the remaining contract ambiguity that can still block critical-path release.

Critical-path closure rules:
- Any skill on a critical publish/render/healing route must have final input/output/retry/recovery/test truth before that route is fully releasable.
- Packet linkage is acceptable for auditability, but not sufficient for unattended or critical-path release if the linked field is execution-critical.
- Release blocking is route-specific, not estate-wide by default.

80. L3 FORENSIC TROUBLESHOOTING SPINE AND PARSER DEPTH
======================================================================
SELF_TROUBLESHOOTING_ENGINE_v14

Mission:
Turn detected failures into exact, navigable, operator-safe troubleshooting flows with direct links from alert to root cause, related runbooks, and allowed recovery actions.

Engine rules:
- Every failure class must map to a primary target area and a deep-link destination.
- The engine distinguishes what can be auto-fixed, what requires operator action, and what requires founder approval.
- Troubleshooting output must link to Self-Healing actions, release blockers, and User Manual sections.

| Failure_Class                       | Detection_Source                                       | Primary_Target_Area                    | Deep_Link_Target                            | Diagnostic_Panel                | Suggested_Actions                                        | Automated_First_Response_or_Manual_Steps              | Escalation_Path                                    | Related_Runbook   | Related_Manual_Section |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| Callback/Auth Failure               | provider health / callback timeout / auth refresh fail | Provider Usage + Publish/Avatar panels | Diagnostics > Provider RCA > callback trace | callback verifier / auth panel  | check callback route, refresh lifecycle, fallback poller | poller switch, token refresh, hold affected route     | Founder if release-blocking or premium impact      | provider runbook  | HELP-PROVIDERS         |
| Queue Saturation                    | queue depth + pressure threshold                       | Central Command + Queue Ops            | Diagnostics > Queue > worker lane           | queue metrics / worker topology | inspect queue class, stuck jobs, saturation source       | pause intake, reprioritize, DLQ spill, worker recycle | Builder/Founder on persistent saturation           | queue runbook     | HELP-CENTRAL-COMMAND   |
| Render Failure                      | render exit / GPU/Vayu stop / provider fail            | Render panel + Diagnostics             | Diagnostics > Render RCA                    | render timeline / worker logs   | inspect Vayu stop, asset validity, provider health       | checkpoint resume, reroute, safe-mode degrade         | Founder if premium reroute or destructive rollback | render runbook    | HELP-DIAGNOSTICS       |
| Policy/Governance Rejection         | Yama / tribunal fail                                   | Founder Queue + QA                     | Diagnostics > Verdict Trace                 | policy/verdict panel            | review failing rule and artifact                         | remodify, reject, or founder override where legal     | Founder only for override                          | policy runbook    | HELP-HOME              |
| UI Mutation Error                   | API schema fail / permission denied / stale data       | screen-local banner + Diagnostics      | Diagnostics > UI/API trace                  | screen dto/payload panel        | inspect payload, role guard, stale data version          | retry, refresh, downgrade to read-only                | Builder if schema drift                            | ui runbook        | HELP-CENTRAL-COMMAND   |
| Analytics Drift / Evolution Regress | analytics anomaly / quality regression                 | Optimization + Diagnostics             | Diagnostics > Evolution RCA                 | mutation trace / KPI diff       | review proposal, compare champion-challenger             | rollback, freeze mutations, revert parameter          | Founder/Builder when learning budget or policy hit | evolution runbook | HELP-CREATOR           |


L3_FORENSIC_TROUBLESHOOTING_SPINE_v15

Purpose:
v14 added a troubleshooting engine and STAR packeting. v15 deepens that into an L3 forensic spine with explicit telemetry classes, evidence chains, parser rules, and operator navigation.

Core law:
- Every incident packet must be reconstructable from telemetry sources and evidence-chain lineage.
- Troubleshooting must move from alert -> owning screen -> route -> director/skill/provider/worker -> evidence packet -> RCA.
- Where lower-level OS-specific forensics are not source-proven in this corpus, they remain NOT_PROVEN_IN_SOURCE and are not hallucinated as implemented truth.

| Telemetry_Class_ID | Layer                           | Primary_Sources                                               | Key_Fields                                                     | Primary_UI_Surface                | Primary_Use                         |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| TEL-KER-001        | Kernel / Route Telemetry        | intent_router, neural_router, worker router, route envelopes  | route, director, worker, cost, policy, fallback, latency       | Route Inspector / Mission Control | kernel-level runtime failures       |
| TEL-PRV-001        | Provider / Callback Telemetry   | callback payloads, poller state, auth refresh, quota failures | provider, callback, token, route, retry, cost                  | Provider Usage / Diagnostics      | provider/auth/release blockers      |
| TEL-WRK-001        | Worker / Queue Telemetry        | queue manager, allocator, worker lifecycle, DLQ               | worker class, queue depth, saturation, retry, recycle          | Worker Health / Queue Ops         | backpressure and worker instability |
| TEL-SKL-001        | Skill / Contract Telemetry      | skill invocation envelopes, validator/test hooks              | skill id, route id, contract version, acceptance, failure mode | Skill Diagnostics                 | contract/runtime mismatches         |
| TEL-UI-001         | UI / Screen Telemetry           | screen commands, API calls, DTOs, role guards                 | screen id, actor, payload, audit write, denial state           | Screen Diagnostics                | UI command failures                 |
| TEL-MED-001        | Media / Avatar / Sync Telemetry | voice, avatar, lip-sync, expression, render                   | clip id, phoneme, viseme, sync score, render stage             | Avatar / Voice Diagnostics        | media realism/sync failures         |

FORENSIC_TELEMETRY_SOURCE_MAP
| Failure_Class                     | Primary_Telemetry_Source | Secondary_Sources | Primary_Target_Area        | Deep_Link_Target              | Runbook_Link         |
| --------------------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------- |
| route selection failure           | TEL-KER-001              | TEL-UI-001        | Route Inspector            | /central-command?route_id=... | RUNBOOK-ROUTE-001    |
| provider callback/auth failure    | TEL-PRV-001              | TEL-KER-001       | Provider Usage             | /provider-usage?provider=...  | RUNBOOK-PROVIDER-001 |
| queue saturation / worker stall   | TEL-WRK-001              | TEL-KER-001       | Worker Health              | /workers?queue=...            | RUNBOOK-WORKER-001   |
| skill contract/runtime mismatch   | TEL-SKL-001              | TEL-KER-001       | Skill Diagnostics          | /skills?skill_id=...          | RUNBOOK-SKILL-001    |
| screen/API command failure        | TEL-UI-001               | TEL-KER-001       | Screen Diagnostics         | /diagnostics?screen_id=...    | RUNBOOK-UI-001       |
| lip-sync / avatar realism failure | TEL-MED-001              | TEL-PRV-001       | Avatar / Voice Diagnostics | /avatar?clip_id=...           | RUNBOOK-MEDIA-001    |

TROUBLESHOOTING_EVIDENCE_CHAIN_LAW
1. incident_id must bind to route_id or screen_id or worker_id at minimum.
2. every RCA packet must link to raw telemetry references, not only human summary text.
3. STAR fields are reporting fields; parser confidence and source lineage must also be preserved.
4. manual operator notes are appended, never substituted for machine telemetry.
5. unresolved low-level host forensic layers remain NOT_PROVEN_IN_SOURCE unless explicitly defined in source.


STAR_FORENSIC_PARSER_ENGINE_SPEC_v15

Purpose:
v14 had STAR-style packet fields. v15 adds the missing parser-engine specification so STAR is not only a reporting shell.

Parser stages:
1. ingest telemetry envelopes from route/provider/worker/skill/UI/media classes
2. normalize timestamps, IDs, actor context, and route lineage
3. extract Situation, Task, Action, Result from machine signals plus operator notes
4. bind root cause hypotheses to evidence references
5. score parser confidence
6. validate required packet fields before the RCA packet is accepted

Required parser fields:
- incident_id
- source_telemetry_ids
- parser_confidence
- evidence_chain_complete_YN
- situation
- task
- action
- result
- owner
- impact_scope
- root_cause
- remediation
- prevention_rule

Validation rules:
- no STAR packet is machine-complete without source_telemetry_ids
- parser_confidence below acceptance threshold routes to human review
- root_cause must reference at least one telemetry artifact or explicit human note
- prevention_rule must bind to an owning layer or remain explicit as unresolved

NOT_PROVEN_IN_SOURCE boundary:
- exact host-forensic parser adapters equivalent to journalctl/dmesg are not proven as implemented truth in the accessible corpus and therefore are not asserted here as final runtime components.


VDI_CITRIX_RCA_STATUS_NOTE_v15

Accessible corpus status: Citrix / VDI appear in project knowledge sources and legacy context, but a finalized VDI/Citrix RCA subsystem is not proven as mainline runtime truth for this Windows/macOS Creator OS PRD. Therefore v15 preserves it as historical or optional backend context rather than asserting it as canonical implemented mainline logic.
