81. SELF-EVOLVING DEPTH AND RECURSIVE GOVERNANCE
======================================================================
SELF_EVOLVING_LAYER_REGISTER_v13

Self-Evolving is now a governed runtime layer rather than a promise-only optimization loop. It binds mutation proposals, evaluation gates, rollback, budget law, and anti-drift governance to the existing analytics/evolution logic in v12.

| Evolution_ID | Subsystem                          | Trigger                                                              | Eligible_Action                                          | Ineligible_Action                                    | Approval_Boundary                                                      | Mode_Scope         | Audit_Output               | Dashboard_Surface             | Dependencies                                  | Acceptance_Criteria                                 |
| ------------ | ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| SE-001       | Mutation Proposal Gate             | analytics drift, fatigue score, threshold breach, route inefficiency | propose skill/route/prompt/threshold change              | no silent mainline mutation                          | No automatic mainline merge without evaluation gate                    | all                | mutation proposal envelope | Optimization / Evolution      | Analytics loop / Pattern Recognition / Narada | proposal fully scoped with rollback and owner       |
| SE-002       | Evaluation Gate                    | new proposal created                                                 | run champion-challenger test or offline validator suite  | no production cutover on untested mutation           | Founder/tribunal for high-impact policy/cost changes                   | local,hybrid,cloud | evaluation scorecard       | Experiment panel              | Mutation Engine / QA                          | evidence produced for accept/reject                 |
| SE-003       | Skill Evolution Engine             | validated mutation in skill behavior                                 | rev new .md skill or modifier pack                       | no overwrite of canonical skill without version bump | Human if policy/cost/hardware class changes                            | local,hybrid,cloud | skill evolution log        | Skill Registry / GitHub       | Skill repo architecture                       | versioned artifact emitted                          |
| SE-004       | Route Evolution Engine             | route quality-cost mismatch or provider drift                        | adjust route preference, fallback chain, or exec bias    | no unapproved provider class introduction            | Founder/Kubera/Yama for payment/policy shifts                          | hybrid,cloud       | route evolution ledger     | Routes / Cost screens         | Route matrices / Worker router                | route diff + rollback plan recorded                 |
| SE-005       | Threshold Evolution Engine         | stable evidence that thresholds are too lax/strict                   | propose new timeout, retry, alert, or quality thresholds | no threshold mutation without bounded range          | Human for safety or legal thresholds                                   | all                | parameter change audit     | Diagnostics / Parameter panel | Parameter register                            | new threshold validated and revertable              |
| SE-006       | Model/Provider Selection Evolution | provider health, cost, quality, or hardware drift                    | re-rank provider selection or model family               | no secret/provider scope breach                      | Human for enterprise/payment/provider onboarding                       | hybrid,cloud       | selection change audit     | Provider Usage                | Provider registry                             | selection change preserves policy and budget law    |
| SE-007       | Rollback / Freeze Controller       | bad mutation, regression, cost spike, legal or quality breach        | rollback to prior version, freeze mutations in scope     | no continued auto-evolution in failing scope         | No for automatic rollback within tested boundary; Yes for broad freeze | all                | rollback/freeze record     | Founder / Diagnostics         | release/blocker ledger                        | regression contained and canonical version restored |


SELF_EVOLVING_DEPTH_AND_MUTATION_GOVERNANCE_LAYER_v15

Purpose:
v13 introduced a first-class Self-Evolving layer. v15 deepens it with mutation depth control, lineage, backpropagation law, and anti-drift governance.

Mutation depth model:
- depth 0: telemetry observation only
- depth 1: proposal only; no live mutation
- depth 2: sandbox or offline evaluation
- depth 3: bounded promoted mutation with rollback ready
- depth >3: forbidden unless explicit founder/tribunal approval

Recursive depth control:
- recursive evolution chains must carry mutation_lineage_id and depth_count
- every mutation must reference its parent artifact or parameter version
- no silent multi-hop mutation without lineage trace

Telemetry-to-registry backpropagation law:
- telemetry may propose registry or threshold changes only through a mutation proposal packet
- no raw telemetry directly mutates canonical registry truth
- accepted mutations must pass evaluation + validation + rollback readiness
- all promoted changes must emit audit + diff + rollback references

Self-correction validation loop:
1. collect telemetry
2. propose bounded mutation
3. evaluate against champion baseline
4. validate policy/cost/hardware constraints
5. promote or reject
6. monitor post-promotion regression
7. rollback/freeze on failure or drift

Anti-drift rules:
- no mutation may bypass policy or budget gates
- no mutation may widen its own permissions
- no mutation may silently alter founder-only thresholds
- hardware-degraded modes may observe and propose, but cannot self-promote premium or enterprise-affecting mutations


RDT_NEEDS_CONFIRMATION_PACKET_v15

Status: NEEDS_CONFIRMATION

Desired primitive:
Recursive Depth Token (RDT)

Why it is desired:
- provides a machine-safe way to bound mutation recursion, self-correction depth, and chained adaptive actions
- makes autonomous evolution and recursive decomposition easier to reason about at kernel level

What gap it fills:
- depth-count governance for self-evolving loops
- bounded recursion for work decomposition and mutation chains
- stronger anti-drift enforcement

What source is missing:
- the accessible corpus in this project does not prove an already-finalized RDT schema or canonical runtime contract

What artifact is required to finalize:
- a source-backed RDT schema / policy pack / lineage contract

Interim legal posture:
- v15 uses mutation depth model and lineage counters as a governance substitute
- do not treat RDT as implemented truth until the missing artifact exists


======================================================================
82. PLUGIN HOT-SWAP AND DYNAMIC LOADING GOVERNANCE
======================================================================
PLUGIN_HOT_SWAP_AND_DYNAMIC_LOADING_LAW_v15

Purpose:
v14 had plugin lifecycle but not a strong hot-swap / dynamic-loading governance law. v15 closes that structural gap without hallucinating an unsupported exact loader implementation.

Hot-swap law:
- live plugin changes are allowed only when compatibility, permissions, route impact, and rollback are known
- no plugin may hot-swap into a running critical route if safety or provider callback stability cannot be preserved
- no-reboot posture is a governance goal, not an excuse to skip validation
- exact loader mechanism (for example importlib/ctypes-specific implementation) is NOT_PROVEN_IN_SOURCE as a finalized runtime detail in the accessible corpus and is therefore not asserted as implemented truth here

| Change_Type                                | Hot_Swap_Eligible                 | Required_Revalidation                           | Audit_Write | Observability_Hook | Mode_Scope                           | Rollback_Rule                             |
| ------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| manifest-only config change                | Yes                               | schema + permission + compatibility check       | yes         | yes                | all except in-flight destructive ops | rollback to prior manifest version        |
| version patch update same interface        | Conditional                       | compatibility matrix + route impact check       | yes         | yes                | local,hybrid                         | rollback if contract diff or health fail  |
| plugin class change / capability expansion | No live hot-swap without approval | policy + secret scope + fallback check          | yes         | yes                | all                                  | disable and stage for reviewed deployment |
| provider bridge rebind                     | Conditional                       | route + callback + provider fallback validation | yes         | yes                | hybrid,cloud                         | return to prior bridge or poller mode     |

SKILL_PLUGIN_RUNTIME_REBIND_RULES
- skill-to-plugin bindings must remain version-aware
- a plugin hot-swap that changes output shape requires skill validator re-check
- route criticality outranks plugin convenience
- web/hosted AI surfaces must respect plugin-host capability limits

PLUGIN_COMPATIBILITY_REVALIDATION_MATRIX
| Plugin_Class       | Must_Revalidate                                          | Affected_Areas                               | Failure_if_Skipped                 |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| browser automation | permissions, selectors, auth posture                     | dashboard actions, scraping, publish assists | false success / brittle automation |
| media bridge       | output contract, callback handling, worker compatibility | voice/avatar/render                          | broken media pipeline              |
| analytics bridge   | quota/auth schema                                        | analytics/evolution                          | bad telemetry or drift             |
| repo/build plugin  | branch policy, destructive rights                        | GitHub/runtime                               | unsafe repo mutation               |

======================================================================
P1-3 — INSERT DIRECTLY AFTER: PLUGIN_HOT_SWAP_AND_DYNAMIC_LOADING_LAW
======================================================================

SECTION: DYNAMIC_LOADING_RUNTIME_GOVERNANCE

Because exact importlib/ctypes loader details are not source-final, keep governance exact and mechanism honest.
Rules:
- hot-load allowed only for non-critical plugin classes by default
- critical-path plugin/skill rebind requires compatibility revalidation and route quiescence
- failed load must rollback to last-known-good binding
- no live-route mutation without route protection lock and audit event

SECTION: PLUGIN_SKILL_HOT_SWAP_COMPATIBILITY_MATRIX

Columns:
Module_Class
HotSwap_Eligible_YN
Compatibility_Check
Rollback_Path
Live_Route_Allowed_YN
Release_Blocking_YN

SECTION: LIVE_ROUTE_PROTECTION_DURING_REBIND_RULES

- active publish/render routes may not be rebound mid-flight
- research/analytics routes may allow controlled rebind if state invariants hold
- Vayu/Chitragupta/Ganesha/Yama/Kubera adapters are never hot-swapped on active critical path without maintenance mode

83. PARAMETER DATA-QUALITY AND MACHINE-SAFE NUMERIC LAW
======================================================================
PARAMETER_DATA_QUALITY_RECONCILIATION_SUPPLEMENT_v14

This supplement preserves all current parameter text while classifying values that are malformed, overly freeform, or not yet machine-safe enough for automated runtime execution.

Note:
- Some malformed strings were cited in the v13 flaw audit rather than preserved in the canonical appendix itself.
- Those values remain visible here as reported corruption examples so builders can normalize them without silently erasing the audit trail.

| Parameter_ID | Original_Value                                       | Defect_Type                     | Canonical_Interpretation                                                               | Source_Confidence | Machine_Safe_YN | Critical_Path_Impact          | Required_Fix_Artifact        | Required_Owner  |
| ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | --------------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------- | --------------- |
| PRM-202      | 0.50.1.00 (reported corruption in prior audit trail) | merge/format ambiguity          | 0.50..1.00                                                                             | Medium            | Yes             | Yes                           | parameter register / builder | Builder         |
| PRM-204      | 30.1800s (reported corruption in prior audit trail)  | merge/format ambiguity          | 30..1800s                                                                              | Medium            | Yes             | Yes                           | parameter register / builder | Builder         |
| PRM-211      | 1.10 / 0.5 (reported ambiguity in prior audit trail) | rendering ambiguity             | 1 step..1 job / use explicit integer or duration form only                             | Low               | No              | Conditional                   | parameter register / builder | Builder         |
| PRM-205      | project specific                                     | non-machine-safe freeform value | must resolve to namespace-specific numeric/contract value before enterprise automation | High              | No              | Yes for enterprise automation | enterprise budget policy     | Founder+Kubera  |
| PRM-207      | policy bounded                                       | non-machine-safe freeform value | replace with validator-backed admission rule set before factory automation             | High              | No              | Conditional                   | factory threshold artifact   | Founder+Builder |


PARAMETER_DATA_QUALITY_RECONCILIATION_SUPPLEMENT_v15

Purpose:
v14 preserved malformed or ambiguous parameter values under no-deletion law. v15 keeps them visible but adds machine-safe normalization guidance and release impact classification.

| Parameter_ID | Original_Value   | Defect_Type                        | Canonical_Interpretation                                | Source_Confidence | Machine_Safe_YN                    | Critical_Path_Impact        | Required_Fix_Artifact                       |
| ------------ | ---------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| PRM-202      | 0.50.1.00        | merge/format corruption            | 0.50..1.00                                              | Medium            | Yes                                | queue saturation thresholds | parameter normalization artifact            |
| PRM-204      | 30.1800s         | range delimiter corruption         | 30..1800s                                               | Medium            | Yes                                | provider failover timing    | provider/runtime parameter fix              |
| PRM-205      | project specific | non-machine-safe narrative literal | founder-defined namespace cap required                  | Low               | No until bound to budget namespace | evolution budget governance | enterprise_budget_policy.yaml or equivalent |
| PRM-211      | 1 step..1 job    | semi-structured narrative range    | request-bounded TTL with class-specific values required | Low               | Conditional                        | micro-agent runtime policy  | micro-agent runtime matrix                  |

MACHINE_SAFE_NUMERIC_NORMALIZATION_LAW
- numeric ranges must use one canonical delimiter pattern: min..max
- durations must include explicit units
- narrative literals are not machine-safe unless paired with a formal owner-defined value source
- parameters that influence route legality, provider callback timing, or worker safety are release-significant

85. BEST PRACTICES AND BEST RESOLUTION FRAMEWORK

DNA_BEST_PRACTICES_AND_IMPLEMENTATION_DISCIPLINE_v14

These are operational best-practice notes shaped by the strongest finalized DNA materials in the project, especially the universal Director DNA and universal Skill DNA patterns.

| DNA_Family                | Purpose                              | Implementation_Best_Practices                                                   | Failure_Avoidance_Practices                              | Observability_Practices                                 | Dashboard_Control_Implications                               | Security_Practices                              | Portability_Practices                           | Anti_Drift_Notes                                              |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Director DNA              | director decisions and arbitration   | keep reads/writes/lock authority explicit per row                               | never allow hidden cross-domain mutation                 | log every override, veto, reroute, and approval         | surface decision, reason, and blocker state in dashboard     | least-privilege namespace writes                | bind modes/hardware/cost to row truth           | do not let historical aliases replace canonical director row  |
| Skill DNA                 | atomic execution units               | single-responsibility skill contract with deterministic input/output boundaries | no skill without retry/recovery/failure notes            | trace skill invocation, fallback, and validator results | show route + worker + acceptance linkage                     | tool policy + patch policy must be bounded      | respect local/web/GitHub portability notes      | do not let examples stand in for estate-wide closure          |
| Agent DNA                 | ecosystem/agentic runtime            | explicit parent system, permissions, tool rights, and review triggers           | no hidden tool escalation                                | audit every agent action that mutates state or code     | show linked directors/skills and runtime boundary            | web/local/GitHub boundaries explicit            | keep counts and estate posture source-faithful  | do not fake a row roster the source does not provide          |
| Worker DNA                | execution workers and queues         | deterministic queue class + worker class + topology mapping                     | no silent worker recycle or DLQ purge                    | worker lifecycle and saturation visible in diagnostics  | surface queue pressure and recycle events                    | least-privilege secret scope per worker         | hardware-aware Vayu rules mandatory             | avoid mixing local/cloud semantics without explicit route law |
| Route Controller DNA      | route ownership and fallback         | owner/veto/fallback/quality/deadline all explicit                               | no route without legal downgrade path                    | route decision envelopes auditable                      | dashboard must show chosen route and fallback chain          | Kubera/Yama/Founder gate respected              | mode and hardware specificity explicit          | do not call route covered if only named once                  |
| Self-Healing Crew DNA     | auto-recovery subsystem              | bounded auto-actions only                                                       | never cross founder-only destructive boundaries silently | every healing action writes RCA-linked audit row        | mission control must show failing node + next safe step      | safe-mode and hardware-aware behavior mandatory | local/hybrid/cloud healing differences explicit | do not normalize away incident-class differences              |
| Self-Evolving Crew DNA    | governed mutation subsystem          | proposal->evaluation->rollback/freeze always explicit                           | no auto-mainline mutation without gate                   | mutation audit and KPI diff mandatory                   | evolution surfaces visible in optimization screens           | budget and policy limits explicit               | hardware-aware learning posture explicit        | anti-drift and anti-corruption rules mandatory                |
| Troubleshooting Crew DNA  | diagnostics and operator guidance    | map alert to exact failing area and runbook                                     | do not hide failure class or ambiguity                   | every suggested action should be auditable              | dashboard deep-link must point to root and supporting panels | permission-aware troubleshooting                | support novice and expert guidance              | never leave operator with prose-only advice                   |
| Dashboard/Manual Crew DNA | help and control plane               | contextual help at screen/widget/form level                                     | do not bury release blockers or warnings                 | audit UI mutations and reversals                        | manual/help linked to screens and troubleshooting            | role-aware help visibility                      | offline/help-cache posture explicit             | avoid generic docs divorced from control plane                |
| Factory/Training Crew DNA | voice/avatar training and registries | training/inference split explicit                                               | no training without policy/consent gate                  | registry writes and model admissions auditable          | factory screens show acceptance and failure surfaces         | resource profile + deployment path explicit     | portable only where allowed                     | do not treat factory modules as generic future ideas          |

Purpose:
v14 and earlier artifacts contained many laws but not one consolidated best-practices / best-resolution / best-workflow layer. v15 closes that gap with operational guidance, not motivational prose.

| Family                  | Best_Practices                                                           | Best_Resolution_Patterns                           | Best_Observability_and_Governance                         | Portability_or_Hardware_Note                          |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| Directors               | declare domain/subdomain/namespace clearly; route ownership explicit     | bounded reroute/degrade over silent fail           | audit lineage on override and critical decisions          | hardware-aware allowed modes per director             |
| Skills                  | contract-first input/output; validator-linked                            | critical-path skills need fallback + observability | trace failure to route and screen impact                  | portable repo + local/web/GitHub notes mandatory      |
| Agents/Micro-Agents     | least privilege and request-bounded TTL                                  | spawn only with owner, route, and audit context    | kill/rollback path exists before automation               | hosted/local portability explicit                     |
| Plugins                 | hot-swap only after compatibility revalidation                           | no secret-scope widening via live patch            | observe plugin health and route blast radius              | web/plugin-host limitations explicit                  |
| Routes                  | owner/veto/fallback/worker/provider law explicit                         | degrade safely before premium overreach            | route legality must survive ambiguity checks              | tie routes to screen visibility and audit path        |
| Troubleshooting         | alert -> root -> evidence -> STAR -> runbook                             | never trust summary without source telemetry       | human and automated remediation separated                 | help/manual linkage required                          |
| Self-Healing            | safe auto-actions only; destructive or premium-crossing actions escalate | recovery requires checkpoint or explicit fallback  | no silent auto-remediation without audit                  | hardware stress and budget law gate healing           |
| Self-Evolving           | mutation proposals only through evaluation/rollback gates                | lineage and depth counters mandatory               | no self-widening permissions                              | budget and hardware posture bound to evolution rights |
| Dashboard/Control Plane | command-level guards and audit writes for primary screens                | important failures need deep link and help path    | novice and advanced/manual surfaces both required         | stale/partial/error states first-class                |
| Media/Avatar            | sync and temporal coherence are measured, not assumed                    | use acceptance thresholds and review gates         | fallbacks must exist for low-resource or provider failure | clone-grade realism requires consent and governance   |

======================================================================
86. REALISTIC AI AVATAR PRODUCTION DISCIPLINE
======================================================================
REALISTIC_AI_AVATAR_PRODUCTION_DISCIPLINE_v15

Purpose:
The project ambition is clone-grade, studio-quality avatar output. v15 strengthens the media/avatar layers with explicit realism, coherence, governance, and review discipline.

Avatar production discipline:
- identity preservation: actor identity, face shape, skin tone, and presenter continuity must remain stable across takes
- face consistency law: no silent face drift across segments or rerenders
- shot consistency law: framing, lens feel, camera height, and lighting style must remain coherent within a content package
- pose/gesture consistency: body language must match script intent and persona constraints
- temporal coherence: adjacent frames must avoid jitter, sudden facial jumps, or impossible pose transitions
- training/inference distinction: model training flows are governed differently from inference/render flows
- clone-grade realism safeguards: consent, persona rights, and review gates are mandatory before high-fidelity identity cloning or training
- hardware-aware posture: local degraded modes may preview or low-fidelity render, while high-fidelity avatar paths may require hybrid/cloud offload

Runtime controls:
- avatar identity profile
- shot template profile
- gesture intensity profile
- expression ceiling/floor
- lighting/scene consistency preference
- offload threshold for heavy visual render

Acceptance gates:
- identity consistency pass
- lip-sync / viseme quality pass
- temporal coherence pass
- uncanny-artifact pass/fail
- packaging-fit review
- founder/creator approval rules for public-facing clone-grade content

Failure surfaces:
- face drift
- gesture mismatch
- temporal jitter
- scene inconsistency
- low-resource preview diverging too far from production render
- provider render artifact / callback fail

Fallback paths:
- lower-fidelity preview
- reduced motion / static presenter fallback
- provider reroute
- founder/manual review hold

---------------------------------------------------------------------

The full v16 block below is preserved verbatim.

C-1 — INSERT DIRECTLY AFTER: REALISTIC_AI_AVATAR_PRODUCTION_DISCIPLINE
======================================================================

SECTION: AVATAR_MEDIA_RUNTIME_CLOSURE

Closed source-backed realism controls:
- face consistency law
- shot consistency
- pose variation +/-15°
- natural blink 12-20/min and gaze drift
- skin realism via SSIM >=0.85
- 1080p minimum, 4K target
- source material requirements: 2-5 min 1080p training video, soft frontal lighting, 15-20 min clean voice data
- long-running avatar jobs must use non-blocking orchestration
- HeyGen async handling with immediate S3 persistence due URL TTL

======================================================================
87. LIP-SYNC AND EMOTION-SYNC ENGINEERING LAYER
======================================================================
LIP_SYNC_AND_EMOTION_SYNC_ENGINEERING_LAYER_v15

Purpose:
This layer translates the project’s realism goals into measurable engineering law.

Core contracts:
- phoneme alignment workflow: phoneme_timeline drives mouth-shape/viseme expectation
- viseme mapping law: mouth shapes must map to supported viseme classes with measurable tolerance
- frame alignment tolerance: target alignment preserves the DNA/PRD expectation of tight sync where already source-backed
- audio drift detection: detect late/early mouth motion, dropped phoneme spans, or drift after rerender
- correction loop: resync, re-time, or reroute before publish approval
- emotion-intent mapping: script emotion tags + prosody signals inform facial micro-expression and body-language timing
- micro-expression timing: eye/brow/jaw/cheek changes must align with emotional beats
- jaw/cheek/eye coordination: mouth motion alone is not sufficient for realism
- prosody analyzer integration: emotional emphasis and cadence modulate expression and edit timing
- acceptance thresholds: sync score, emotion coherence score, and drift ceiling must be reviewable
- fallback/resync logic: if exact sync fails, rerun resync path, reduce motion intensity, or hold for review

Observability metrics:
- lip_sync_score
- viseme_alignment_score
- audio_drift_ms
- emotion_coherence_score
- micro_expression_confidence
- resync_attempt_count

- realism tribunal must evaluate lip-sync, eye movement realism, skin lighting, voice emotion alignment
- if score <0.85 render rejected / karmic rejection loop

======================================================================
88. EDITING PATTERN AND RETENTION ENGINEERING LAYER
======================================================================
EDITING_PATTERN_AND_RETENTION_ENGINEERING_LAYER_v15

Purpose:
The Creator OS is not only a pipeline; it is a retention-driven production system. v15 turns editing/retention ambition into technical workflow law.

Editing pattern discipline:
- pacing logic: edit density must match topic complexity and audience expectation
- pattern interrupt law: use structural interruption intentionally, not randomly
- retention hook timing: first beats, first scene shifts, and first payoff windows are explicit review points
- scene density rules: informational density and visual density must remain readable
- cut rhythm models: cuts, punch-ins, zooms, reframes, and text hits must follow a deliberate cadence
- text/emphasis timing: subtitles and emphasis overlays must avoid cognitive overload
- sound-design synchronization: cues and music transitions must align to narrative and emotional beats
- beat-sync logic: where music or montage exists, edit markers should align to beat intent rather than arbitrary cuts
- emotional pacing: tension, surprise, clarity, and payoff need deliberate spacing
- payoff cadence: viewers should receive clear information or narrative payoff at planned intervals
- educational clarity pacing: high-information content must preserve legibility, recap, and breath points
- storytelling rhythm: intro, conflict, reveal, proof, payoff, CTA, and loop-back must stay structurally clear

Retention mechanics translation layer:
- hook score
- 3-second retention checkpoint
- scene novelty pacing
- drop-risk detection points
- repair pattern triggers
- title/thumbnail promise alignment check
- payoff density per minute
- CTA timing discipline

SECTION: EDITING_RETENTION_RUNTIME_CLOSURE

- 3-second retention rule
- captions, zoom cuts, title cards, end screens, lower thirds, sound-design synchronization
- beat-sync / pacing / scene ordering / transition selection families
- MrBeast-style retention mechanics appear in skill tree as editing intelligence pattern, but convert to technical rules, not fan commentary
- hook timing and first-30s psychological hook maps are optimization-critical artifacts
- proxy rendering / frame tiling / lazy loading support average hardware compatibility

cut density
subtitle pacing
hook timing
retention checkpoint timing
zoom frequency
sound cue spacing
review thresholds

90. DNA BEST PRACTICES AND CREW CONSTRUCTION LIBRARY

MODULE_AND_CREW_DNA_EXAMPLE_LIBRARY_v14

Example DNA templates to guide future module and crew creation without inventing unrelated architecture.

| Example_Name                      | Module_or_Crew_Class  | Constitutional_Role                              | Inputs                                                  | Outputs                                  | Allowed_Actions                              | Forbidden_Actions                                   | Dependencies                             | Governance_Rules                                    | Failure_Surfaces                          | Observability_Hooks                  | Acceptance_Standard                            |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- |
| Director DNA Example              | Director              | constitutional arbiter + owner of bounded domain | creator/founder intent, dossier fields, route envelopes | decision, approval, veto, reroute, notes | domain-bounded read/write/lock only          | cross-domain mutation without law, secret overreach | owning rows, routes, modes, thresholds   | founder/policy/cost gates                           | ambiguous shadow/domain, illegal override | decision trace + blocker link        | must be row-bound and audit-visible            |
| Skill DNA Example                 | Skill                 | atomic execution brain                           | input contract + upstream artifacts                     | output contract + validator/test links   | allowed tools/patch rules only               | unbounded mutation, hidden retries                  | route/worker/fallback, TTL, concurrency  | approval/veto hooks when relevant                   | missing test or retry/recovery ambiguity  | invocation trace + acceptance result | must be factory-buildable and repo-loadable    |
| Agent DNA Example                 | Agent                 | bounded orchestration helper                     | linked director/skill scope                             | agent action or proposal                 | allowed tools and permissions only           | unapproved state/code mutation                      | local/web/GitHub + runtime boundary      | human review triggers by class                      | estate ambiguity or permission drift      | agent action logs                    | must map to parent system and portability      |
| Micro-Agent DNA Example           | Micro-Agent           | short-lived helper                               | parent scope + task envelope                            | bounded assist output                    | TTL-limited, parent-bound tools only         | cross-parent state bleed                            | mode + hardware limits                   | escalate on blocked tool or bad state               | spawn drift / stale scope                 | spawn/kill logs                      | must stay request-bounded                      |
| Plugin DNA Example                | Plugin                | external capability adapter                      | plugin input contract + environment                     | plugin output/event                      | sandbox, secret, compatibility scope         | unsupported runtime or unsafe host access           | OS/runtime compatibility + fallback      | policy and approval rules                           | host/runtime mismatch                     | plugin health + failure events       | must declare install/update/rollback semantics |
| Worker DNA Example                | Worker                | execution carrier                                | task envelope + route class                             | artifact or state update                 | worker secret scope + resource limits        | unsafe recycle/purge or unauthorized access         | env/hardware topology + Vayu class       | queue + founder gates where destructive             | queue saturation / hardware stress        | worker metrics + lifecycle logs      | must be mapped to topology and route matrix    |
| Self-Healing Crew DNA Example     | Self-Healing crew     | detect and recover bounded failures              | incident envelope + telemetry                           | recovery action / escalation             | safe auto-actions only                       | destructive auto-heal beyond approval boundary      | mode/hardware-aware healing              | founder threshold on destructive or premium actions | recovery loop / false heal                | RCA + incident logs                  | must link to troubleshooting and dashboard     |
| Self-Evolving Crew DNA Example    | Self-Evolving crew    | governed adaptation                              | analytics + mutation proposal                           | proposal / accepted change / rollback    | proposal-evaluate-freeze-rollback only       | silent mutation to mainline                         | budget + hardware-aware learning posture | founder/Kubera/Yama gates as needed                 | regression / drift / budget spike         | mutation diff + KPI logs             | must never bypass rollback and audit           |
| Troubleshooting Crew DNA Example  | Troubleshooting crew  | pinpoint and guide repair                        | alerts, incidents, blocker states                       | diagnostic path + suggested actions      | read-only diagnostics unless approved action | speculative destructive repair                      | screen/runbook/manual portability        | role-aware escalation                               | misclassification / missing link          | deep-link + runbook telemetry        | must map every major failure class             |
| Dashboard/Manual Crew DNA Example | Dashboard/manual crew | help + command surfaces                          | screen context + role + blocker state                   | help block / command / audit write       | only allowed commands by role                | unguarded destructive actions                       | local/web help availability              | founder vs creator help posture                     | stale docs / wrong guard                  | screen analytics + help usage        | must include contextual help and safety notes  |
| Factory/Training Crew DNA Example | Factory/training crew | training and registry governance                 | factory inputs + consent + resource profile             | trained artifact + registry entry        | strict policy/consent gates                  | training without data policy or acceptance          | hybrid/cloud posture explicit            | founder/policy approvals                            | bad dataset / drift / failed acceptance   | factory metrics + registry audit     | must separate training from inference cleanly  |


DNA_BEST_PRACTICES_AND_CREW_CONSTRUCTION_LIBRARY_v15

Purpose:
v14 added DNA best-practices and example libraries. v15 deepens them into a stronger crew-construction standard so new modules do not get invented loosely or without operational boundaries.

DNA best-practice layers by family:
- Director DNA: constitutional role, domain/subdomain, authority boundary, override boundary, dashboard visibility, failure surfaces
- Skill DNA: input/output contract, tool policy, retry/recovery policy, portability, acceptance test
- Agent DNA: parent system, permissions, tool rights, runtime boundary, audit visibility
- Micro-Agent DNA: request-bounded TTL, parent binding, tool/shard rights, kill conditions
- Plugin DNA: manifest identity, hot-swap eligibility, compatibility matrix, fallback law
- Troubleshooting crew DNA: evidence chain, parser linkage, alert-to-root navigation, runbook binding
- Self-Healing crew DNA: auto-action boundaries, approval thresholds, rollback legality
- Self-Evolving crew DNA: mutation lineage, evaluation gate, rollback/freeze, anti-drift controls
- Dashboard/help/manual crew DNA: contextual help, role-aware guidance, mutation safety notes
- Media/avatar/factory crew DNA: realism thresholds, sync rules, review gates, portability constraints

Example DNA Template:
- Name
- Purpose
- Constitutional Role
- Inputs
- Outputs
- Boundaries
- Allowed Actions
- Forbidden Actions
- Dependencies
- Runtime Modes
- Governance Rules
- Escalation Rules
- Failure Surfaces
- Recovery Expectations
- Observability Hooks
- Portability Notes
- Best Practices
- Acceptance Standard

- Where source proof is still incomplete, the skill remains route-scoped, release-gated, and owner-linked rather than silently normalized.

======================================================================
ROUTE_OWNER_VETO_FINALITY_MATRIX_v18
======================================================================
Rule:
- No route is release-legal if owner, veto authority, required skills, allowed providers, worker class, fallback chain, or downgrade path remains unresolved.
- Route release inherits provider/auth, director, and critical-path skill closure state; no lower-layer ambiguity is silently masked.

======================================================================
ROUTE_FALLBACK_AND_DOWNGRADE_BINDING_v18
======================================================================
- ROUTE-FAST-001 -> safe standard lane -> attended tribunal if vetoed.
- ROUTE-STANDARD-001 -> route-specific fallback chain -> founder review on legal/policy conflict.
- ROUTE-AVATAR-001 -> premium provider -> local preview / static presenter -> founder manual review.
- ROUTE-VOICE-001 -> premium voice -> XTTS/local voice -> attended publish if auth open.
- ROUTE-PUBLISH-001 -> unattended publish only when callback/auth closure is complete -> otherwise attended manual upload.
- ROUTE-HEAL-001 -> replay -> manual rollback -> founder-controlled safe mode.

======================================================================
CHITRAGUPTA_OPS_AUTHORITY_LAYER_v18
======================================================================
- Chitragupta is the canonical audit and RCA authority for incident evidence, blocker lineage, route trace continuity, and recovery/evolution audit writes.
- Chitragupta may classify, correlate, and surface incidents automatically, but destructive recovery, release overrides, and policy exceptions remain founder/tribunal/Yama/Kubera governed.
- Any unresolved provider/auth or critical-path route blocker must appear in Mission Control and Chitragupta views before the route is marked release-legal.

ALERT_TO_ROOT_RCA_RUNTIME_DTO_MAP_v18
======================================================================
| Failure_Class | Detection_Source | Deep_Link_Target | Suggested_Actions | Escalation_Path | Related_Runbook |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| callback_failure | provider heartbeat / webhook miss | MC-PROVIDER-TRACE | switch to poller, review callback contract, attended fallback | Builder -> Founder | provider-auth-runbook |
| route_illegal | router legality check | MC-ROUTE-TRACE | downgrade, tribunal path, fix owner/veto | Ganesha -> Yama | route-governance-runbook |
| render_failure | Vayu / render worker | MC-WORKER-TRACE | checkpoint resume, low-motion fallback, provider reroute | Vayu -> Vishnu -> Founder | render-recovery-runbook |
| policy_veto | Yama / tribunal | MC-BLOCKERS | remodify, attended founder review | Yama -> Founder | governance-runbook |
| queue_saturation | queue manager | MC-WORKER-TRACE | defer, reclassify, spill to DLQ | Aruna -> Vayu | queue-runbook |


======================================================================
DYNAMIC_LOADING_IMPLEMENTATION_BOUNDARY_v18
======================================================================
Intended_Insertion_Point: directly below plugin hot-swap / dynamic-loading governance sections.
| Boundary_Field | Canonical_Value | Source_Proof_Status | Release_Impact_if_Partial |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| loader_mechanism_source_proven_YN | Partial | governance proven, low-level mechanism not exhaustively source-final | medium |
| implemented_loader_mechanisms | manifest-driven .md skill loading, schema/registry reload, guarded plugin process replacement in maintenance mode | source-backed at governance/runtime shape level | low |
| not_proven_loader_mechanisms | arbitrary importlib/ctypes live injection into critical-path runtime | NOT_PROVEN_IN_SOURCE | high if claimed as implemented |
| allowed_hot_load_classes | .md skill manifests, config/registry bundles, non-critical plugin adapters in maintenance/quiesced mode | source-backed governance | low |
| blocked_hot_load_classes | active critical-path provider adapters, route-legal governor adapters, destructive kernel swaps | source-backed governance | high |
| rebind_preconditions | route quiesced or maintenance mode; compatibility revalidated; rollback snapshot present | source-backed governance | medium |
| rollback_contract | previous plugin/skill binding restored if validation fails | source-backed governance | low |
| route_quiescence_requirement | mandatory for critical-path route/plugin rebinding | source-backed governance | high |
| observability_during_swap | swap event, validation result, rollback event, affected route ids | source-backed governance | low |

======================================================================
HOT_SWAP_GOVERNANCE_VS_IMPLEMENTATION_MATRIX_v18
======================================================================
| Area | Governance_Closed_YN | Implementation_Closed_YN | Note |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Hot-swap eligibility rules | Yes | Yes (policy level) | canonical in v18 |
| Live-route protection | Yes | Yes | route quiescence mandatory |
| Low-level dynamic library loader | No | No | NOT_PROVEN_IN_SOURCE as finalized runtime truth |
| No-reboot plugin insertion for non-critical adapters | Yes | Conditional | only in guarded/quiesced mode |
| No-reboot critical-path adapter replacement | No | No | blocked |

======================================================================
LIVE_ROUTE_PLUGIN_CHANGE_PROTECTION_RULES_v18
======================================================================
- No plugin/skill rebinding on a critical live route without route quiescence or maintenance mode.
- Any failed compatibility revalidation triggers automatic rollback to the last approved binding.
- Release-legality is preserved only when hot-swap stays within allowed_hot_load_classes above.


======================================================================
ARUNA_ATOMIC_WORK_UNIT_REGISTER_v18

======================================================================
DEGRADED_MODE_BEHAVIOR_BY_DEVICE_CLASS_v18
======================================================================
- Windows 8 GB / standard CPU / 256 GB SSD: local-first governance, research, dashboard, attended publish; heavy media must offload or degrade.
- Windows 16 GB / stronger CPU / optional GPU: hybrid capable; premium media paths conditional on GPU/provider readiness.
- Windows 32 GB+ / stronger CPU/GPU: full local/hybrid previews and stronger render participation.
- Apple Silicon / M4: strong local inference/preview; premium provider routes still governed by provider/auth closure.

DNA_TEMPLATE_SPECIALIZATION_RULES_v18
======================================================================
- Parent template defines mandatory fields; child specialization may add fields but may not remove mandatory governance, observability, portability, or failure-surface blocks.
- Override precedence: canonical runtime register > child specialization > parent template > historical carry-forward wording.
- Any specialization that changes route legality, provider legality, release gating, or critical-path behavior requires validator_chain approval and founder/architect review.

- face consistency law applies across shot, scene, and rerender boundaries

- clone-grade realism paths require founder/manual review if provider/auth or quality blockers remain
- local degraded mode may preview using low-motion/static presenter; premium publish path still obeys provider/auth release gates

======================================================================
AVATAR_IDENTITY_CONSISTENCY_AND_TEMPORAL_COHERENCE_MATRIX_v18
======================================================================
| Check | Threshold | Failure_Action | Release_Blocking_YN |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| identity consistency | >=0.90 equivalent profile confidence | founder review + fallback presenter | Yes |
| realism score | >=0.85 | tribunal/manual review | Yes |
| temporal coherence | no visible jitter across adjacent shots | rerender or low-motion fallback | Conditional |
| pose/gesture continuity | approved within shot family | reduce motion / static presenter | Conditional |
| audio-video sync linkage | lip-sync and render clocks aligned | resync + rerender | Yes |

- review surfaces: avatar panel, diagnostics, founder queue, mission control provider trace.
- fallback surfaces: static presenter, reduced motion, provider reroute, attended review hold.

======================================================================
SYNC_FAILURE_DIAGNOSTIC_AND_RESYNC_MATRIX_v18
======================================================================
| Failure_Class | Diagnostic_Signal | Canonical_Response | Release_Blocking_YN |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| lip_drift | lip_sync_score < 0.75 | resync path + rerender | Yes |
| phoneme/viseme mismatch | wav2lip confidence < 0.92 | phoneme alignment pass then rerender | Yes |
| emotion incoherence | emotion coherence < 0.80 | prosody remap or lower-intensity expression | Conditional |
| timing jitter | frame skew beyond tolerance | checkpoint rollback to last sync-safe point | Conditional |
| provider sync artifact | provider callback/render anomaly | local resync or attended fallback | Conditional |

| urgent retention hook | faster viseme onset + subtle head emphasis | requires sync score to remain above floor |

| HR-014 | Best-practice operational register | below best-practice framework | BEST_PRACTICES_BEST_WORKFLOW_BEST_RESOLUTION_REGISTER_v18 | strengthened | No |  |  | operational patterns now explicit |




======================================================================
103. V19 APPEND-ONLY COST / COMPUTE / DEPLOYMENT CLOSURE LAW
======================================================================
This section is appended only. No prior v18 text is removed or replaced. All economics below obey the cost/deployment honesty law: no fake market pricing, no silent conversion of cost classes into false hard currency, and no deletion of open packets. Historical source figures are preserved only as historical planning anchors, not as live vendor-price claims.

Use the following confidence tags throughout appended economics material:
- SOURCE_PROVEN_EXACT
- INTERNAL_ESTIMATE
- COST_CLASS_DERIVED
- NEEDS_CONFIRMATION
- OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT


--- MODULE_COST_AUTHORITY_AND_ASSUMPTION_NOTE_v19 ---
| Label | Meaning | Examples | Allowed_Use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SOURCE_PROVEN_EXACT | Literal figure or hard constraint explicitly present in accessible source corpus. | Avatar Engine PRD: $14–38/month infra, $65–95/month all-in; Knowledge Base: ~$0.05 first render lean path, ~$51/month optimized production tier. | Historical planning anchor only; not a live market-price claim. |
| INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Internal planning value derived from source-backed cost classes, runtime topology, and compute assumptions. | NCU-based module cost, compute-minute estimate, human-review estimate. | Builder planning, approval thresholds, route comparison, Claude handoff. |
| COST_CLASS_DERIVED | Translated from existing LOW_LOCAL / HYBRID_STANDARD / CLOUD_PREMIUM style classes. | Route cost ladder, module tier assignment, queue/storage overhead class. | Relative comparison and default routing, not financial sign-off. |
| NEEDS_CONFIRMATION | A plausible internal value exists but source does not finalize it strongly enough. | Enterprise spend threshold literal number, exact per-provider callback overhead. | Blocked for unattended / enterprise release until confirmed. |
| OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT | Economics or release cost depends on still-open auth/callback/release packets. | Unattended YouTube callback failure cost, HeyGen verification replay cost. | Visible in ledgers only; not authoritative for release approval. |


--- CANONICAL_MACHINE_SAFE_COST_REGISTER_v19 ---
NCU = Normalized Cost Unit. NCU is an internal planning unit used to compare local, hybrid, cloud, and provider-bound routes without pretending to state current market prices. It is builder-grade and approval-grade, not a finance invoice.

| Rate_Card_Item | NCU_Basis | Use | Confidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| LC_CPU_MIN | 1.0 NCU per local CPU-minute | local orchestration, light transforms, metadata tasks | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| LC_GPU_MIN | 6.0 NCU per local GPU-minute | local lip-sync, render assist, visual QA | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| HC_CPU_MIN | 1.8 NCU per hybrid/cloud CPU worker-minute | hybrid synthesis, API wrappers, analytics jobs | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| HC_GPU_MIN | 10.0 NCU per hybrid/cloud GPU worker-minute | avatar render, sync correction, premium visual jobs | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| P_LIGHT_REQ | 3.0 NCU per light provider request | metadata, analytics, planning-grade provider calls | COST_CLASS_DERIVED |
| P_TTS_MIN | 8.0 NCU per premium TTS minute | premium voice generation | COST_CLASS_DERIVED |
| P_AVATAR_MIN | 20.0 NCU per avatar render minute | premium avatar rendering | COST_CLASS_DERIVED |
| HOT_STORAGE_GB_DAY | 0.2 NCU per hot GB-day | artifacts, intermediate renders, cache | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| EGRESS_GB | 1.0 NCU per GB egress | provider callbacks, media transfer, publish flows | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| HUMAN_REVIEW_MIN | 2.0 NCU per minute of manual review | founder/QA/manual escalation | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |

NCU tier interpretation for approval logic:
- ZERO_LOCAL: 0.0–2.0 NCU
- LOW_LOCAL: >2.0–6.0 NCU
- HYBRID_STANDARD: >6.0–20.0 NCU
- HYBRID_PREMIUM: >20.0–50.0 NCU
- CLOUD_PREMIUM: >50.0–120.0 NCU
- ENTERPRISE: >120.0 NCU or any route with organization-specific spend controls



--- CLAUDE_NATIVE_BUILD_STRATEGY_MAP_v19 ---
This matrix injects the verified Claude-native build split into the module model. Claude is the project brain, repo-aware builder, workflow/governance layer, and structured artifact generator. Heavy media execution remains outside Claude-native chat features.

| Module_or_Layer | Projects | GitHub_Integration | Skills | MCP_or_Connectors | Claude_Code_or_Agent_SDK | External_Execution | Recommended_Claude_Role | Claude_Cost_Posture |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PRD / DNA corpus | Primary | Optional | Support | Not required at start | Later acceleration | No | project memory + continuity brain | FREE_START / LOW |
| Registry / schema generation | Support | Primary | Primary | Optional | Primary for repo edits | No | repo-aware builder | LOW → PAID_ACCEL |
| Director / skill / route closure | Support | Primary | Primary | Optional | Primary | No | contract and register generator | LOW → PAID_ACCEL |
| Mission Control / ops runbooks | Primary | Support | Primary | Primary | Support | Observability backends only | ops intelligence layer | LOW / MCP_BETA |
| Dashboard / help / manuals | Primary | Support | Primary | Optional | Primary when code exists | Frontend host only | content + scaffold generator | FREE_START → LOW |
| Cost / compute / deployment packs | Primary | Support | Primary | Optional | Support | Cloud billing is source of truth | planning economics brain | FREE_START / LOW |
| .md skill army / repo-native skills | Support | Primary | Primary | Optional | Primary | No | skill author + validator | LOW → PAID_ACCEL |
| Plugin governance / tool bridges | Support | Support | Support | Primary | Primary for implementation | Tools/providers remain external | tool-connected controller | MCP_BETA / LOW_MED |
| Browser verification | No | No | Support | Optional | Support | Browser runtime | UI verifier | PAID_BETA |
| Heavy avatar / voice / render execution | Support only | No | Support only | Support for orchestration | Support for job prep | Primary | orchestrate, validate, QA | EXTERNAL_PROVIDER_VARIABLE |
| Publish / analytics APIs | Support | No | Support | Primary | Support | Primary | policy/orchestration + summary | LOW_MED + EXTERNAL_API |
| Self-healing / troubleshooting | Primary | Support | Primary | Primary | Primary for scripts | Actual infra actions remain external | RCA + remediation planner | LOW → PAID_ACCEL |
| Self-evolving / optimization loop | Primary | Primary | Primary | Support | Primary | Provider-backed experiments external | propose + scaffold, not auto-promote | LOW → PAID_ACCEL |

| RDC-001 | Research-only baseline | 3-8 NCU | 5-10 NCU | 6-12 NCU | optional long-context request | n/a | 1-2 NCU | 0-2 NCU | 0 NCU | 0 NCU | reduce source count / stay local | 1-3 NCU | 2-15m | CPU / hybrid text | medium | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| RDC-002 | Hybrid synthesis standard review | not recommended as sole lane | 8-18 NCU | 10-22 NCU | premium synthesis possible | 2-5 NCU | 1-3 NCU | 2-4 NCU | 2-4 NCU if manual review | 0 NCU | drop to research-only or fewer rounds | 2-6 NCU | 5-25m | CPU + provider | high for report quality | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| RDC-003 | Tribunal / debate review | 12-20 NCU if fully local | 15-30 NCU | 18-36 NCU | premium reasoning optional | 3-8 NCU | 2-4 NCU | 4-8 NCU | 3-6 NCU manual governance | 6-12 NCU | collapse to standard review | 4-10 NCU | 10-45m | CPU-heavy / reasoning | high for high-risk claims | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| RDC-004 | Founder escalation with contradiction packet | 5-9 NCU | 7-14 NCU | 9-16 NCU | optional premium reasoning | 2-5 NCU | 1-3 NCU | 3-6 NCU | 4-8 NCU | optional 6-12 NCU | defer decision or route to builder packet | 2-5 NCU | 5-20m | CPU / review | high governance | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |


--- TRIBUNAL_ESCALATION_COST_MATRIX_v19 / RESEARCH_ROUTE_COST_LADDER_v19 ---
| mode | likely_cost_band | what_you_get | when_to_use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| RESEARCH_ONLY | 3-12 NCU | fast fact pattern, low-cost synthesis | creator drafts, low-risk research |
| STANDARD_REVIEW | 8-22 NCU | synthesis + review + contradiction handling | default publishable intelligence |
| TRIBUNAL_DEBATE | 15-36 NCU | multi-round challenge, contradiction resolution, governance grade confidence | high-risk claims, founder review, premium analysis |

--- MODULE_TO_DEPLOYMENT_TOPOLOGY_MATRIX_v19 ---
| module_name | local_allowed_YN | hybrid_allowed_YN | cloud_allowed_YN | hosted_AI_allowed_YN | GitHub_repo_linked_YN | browser_safe_YN | requires_GPU_YN | requires_secret_manager_YN | requires_callback_or_poller_YN | requires_durable_queue_YN | requires_durable_storage_YN | local_only_constraints | hybrid_preferred_reason | cloud_required_reason | unsafe_local_conditions | deployment_notes | claude_role |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Kernel / legality spine | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes (support) | Yes | n/a | No | No | No | Recommended | Recommended | none | best for mixed workloads | none | none major | Keep close to route registry and dossier engine. | Projects/Skills/Claude Code primary |
| Veins / dossier state | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | No runtime | No | No | No | No | Recommended | Yes | weaker durability | best balance of durability and locality | team/always-on ops | weak disks or untrusted devices | Patch-safe storage must be authoritative. | runtime external; Claude reads/writes via contracts |
| Director runtime | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes for closure artifacts | n/a | No | No | No | Recommended | Yes | fine for review lanes | allows premium research/debate escalation | team governance surfaces | none major | Do not colocate heavy media here. | Projects/Skills/GitHub primary |
| Skill execution mesh | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | Yes | No | Some skills only | Provider-bound skills yes | Provider-bound skills yes | Recommended | Recommended | heavy skills degrade quickly | best default | heavy or high-concurrency lanes | weak hardware for heavy media | Place by skill family, not one-size-fits-all. | GitHub/Claude Code/Skills primary |
| Mission Control | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes for configs | Yes | No | No | No direct, but traces callback systems | No direct | Yes for telemetry | limited ops visibility if air-gapped | best for shared ops | org-wide observability | weak storage / limited retention | Should be a primary ops surface, not just supplement. | Projects/MCP/Skills primary |
| Chitragupta health & recovery | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | Support only | No | No | No | Yes for provider-aware recovery | Yes | Yes | reduced fleet visibility | best for real ops | distributed runtime / enterprise | no durable telemetry store | Bind to RCA/replay/log stores. | Projects/Skills/MCP support |
| Self-healing runtime | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | Support only | No | No | Sometimes for provider failover | Often | Yes | Yes | limited remediation scope | best recovery posture | distributed fleets | no safe restart / replay ability | Keep restart and replay boundaries explicit. | Projects/Skills/MCP/Claude Code |
| Self-evolving runtime | Yes (proposals) | Yes | Yes | Partial | Yes | No | No direct except media experiments | No direct | No direct | Recommended | Yes | do not self-promote on local only | proposal + validation best split | large benchmark/test fleets | unreviewed production mutations | Repo automation and validator chain must gate promotions. | Projects/GitHub/Claude Code primary |
| Plugin host | Yes | Yes | Partial | Partial | Yes | plugin-specific | Plugin-specific | Sometimes | Sometimes | Sometimes | Low | browser/account risk local | safer isolation | headless automation farms | untrusted local + credentialed plugins | Live-route protections required for hot-swap. | MCP/Claude Code primary |
| Browser automation | Yes | Yes | Partial | No direct | No runtime | n/a | No | Yes if credentialed | No direct | Recommended | Logs only | session/account risk | safer isolation and replay | farm scale | untrusted local or weak sandboxing | Prefer isolated worker surface. | MCP/Claude Code support |
| Avatar synthesis | Preview only | Yes | Yes | No direct | No runtime | No | Yes or external provider | Yes if provider-backed | Yes | Yes | Yes | weak local realism and throughput | best default creator deployment | premium / concurrent / enterprise lanes | 8 GB class and no GPU | Provider callback closures remain release-sensitive. | Claude orchestrates, external runtime executes |
| Lip-sync / emotion-sync | Yes on stronger machines | Yes | Yes | No direct | No runtime | No | Usually yes for speed | No direct | No direct | Recommended | Yes temp artifacts | slow on weak devices | best for standard creators | premium/high-concurrency correction | 8 GB class heavy correction loops | Tie to media thresholds and rerender logic. | Claude defines QA; runtime external/local mix |
| Editing / render / export | Yes on mid/high systems | Yes | Yes | No direct | No runtime | No | Often beneficial | No | No direct | Recommended | Yes | slow or blocked on weak hardware | best time/cost compromise | batch and enterprise scale | weak disk, RAM, or thermal limits | Heavy route offload tables should govern this. | Claude plans/QA only |
| Publish / analytics surfaces | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Support only | API/UI yes | No | Yes | Yes for unattended flows | Recommended | Yes for logs/history | attended flows only if callback not finalized | best auth + control split | always-on unattended publish | untrusted local or unresolved callback auth | Release legality depends on auth closure. | MCP/connectors + external APIs |
| Claude-native build intelligence layer | Yes | Yes | n/a | Yes | Yes | Yes for web surfaces | No | Only when connected tools need it | No direct | No | Project/repo artifacts only | usage limits / plan boundaries | repo + tool bridges | n/a | none major | Use Claude for thinking/build/governance, not heavy media runtime. | Projects/GitHub/Skills/MCP/Claude Code/Agent SDK |


--- LOCAL_HYBRID_CLOUD_BOUNDARY_RULES_v19 ---
Boundary rule summary:
1. Governance, registry, skill loading, documentation, and route legality may start local, but durable queue/storage becomes mandatory as soon as unattended or team workflows appear.
2. Heavy avatar, lip-sync correction, render/export, and large debate swarms move hybrid or cloud when local device classes cross the offload trigger thresholds defined below.
3. Any provider-bound unattended publish path requires callback/poller closure plus secrets placement outside untrusted_local surfaces.
4. Claude-native tooling owns thinking, structuring, repo-intelligence, and workflow/governance. External providers/workers own premium media generation, publish APIs, and high-throughput async jobs.

--- SECRETS_AND_STATE_PLACEMENT_MATRIX_v19 ---
- Secrets manager required: publish/auth providers, premium media providers, browser automation with credentialed sessions, enterprise deployment surfaces.
- Durable state required: dossier shards, route traces, callback correlation, replay packets, Mission Control telemetry, audit logs.
- Unsafe on untrusted_local: premium credentials, unattended publish callbacks, enterprise spend authority, durable mutation state.

--- QUEUE_STORAGE_AND_CALLBACK_PLACEMENT_MATRIX_v19 ---
- Queue mandatory for: hybrid/cloud media, callback/poller provider flows, replay-safe publish flows, self-healing replay, render/export batches.
- Callback/poller service mandatory for: HeyGen-style async render, YouTube unattended publish/auth closure, any provider with delayed completion semantics.
- Long-lived storage mandatory for: rendered assets, sync intermediates, analytics history, RCA and audit packets.

| CMP-009 | Editing / retention pass | 4 vCPU | 8 vCPU | 8 GB | 16 GB | 2 GB optional | 4-8 GB helpful | 10-40 GB | low-medium | 2-10m | 1-2 heavy edits | long videos / complex effects / many variants | lower export tier / simpler edit pattern | slower export and simpler effects | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | estimated |
| CMP-010 | Render / export | 4 vCPU | 8-12 vCPU | 8 GB | 16-32 GB | optional 2 GB | 4-8 GB helpful | 20-100 GB | low | 2-20m | 1 heavy export | 4K/long-duration/batch export | lower resolution/bitrate | render time or output tier degradation | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | estimated |
| CMP-011 | Mission Control / Chitragupta | 2 vCPU | 4 vCPU | 4 GB | 8 GB | n/a | n/a | 5-20 GB logs + cache | medium | always-on | many readers, few writers | shared team ops or long retention | shorter retention / fewer traces | lower observability horizon | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | estimated |
| CMP-012 | Self-healing analytics / replay | 4 vCPU | 6-8 vCPU | 8 GB | 12-16 GB | n/a | n/a | 5-20 GB | medium | event-driven | 1-3 incident loops | fleet-wide incidents or replay storms | manual review instead of auto-remediation | slower recovery | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | estimated |
| CMP-013 | Self-evolving mutation / validation | 4 vCPU | 8 vCPU | 8 GB | 16 GB | n/a unless media benchmarks | 4-8 GB if media comparison involved | 5-30 GB | medium | minutes to hours batch | 1-2 | benchmark swarms or media validations | proposal only, no heavy validation | less confidence in mutation gains | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | estimated |


--- HEAVY_ROUTE_COMPUTE_PROFILE_MATRIX_v19 ---
- Avatar premium route: GPU-heavy, 32 GB+ preferred or provider offload.
- Lip-sync / emotion-sync correction route: mid/high device or hybrid GPU.
- Tribunal + research route: CPU/RAM-heavy, local on 16 GB+ only for moderate scale.
- Render/export route: storage and CPU/GPU mixed; offload recommended for batch.

--- LOCAL_OFFLOAD_TRIGGER_THRESHOLDS_v19 ---
Offload when any of the following become true:
- expected runtime exceeds creator patience window for the active mode,
- RAM pressure exceeds 70% on 8 GB class or 80% on 16 GB class,
- GPU job count exceeds safe_local_concurrency,
- callback-bound provider flow requires unattended handling,
- queue saturation predicts missed deadline profile.

109. LOCAL EXECUTION COST MODEL
======================================================================
Intended parent insertion point in v18: runtime modes, hardware, and cost sections.

--- LOCAL_EXECUTION_COST_MODEL_v19 / LOCAL_MACHINE_AMORTIZATION_AND_POWER_MODEL_v19 ---
| cost_component | cost_basis | estimate_model | notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Electricity / power | active CPU/GPU minutes | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Not free; scales with render and sync workloads. |
| Workstation amortization | device lifespan / active production use | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | More important for dedicated creator rigs and Apple Silicon premium devices. |
| SSD / cache wear | scratch writes + render temp files | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Media-heavy paths materially increase wear. |
| Local GPU depreciation | GPU-bound avatar/sync/edit workloads | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Use when comparing local GPU vs provider offload. |
| Local queue occupancy | machine blocked by long jobs | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Opportunity cost if creator cannot use device interactively. |
| Operator intervention | manual QA / retries / restarts | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Often the most ignored local cost in creator flows. |
| Local storage growth | artifacts, caches, checkpoints | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Must be visible on 256 GB SSD class machines. |
| Maintenance overhead | updates, drivers, cleanup, reindex | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | Higher on mixed plugin/media stacks. |


Interpretation: local may be cheaper than premium provider execution, but it is not free. Weak-device creator economics must include power, amortization, queue occupancy, storage growth, and operator time.

110. CLOUD / HYBRID RUNTIME COST STACK
======================================================================
Intended parent insertion point in v18: deployment and production posture, provider registry, worker router, and portability sections.

--- CLOUD_RUNTIME_COST_STACK_v19 / HYBRID_RUNTIME_COST_STACK_v19 / QUEUE_STORAGE_EGRESS_COST_MODEL_v19 ---
| runtime_type | cost_elements | why_it_matters | confidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Cloud worker compute | worker instance / serverless / GPU time | core non-provider infra spend | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| Object and artifact storage | intermediate outputs, final outputs, checkpoints | media-heavy long-tail cost | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| Queueing / orchestration | message ops, step transitions, DLQ retention | hidden scaling cost | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| Egress / transfers | provider uploads/downloads, publish transfers | media routes amplify transfer cost | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| Logs / telemetry | ingest, retention, query, RCA traces | Mission Control and Chitragupta are not free | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| Callback handling | webhook endpoint, pollers, retries | open auth packets can amplify cost | OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT |
| Checkpoint / backup / archive | replay-safe snapshots, long retention | reliability and auditability overhead | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |
| Idle capacity / autoscale overhead | warm pools, scale-up lag, buffer headroom | needed for predictable QoS | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE |


Hybrid interpretation rule: hybrid cost = local orchestration cost + queue/storage/callback service cost + selective provider or cloud worker cost. Hybrid is usually the best default lane for Shadow Empire because it preserves creator-device usability while containing premium spend.

Key law: unresolved callback/auth packets are not only governance blockers; they are spend-amplifiers. Every open callback packet creates uncertainty in retry, replay, and manual-review cost.
