======================================================================
APPENDIX — CLAUDE-NATIVE BUILD STRATEGY
======================================================================
This appendix defines the recommended Claude-native integration posture for Shadow Empire as a low-cost project brain, repo-aware build layer, and workflow/governance layer, while preserving external execution responsibility for heavy media and provider-bound runtime paths.

Current official Anthropic product documentation supports the core split used here:
- Projects are available to all users, including free accounts, and free users can create up to five projects.
- GitHub integration gives Claude direct repository context for software-development tasks.
- Skills extend Claude with specialized knowledge/workflows and require code execution to be enabled where applicable.
- MCP/connectors are the bridge to external tools, data, APIs, logs, dashboards, and remote MCP servers.
- Claude Code is the stronger agentic coding layer for repo-aware implementation and automation.
- Heavy media execution still belongs outside Claude-native chat surfaces when it depends on provider-bound generation, rendering, or asynchronous runtime jobs.

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-01 — Claude-Native Build Strategy Overview
--------------------------------------------------

| Field | Content |
| --- | --- |
| Purpose | Define how Shadow Empire should use Claude-native capabilities without pretending Claude is the full execution runtime. |
| Core Principle | Claude should be the project brain, repo-aware build assistant, and workflow/governance layer; heavy media generation and provider-bound execution should remain selectively offloaded. |
| Why This Exists | To reduce orchestration cost, improve repo-context continuity, and create a clean Claude handoff model for PRD, DNA, registries, validators, routes, control-plane logic, and release discipline. |
| Builder Rule | Use Claude-native features first for planning, schema/registry generation, audits, review flows, docs, deck creation, and repo operations. Use external providers for avatar/video/voice/render/publish heavy execution. |
| Cost Position | Low-cost to bootstrap for design/governance/repo-intelligence layers; not a zero-cost replacement for all media/provider/runtime costs. |
| Accuracy Position | Claude-native improves continuity, consistency, and build velocity, but does not guarantee 100% accuracy; correctness still depends on sources, registries, validation, connectors, human review, and provider behavior. |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-02 — Claude Capability Suitability Matrix
--------------------------------------------------

| Capability | Best Use in Shadow Empire | Use as Primary? | Notes |
| --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| Projects | PRD, DNA, architecture law, release blockers, decision history, deck iterations | Yes | Best persistent workspace for master context and project-specific guidance. |
| GitHub Integration | Repo reading, registry/schema review, codebase context, diff-aware reasoning | Yes | Best for understanding actual repo truth rather than prompt-only summaries. |
| Skills | Repeatable workflows like PRD audits, route checks, blocker checks, deck review, registry review | Yes | Best for reusable specialized workflows once code execution/tooling is enabled where needed. |
| MCP / Connectors | Tools, logs, docs, dashboards, analytics, internal systems, publish surfaces | Yes | Best bridge to external systems and live operational context. |
| Claude Code | Multi-file repo edits, validators, loaders, tests, automation, scaffolding | Yes, later phase | Best implementation accelerator after the repo structure is stable enough for code generation and mutation. |
| External Providers | Avatar, voice, render, publish, analytics APIs | Yes | Keep heavy/provider-bound execution outside Claude-native chat/runtime surfaces. |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-03 — Module-to-Claude Tool Mapping Register
--------------------------------------------------

| Module / Layer | Projects | GitHub | Skills | MCP / Connectors | Claude Code | External Providers | Primary Build Recommendation |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| PRD Master | Yes | Optional | Yes | Optional | Later | No | Maintain master PRD in Projects; use Skills for audit and consistency checks. |
| DNA Corpus | Yes | Optional | Yes | Optional | Later | No | Keep DNA libraries and examples in Projects; generate variants and reviews via Skills. |
| Architecture Laws | Yes | Optional | Yes | Optional | Later | No | Preserve as canonical workspace material; do not fragment across provider runtimes. |
| Registry Specs | Support | Yes | Yes | Optional | Yes | No | Generate and patch YAML/JSON/MD registries via Claude Code with repo context. |
| Route Catalog | Support | Yes | Yes | Optional | Yes | No | Use GitHub + Claude Code for final route rows, owner/veto tables, and validation hooks. |
| Director Runtime Register | Support | Yes | Yes | Optional | Yes | No | Claude Code for row closure, Skills for consistency review, Projects for canonical context. |
| Skill Runtime Register | Support | Yes | Yes | Optional | Yes | No | Best fit for repo-native generation, validator chaining, and `.md` skill portability. |
| .md Skill Army | Support | Yes | Yes | Optional | Yes | No | Repo-first generation with Skills + Claude Code + GitHub context. |
| Mission Control | Support | Optional | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | Use Claude-native for ops logic and summarization; MCP for logs, dashboards, and evidence. |
| Chitragupta / RCA | Support | Optional | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | Claude interprets incidents and builds RCA; tooling remains source of truth. |
| Dashboard / Help / Manual | Yes | Optional | Yes | Optional | Yes | No | Strong Claude-native authoring surface for help layers, manual content, and role guidance. |
| Creator Modes | Yes | Optional | Yes | Optional | Yes | No | Best maintained as structured documentation + config + UI copy. |
| Self-Healing Logic | Support | Optional | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | Claude proposes logic and runbooks; infra/runtime still executes repairs. |
| Self-Evolving Logic | Support | Optional | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | Claude proposes route/registry improvements; governance controls promotion. |
| Research / Debate / Tribunal | Yes | Optional | Yes | Optional | Yes | Optional | Strong Claude-native reasoning workflow with optional external evidence/tool connectors. |
| Plugin Governance | Optional | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | MCP/connectors plus repo config is the correct fit for plugin/tool governance. |
| Browser Testing / UI Verification | Optional | Optional | Optional | Yes | Yes | No | Best later with browser-connected tooling and Claude Code-driven test loops. |
| Repo Automation / PR Workflows / CI Helpers | Optional | Yes | Optional | Optional | Yes | No | Claude Code + GitHub Actions are the clean fit for repo automation. |
| Media Factory Orchestration | Support | Optional | Yes | Optional | Yes | Yes | Claude coordinates, validates, and reviews; providers/workers execute heavy jobs. |
| Avatar Generation | Optional | No | Optional | Optional | Optional | Yes | Keep provider/GPU-heavy execution external; Claude defines contracts and QA criteria. |
| Lip-Sync / Emotion-Sync | Optional | No | Optional | Optional | Optional | Yes | Claude defines thresholds and QA; rendering/sync stays external. |
| Editing / Retention System | Support | Optional | Yes | Optional | Yes | Yes | Claude plans and validates; editing/render stack executes. |
| Publish / Analytics | Optional | Optional | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Connect API/tool layer with MCP; keep actual publish/auth execution external. |
| Agent Estate / Crew Logic | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Partial | Claude-native is ideal for defining agent classes, permissions, and workflow roles; execution still splits by class. |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-04 — Module Execution Boundary Rules
--------------------------------------------------

| Module Class | Claude-Native Role | Non-Claude Role | Boundary Rule |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Planning / Governance | Authoritative | None required at first | Keep in Projects, Skills, and Claude Code workflow. |
| Repo / Registry / Schema | Primary builder | GitHub remains source of truth | Use GitHub + Claude Code as mainline implementation surface. |
| Tool / Data Access | Orchestration and interpretation | External system remains source of truth | Use MCP/connectors for access; do not treat Claude as the system of record. |
| Media Generation | Planning, QA, orchestration | Heavy execution | Keep premium media/render/provider work external. |
| Publishing / Analytics | Policy, summaries, orchestration | Actual API operations | Use connectors/providers for live operations and refresh flows. |
| Ops / RCA | Interpretation, runbooks, summaries | Logs/metrics/infra control systems | Use MCP/tool layer for evidence and action surfaces. |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-05 — Claude-Native Adoption Phases
--------------------------------------------------

| Phase | Goal | Claude-Native Tools | What Gets Built | What Stays External |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Phase 1 — Low-Cost Brain | Build architecture continuity and handoff quality | Projects, GitHub, Skills, file creation | PRD, DNA, registries, blocker ledgers, decks, checklists, route maps, cost/deployment packs | Heavy media, render, publish APIs |
| Phase 2 — Repo Build Acceleration | Turn docs into repo artifacts | GitHub, Claude Code, Skills, MCP | Validators, loaders, schemas, test harnesses, CI helpers, module closure patches | Heavy generation providers |
| Phase 3 — Tool-Connected Operations | Add live ops and automation | MCP/connectors, Claude Code, GitHub Actions | Mission Control helpers, RCA workflows, release checks, browser/test workflows | Full external execution runtimes |
| Phase 4 — Selective Production Automation | Use Claude as governed build/ops assistant | Claude Code, MCP, Skills | PR automation, issue-to-code, runbook support, review gates | Avatar/video/voice/render heavy paths |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-06 — Cost Posture by Claude Phase
--------------------------------------------------

| Phase | Cost Character | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Phase 1 | Low / near-zero-cost to start | Best phase for free/cheap PRD, DNA, registry, and workflow design work. |
| Phase 2 | Moderate | Claude Code and heavier automation move you toward paid usage. |
| Phase 3 | Moderate to variable | Connector/tool usage and live automation add operational complexity and usage overhead. |
| Phase 4 | Variable | External providers dominate media/publish/runtime cost, not Claude alone. |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-07 — Recommended Claude Skills for Shadow Empire
--------------------------------------------------

| Skill Name | Purpose | Inputs | Outputs | Best Tool Stack |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PRD Gap Auditor | Find missing, weak, or packetized PRD sections | PRD + source corpus | gap report | Projects + Skills |
| Registry Closure Checker | Verify route/skill/director rows are machine-final | registry files | closure ledger | GitHub + Skills + Claude Code |
| Release Blocker Checker | Audit unattended/enterprise blockers | PRD + provider sections | blocker matrix | Projects + Skills |
| Deck Critic | Review pitch deck against technical brief | deck + prompt + PRD | defect ledger | Projects + Skills |
| Cost Model Builder | Generate module economics and compute tables | PRD + deployment assumptions | cost matrices | Projects + Claude Code |
| Mission Control Summarizer | Summarize incidents and RCA flows | logs + ops docs | RCA summary | MCP + Skills |
| Dashboard Manual Builder | Generate help/manual content | dashboard specs | help content | Projects + Skills |
| Claude Handoff Packager | Turn PRD into repo-ready appendices and checklists | PRD + repo context | handoff pack | Projects + GitHub + Claude Code |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-08 — Recommended Claude Subagent / Crew Roles
--------------------------------------------------

| Role | Function | Best Claude Surface | Why |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PRD Architect | Maintains PRD structure and truth | Projects | Strong context retention and project-scoped guidance. |
| Repo Builder | Writes schemas, validators, loaders | Claude Code + GitHub | Best for implementation and multi-file repo mutation. |
| Governance Auditor | Checks route, cost, and release legality | Skills + Projects | Strong repeatable review fit. |
| RCA Analyst | Builds incident summaries and RCA notes | MCP + Skills | Best for ops reasoning with external evidence. |
| Deck Engineer | Builds and critiques presentations | Projects + Skills | Fast artifact generation and revision. |
| Cost Analyst | Builds economics, throughput, and deployment models | Claude Code + Projects | Strong structured artifact fit. |
| DNA Librarian | Maintains DNA examples and inheritance patterns | Projects + Skills | Best for repeatable library upkeep and evolution discipline. |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-09 — Claude vs External Execution Decision Rules
--------------------------------------------------

| Decision Question | If Yes | If No |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Is this mainly documentation, planning, review, governance, or repo-structure work? | Use Claude-native first | Continue to next rule |
| Does this need actual multi-file repo edits or command execution? | Use Claude Code | Continue to next rule |
| Does this need external tool/data/app access? | Use MCP/connectors | Continue to next rule |
| Is this a heavy media/render/provider-bound execution path? | Keep external | Continue to next rule |
| Is this a repeatable specialized workflow? | Make it a Skill | Continue to next rule |
| Is this primarily persistent project context or long-running design continuity? | Keep it in Projects | Use repo/tool/runtime surfaces instead |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-10 — Claude Handoff Readiness Rules
--------------------------------------------------

| Rule | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| Projects-first rule | All master PRD/DNA/architecture context must live in a Project workspace. |
| Repo-truth rule | GitHub-connected repo files outrank memory or prose summaries. |
| Skills-for-repeatability rule | Any recurring audit or review flow should become a Skill. |
| MCP-for-tools rule | Any external dashboard/log/app/data dependency should be bridged through connectors/MCP, not copied into prompts. |
| Claude-Code-for-build rule | Real file generation and repo mutation should move to Claude Code once structure stabilizes. |
| External-provider rule | Avatar/voice/render/publish heavy execution stays outside Claude-native chat/runtime. |
| No-fake-free rule | Claude can reduce orchestration cost, but full runtime is not free. |
| No-100%-accuracy rule | Human validation and registry/validator discipline remain mandatory. |

--------------------------------------------------
APPENDIX-CNB-11 — Builder Note: Claude-Native Integration Posture
--------------------------------------------------

| Field | Content |
| --- | --- |
| Recommended placement in PRD | After deployment, portability, and cost sections. |
| Why append this appendix | It gives Claude handoff clarity and makes the build strategy explicit rather than implicit. |
| Main reader | Claude build agent, AI engineer, founder, systems reviewer. |
| Main value | Tells exactly which parts should use Projects, GitHub, Skills, MCP, Claude Code, and external providers. |
| Main caution | Do not present Claude-native tooling as a replacement for heavy provider/runtime execution. |

======================================================================
APPENDIX-CNB PATCH CLOSURE NOTE
======================================================================
This appendix pack keeps the v19 PRD body unchanged and adds an explicit Claude-native operating split:
- Claude-native for project memory, repo-aware build work, reusable workflows, governance review, docs, and repo automation later,
- external providers and non-Claude runtimes for realistic avatar generation, premium voice, render-heavy execution, publish APIs, analytics APIs, and callback-heavy provider-bound flows.

Honesty note:
- low-cost bootstrap does not mean zero-cost runtime,
- repo-aware continuity does not mean 100% correctness,
- official product availability and plan limits may evolve, so production rollout should still verify the current Anthropic account/plan surface before depending on a specific feature in a release path.


======================================================================
115. V19 PATCH CLOSURE NOTE
======================================================================
This v19 append-only economics closure does not claim live vendor pricing. It converts v18 from architecture-strong/economics-thin into architecture-strong/economics-useful by adding builder-grade BOMs, compute sizing, deployment placement, throughput, and Claude-native/external split truth.

Still-open items remain explicit:
- provider callback exact values and verification payloads remain release-sensitive where source is not final,
- enterprise spend threshold literals remain confirmation-dependent,
- exact market pricing remains outside this artifact unless source-proven or attached later through a billing adapter.

This v21 pass is append-only. Detailed_PRD_MASTERPIECE_v20.txt is preserved exactly as baseline text. The sections below are machine-closure overlays created to reduce Claude/builder re-derivation while preserving all unresolved source limits honestly.

======================================================================
| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| execution-critical director rows | must not retain DecisionPacket truth in canonical runtime fields |

| unresolved director row | remains release-gated and artifact-linked rather than silently guessed |

======================================================================
VAYU_SWITCH_THRESHOLD_MATRIX_v21
======================================================================
| trigger_type | Vayu interpretation |
| --- | --- |
| budget_trigger | block or downgrade when budget token absent or cost threshold exceeded |
| deadline_trigger | upgrade/offload when local path cannot satisfy deadline safely |
| quality_floor_trigger | offload or hold when local path violates quality floor |
| health_trigger | reroute or hold when worker/provider/device health is red |
| queue_trigger | defer, downgrade, or offload when queue saturation exceeds safe ceiling |
| callback/provider trigger | attended mode or poller fallback when provider callback path is unresolved |

======================================================================
LOCAL_HYBRID_CLOUD_HYSTERESIS_RULES_v21
======================================================================
- Once upgraded due to quality/deadline/health, a path remains on the upgraded tier until one full stability window completes.
- Downgrades must not thrash between local and hybrid; use route-scoped hysteresis windows.
- Premium provider-bound media paths remain route-gated if auth/callback closure is not canonical.


======================================================================
121. SYNC-ON-INGEST / LIVE REGISTRY MUTATION SUBSYSTEM
======================================================================
======================================================================
SYNC_ON_INGEST_RUNTIME_SUBSYSTEM_v21
======================================================================
Canonical builder interpretation of the source-defined live ingest lifecycle. This is the first-class subsystem that turns skill/crew/provider/policy additions into validated runtime updates without reboot.

| phase_id | phase_name | primary_owner | required_action | output | rollback_rule | permission_rule | ui_hook |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ING-01 | validate | registry validator chain | schema, namespace, portability, route legality, and dependency validation | validation_report | stop on any critical error | builder/founder only | dashboard ingest wizard |
| ING-02 | register | registry service | write canonical registry row + version stamp | registry_entry | revert provisional row on failed register | founder/builder scoped | registry diff screen |
| ING-03 | reindex | Ganesha indexer | rebuild routing/search/index references | reindex_report | revert to previous index snapshot | founder/builder scoped | ingest progress panel |
| ING-04 | hot reload | runtime loader | reload manifests/skills/policies into live runtime | hot_reload_report | revert runtime binding on failed reload | founder/builder scoped | live runtime status badge |
| ING-05 | invalidate route cache | route cache manager | clear affected route plans and dependency caches | cache_invalidation_report | restore last-good cache on failure | automatic once approved ingest starts | route cache panel |
| ING-06 | emit audit | Chitragupta | append ingest event, warnings, and final state | ingest_audit_event | none; append-only | automatic | audit / Mission Control |

======================================================================
| object_name | field | meaning | status_rule |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ingest_result.schema.json | ingest_id | unique ingest event id | mandatory |
| ingest_result.schema.json | artifact_type | skill / crew / provider / policy | mandatory |
| ingest_result.schema.json | artifact_id | canonical runtime id | mandatory |
| ingest_result.schema.json | status | VALIDATED / REGISTERED / REINDEXED / HOT_RELOADED / ROLLED_BACK / FAILED | mandatory |

| ingest_result.schema.json | rollback_reason | exact rollback reason when status is ROLLED_BACK or FAILED | conditional |

======================================================================
LIVE_REGISTRY_MUTATION_AND_REINDEX_FLOW_v21
======================================================================
Rule: any live mutation that changes route legality, skill binding, provider availability, or policy gates must trigger reindex + route cache invalidation before the runtime may treat the new artifact as authoritative.

======================================================================
ROUTE_CACHE_INVALIDATION_RULES_v21
======================================================================
| trigger | invalidation_scope | safety_rule | audit_required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| skill manifest added/changed | all affected routes using family + dependency graph | do not preserve stale priority or legality decisions | yes |
| provider policy change | routes bound to provider + auth path | clear callback/auth-derived caches | yes |
| policy register change | all affected governance routes | old legality verdicts become historical only | yes |
| crew composition template change | workspace/runtime composition caches | require compose revalidation | yes |
| route owner/veto change | affected release-capable routes | invalidate release legality cache immediately | yes |

123. ROLE HIERARCHY NORMALIZATION
======================================================================
======================================================================
AUTH_ADMIN_HIERARCHY_MATRIX_v21
======================================================================
| role | authority_scope | approval_rights | route_override_rights | dashboard_surfaces | cost_authority | release_authority | mutation_authority | review_authority | escalation_path | blocked_actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Founder | all constitutional surfaces | all | all | all | all | all | all | all | all escalations | none except explicit legal/policy gates |
| Krishna | governance + route legality + queue control | approve/deny/review | route override under policy | view core ops + control plane | standard + founder-scoped premium preview | release recommendation only unless founder delegates | builder-safe mutations only when delegated | full strategic review | founder | destructive founder-only acts |
| Owner | channel/pipeline/schedule management | content and schedule approvals | no constitutional override | owned channels/pipelines/analytics | owned budget requests only | no enterprise promotion | channel/pipeline edits | owned content review | Krishna/Founder | founder-only or Yama-only gates |
| Editor | content control and persona assignment | content approval within owned scope | no route override | editorial surfaces only | no premium authority | none | content/persona edits only | editorial review | Owner/Krishna | governance, budget, release |
| Analyst | analytics, diagnostics read, report packaging | no execution approval by default | no override | analytics + diagnostics read | no spend authority | none | no runtime mutation | analysis review only | Owner/Krishna | content mutation, route mutation, release |
| Creator | safe production surfaces | submit/review own work | no override | creator workspace, channels, pipelines | budget requests only | none | owned drafts and runs within safe lanes | own-content review | Owner/Founder | founder/builder surfaces |
| Builder | registry, schema, validator, test, ops tooling | build-time approvals under founder policy | no policy override | builder + ops + registry surfaces | can request premium for build tests | no enterprise promotion without founder | registry/schema/test mutations | technical review | Founder/Krishna | business-only actions |
| Reviewer | review queues and QA surfaces | approve/reject within assigned lane | no override | review + QA + diagnostics | no spend authority | none | review annotations only | review lane | Owner/Founder | registry/runtime mutation |
| Operator | runbooks, Mission Control, incident actions | safe replay/hold under policy | no override | Mission Control + diagnostics | no spend authority | none | safe ops actions only | ops review | Founder/Krishna | founder-only destructive ops |

======================================================================
ROLE_TO_PERMISSION_TO_SURFACE_BINDING_v21
======================================================================
| role | primary_surfaces | blocked_surfaces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Founder | all command, founder mode, Mission Control, registry, release, cost | none except constitutional self-checks |
| Krishna | governance/control-plane/ops/review | founder-only destructive or enterprise-final actions unless delegated |
| Owner | channels, pipelines, schedules, analytics | founder-only governance, registry mutation |
| Editor | content, persona, schedule | founder/builder-only registry and release surfaces |
| Analyst | analytics, diagnostics read | route override, publish mutation, release actions |
| Creator | creator workspace, channels, pipelines | founder mode, registry, destructive ops |
| Builder | registry, tests, Mission Control, diagnostics, repo surfaces | business-only or founder-only approval surfaces |
| Reviewer | review queues, QA panels | route overrides, registry mutation |
| Operator | Mission Control, diagnostics, replay-safe ops | founder-only destructive controls |

======================================================================
FOUNDER_KRISHNA_OWNER_EDITOR_ANALYST_NORMALIZATION_v21
======================================================================
Normalization rule: Founder remains supreme authority. Krishna is the highest non-founder operational/governance administrator. Owner/Editor/Analyst are product/runtime-facing operational roles. Creator/Builder/Reviewer/Operator are execution-facing working roles. No role below Founder silently inherits founder-only destructive or release-promoting powers.

124. SOURCE-SPECIFIC UNDERINJECTED MODULE RESTORATION
======================================================================
======================================================================
SOURCE_NAMED_ENGINE_RESTORATION_PACK_v21
======================================================================
| named_item | source_basis | current_prd_relation | canonical_role_type | canonical_role | exact_builder_implication |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Skill Debate Engine | FILE_1 consolidated master self-evolving-engine/skill-debate | self-evolving layer present but generalized | first-class named engine | multi-skill debate before approve/modify/escalate/reject | Used on contradiction, strategy conflict, and high-impact improvement proposals |
| Skill Memory Engine | FILE_1 consolidated master self-evolving-engine/skill-memory | partly flattened into self-evolving/memory concepts | first-class named engine | stores successful hooks, editing patterns, structures, audience behavior | Requires explicit memory-bank/runtime relation for builders |
| Skill Mutation Engine | FILE_1 consolidated master self-evolving-engine/skill-mutation | present as mutation logic, not explicit engine family | first-class named engine | proposes new strategies and prompt/route/skill mutations | Must remain governance-gated |
| Skill Governance Engine | FILE_1 consolidated master self-evolving-engine/skill-governance | generic governance layer present | first-class named engine | controls high-impact self-evolving decisions through hierarchy tree | Restores source naming and builder clarity |

======================================================================
SOURCE_PROVEN_VS_INTERNAL_ESTIMATE_TAGGING_RULES_v21
======================================================================
- SOURCE_PROVEN_EXACT = directly asserted in source/resource with stable value.
- INTERNAL_ESTIMATE = builder planning number derived from source structure, not a live vendor claim.
- COST_CLASS_DERIVED = translated from LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH or similar class posture.
- NEEDS_CONFIRMATION = resource hint exists but exact value not stable.
- OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT = number/eligibility depends on unresolved auth/callback/release packets.
Rule: Claude must not promote INTERNAL_ESTIMATE, COST_CLASS_DERIVED, NEEDS_CONFIRMATION, or OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT values into contractual pricing or finance-grade truth without human review.

Generator-dependent truth:
- full skill estate population beyond canonical critical-path rows,
- any row explicitly marked generation_required_YN = Yes,
- any route/provider/runtime row still linked to an external artifact or open packet.

Still intentionally open:
- callback URIs and verification values not proven in source,
- exact vendor pricing not proven in source,
- any estate-scale row inventory not source-final.

Build order for Claude:
1. registries, schemas, validators, and ingest subsystem
2. director/skill/route/runtime closure ledgers
3. Mission Control / Chitragupta / control-plane builder surfaces
4. hybrid switch runtime + provider auth gates
5. media factory and provider-bound execution paths only after blockers are honored

DIRECTOR_CANONICALIZATION_DISCIPLINE_NOTE_v22
- The 30-director estate is source-proven as a structural estate, council model, and jurisdiction model.
- The full 30-row runtime contract is NOT literally source-final row by row.
- v21 canonical overlays remain valid for builder continuity, but they must not be mistaken for direct source-final rows.
- allowed_for_machine_trust_YN = Yes means the row is safe as current canonical implementation guidance.
- allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN = Yes means Claude may use the row as build guidance without inventing new values.
- source_final_row_YN = No means the literal row was not present verbatim in source material.

DIRECTOR_RUNTIME_AUTHORITY_STATUS_REGISTER_v22
| authority_class | meaning | builder_action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SOURCE_PROVEN_FINAL | literal row or exact field present in source | build directly |
| SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL | exact role/jurisdiction is structurally clear, but row needed normalization | build using canonical row only |
| CANONICALIZED_FROM_PARTIAL_SOURCE | row closed from multiple source fragments | do not extend beyond canonical fields |
| NEEDS_CONFIRMATION | source is suggestive but not exact | keep blocked and artifact-linked |

CANONICAL_SKILL_ROWS_VS_FACTORY_GENERATED_ROWS_v22
| class | meaning | trust rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| literal source-final row | exact skill contract exists in source | Claude may use without generation |
| canonical runtime row | exact critical-path row normalized from multiple sources | Claude may use as current build overlay but must not expand fields beyond row |
| factory-generated row | produced from family manifest + archetype + validator | Claude must run generator/validator path |
| unresolved row | source or generator dependency still open | keep blocked and artifact-linked |

SKILL_GENERATOR_AND_VALIDATOR_DEPENDENCY_NOTE_v22
- The source explicitly prohibits hand-writing 218 skill files first.
- Canonical build law remains: family manifests -> archetype templates -> skill factory -> contract validator -> generated skill contracts.
- Therefore the skill estate must remain separated into: already canonical rows, generator-dependent rows, and unresolved rows.

ROUTE_RELEASE_TRUST_MATRIX_v22
| route_trust_level | meaning | release implication |
| --- | --- | --- |
| High | owner/veto/fallback/director/skill/provider state sufficiently canonical for current build guidance | attended or bounded release may proceed if provider blockers are closed |
| Medium | route depends on some canonicalized or generated lower-layer truth | release only with explicit trust boundary |
| Low | route depends on open provider/auth or unresolved lower-layer rows | no unattended or enterprise release |
| High-Conditional | route itself is strong, but downstream dependent route still controls release legality | treat as pre-release only |

DYNAMIC_PRELOAD_DORMANT_UNLOAD_POLICY_MATRIX_v22
| policy_class | meaning | builder_note |
| --- | --- | --- |
| preload_modules | modules that must be warm before fast-track execution begins | keep to guard modules + verified route modules |
| dormant_modules | modules available but not active unless trigger fires | use for heavy media or deep analytics |
| unload_policy | what must be removed from hot path after stage completion | protects RAM/VRAM on average hardware |
| hot_cache_retention | what may remain cached safely | retain only route-safe frequently reused state |

FAST_TRACK_ROUTE_BINDING_RULES_v22
- Verified creator_mode_id must bind to a fixed verified_route_id.
- Only Krishna, Aruna, Yama, Kubera, and Vayu remain non-removable hard guards.
- Dynamic unload may not bypass legality, budget, or hardware safety.
- Average-hardware creator modes must declare explicit quality tradeoff and offload relation.

CHITRAGUPTA_COMPOSITOR_RUNTIME_PACK_v22
- Canonical role: deterministic media composition planner at the physical execution layer.
- Build rule: do not render from prose or implicit asset assumptions; require asset_manifest or synthetic_asset_waiver.
- Deployment rule: hybrid or cloud preferred for heavy timelines; local allowed for preview/degraded routes.

ASYNC_RENDER_QUEUE_INFRA_PACK_v22
- Canonical role: queue long-running render/export jobs with checkpoints, retries, and replay-safe status.
- Build rule: route heavy media through queue; do not keep in synchronous UI/control path.
- Safety rule: stale lease or zombie job must pass through failure handling, not silent retry storms.

REJECTION_REWORK_LOOP_SUBSYSTEM_v22
- Canonical role: formal return path after realism or QA rejection.
- Inputs: rejection verdict, failing artifact refs, required remediation.
- Outputs: rework job envelope, audit record, archive linkage.

API_PAYLOAD_ADAPTER_LAYER_v22
- Canonical role: transform route/task envelopes into provider-safe payloads and normalize responses back into runtime-safe objects.
- Build rule: adapters own provider schema drift; workers and directors must not absorb provider-specific payload logic directly.

FINANCIAL_GOVERNOR_RUNTIME_PACK_v22
- Canonical role: physical-execution-side spend governor complementary to Kubera policy law.
- Build rule: no retry storm or premium escalation may bypass cost law at runtime plumbing level.

TABLE_LEVEL_AND_ROW_LEVEL_PROVENANCE_PROPAGATION_RULES_v22
- Every economics table must carry one of: SOURCE_PROVEN_EXACT, INTERNAL_ESTIMATE, COST_CLASS_DERIVED, NEEDS_CONFIRMATION, OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT.
- If a table is estimate-driven, any downstream row copied from it inherits the estimate tag unless independently proven.
- Claude must not collapse estimate labels when generating deployment or cost recommendations.

GAP FAMILY 12 — AGGREGATE SCALE VS EXACT INVENTORY RULE
======================================================================
Authority_Tag: SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + AGGREGATE_POSTURE
AGGREGATE_SCALE_VS_EXACT_OPERATIONAL_INVENTORY_NOTE_v22
- Source-backed scale claims are important for intent and architecture sizing.
- They are not identical to exact operational inventories.
- Claude and builders must separate structural estate scale from exact row-final runtime inventory.

GAP FAMILY 14 — CLAUDE NO-GUESS BUILD RULES
======================================================================
Authority_Tag: SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + MACHINE_TRUST_BOUNDARY
CLAUDE_NO_GUESS_BUILD_RULES_v22
- Claude must not invent provider callback/auth exacts.
- Claude must not assume all 30 directors are literal source-final rows.
- Claude must not assume all 218 skills are literal source-final rows.
- Claude must not treat route legality as unconditional if linked director/skill/provider rows are partial.
- Claude must not treat internal planning economics as contractual pricing.
- Claude must not invent missing physical execution modules.
- Claude must not treat aggregate estate scale claims as exact operational inventories.
- Claude must not promote deferred future modules into current blockers unless a later stage explicitly activates them.

| trust_class | Claude action |
| --- | --- |
| SOURCE_PROVEN_FINAL | may build directly |
| SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL | may build canonical contract without inventing extra exacts |
| CANONICALIZED_FROM_PARTIAL_SOURCE | may use appended canonical row only; do not extend fields |
| INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | may plan, may not promise as exact |
| OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT | do not guess; preserve blocker |
| DEFERRED_FUTURE_MODULE | keep preserved in roadmap/appendix; do not build as current blocker |
| HISTORICAL_CONFLICT_RETAINED | preserve for lineage; do not treat as current implementation authority |

======================================================================
DIRECTOR_CANONICALIZATION_DISCIPLINE_NOTE_v23
======================================================================
| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| SOURCE_FINAL_ROW | literal row is directly and fully proved in source corpus; Claude may use without expansion beyond the row |
| CANONICALIZED_FROM_PARTIAL_SOURCE | row is closed for builder continuity using source-backed structure plus explicit canonicalization discipline |
| GENERATOR_REQUIRED | exact downstream row still needs validator/factory or artifact completion |
| OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT | row is blocked by an explicit artifact or decision packet; do not guess exact runtime truth |
| allowed_for_machine_trust_YN | the row may be used as implementation guidance within the stated trust boundary |
| allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN | Claude may consume the row without inventing missing fields beyond those explicitly provided |

======================================================================
DIRECTOR_MACHINE_TRUST_BOUNDARY_REGISTER_v23
======================================================================
| trust_boundary_class | directors_in_scope | safe_use_case | unsafe_use_case |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Constitutional core | Krishna, Vishnu, Ganesha, Kubera, Yama, Aruna | route legality reasoning, governance scaffolding, hybrid/resource gating | inventing extra per-director fields or assuming all shadow pairs are source-final |
| Canonicalized execution directors | Shakti, Tumburu, Arjuna, Hanuman, Saraswati | builder planning, deployment binding, creator/runtime planning | claiming literal source-final row truth |
| Structural estate remainder | all remaining directors | architectural continuity and population planning | machine-final no-guess runtime execution without required artifacts |

======================================================================
CANONICAL_SKILL_ROWS_VS_FACTORY_GENERATED_ROWS_v23
======================================================================
| row_class | meaning | safe_use_case | unsafe_use_case |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Canonical family row | SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL family truth with runtime promotion rules | planning, route binding, validator chain design | pretending every family member row is already literal and final |
| Critical-path canonical row | route-relevant row closed enough for guarded runtime use | release gating, fallback legality, builder continuity | inventing omitted fields or bypassing validator/policy chain |
| Factory-generated row | runtime row emitted through skill factory + validator chain | repo generation, governed runtime promotion | using as machine-final before generator + validator completion |
| Open / unresolved row | source or artifact incomplete | preserve blocker, bind owner/artifact | guessing contracts, retries, or acceptance tests |

======================================================================
SKILL_GENERATOR_AND_VALIDATOR_DEPENDENCY_NOTE_v23
======================================================================
| field | value |
| --- | --- |
| core_rule | the structural 218-skill estate is source-backed, but exact row-final runtime truth is not universally literal in source |
| factory_dependence | many runtime rows are expected to come from factories, validators, manifests, and route binders |
| Claude rule | Claude may use canonical rows, family rules, and explicit critical-path rows; Claude must not invent full estate closure from scale posture alone |
| builder implication | no generated row becomes machine-final until validator_required_YN = Yes is satisfied and required artifacts exist |

SKILL_MACHINE_TRUST_BOUNDARY_REGISTER_v23

| trust_boundary_class | scope | safe_use_case | unsafe_use_case |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Critical-path canonical skills | M-017, M-018, M-068, M-080, M-200, M-218 and family anchors | guarded release reasoning and builder planning | assuming complete literal estate finality |
| Family structural truth | FAM-KERNEL, FAM-SCRIPT, FAM-MEDIA, FAM-ANALYTICS, FAM-ADAPTIVE | factory design, repo organization, validator chain authoring | skipping generation/validation |
| Generated runtime rows | factory output only | runtime promotion after validation | no-guess runtime use before generation |

======================================================================
ROUTE_RELEASE_TRUST_MATRIX_v23
======================================================================
| trust_level | meaning | builder_rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| High | all linked lower layers are final enough for current release posture | may proceed within current mode and blocker law |
| Medium | route is structurally valid but inherits canonicalized lower-layer truth | proceed only with trust-boundary awareness |
| Conditional | route is usable only in attended/test/founder-supervised modes | do not present as unattended or enterprise safe |
| Low | provider/director/skill exactness still open | preserve as blocked or attended-only |

======================================================================
ROUTE_TRUST_INHERITANCE_NOTE_v23
======================================================================
A route is never more final than its weakest linked authority row. If a linked director row is canonicalized, a linked skill row is generated-only, or a provider callback/auth row is OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT, the route must inherit that weaker trust level. Claude must not flatten route legality into unconditional release readiness.

======================================================================
TABLE_LEVEL_AND_ROW_LEVEL_PROVENANCE_PROPAGATION_RULES_v23
======================================================================
| rule_id | rule |
| --- | --- |
| ECO-PROP-01 | Every economics table must carry a table-level provenance class and every row that differs must override it explicitly. |
| ECO-PROP-02 | SOURCE_PROVEN_EXACT may be used for contractual or procurement-sensitive reasoning only when the source corpus literally proves the value. |
| ECO-PROP-03 | INTERNAL_ESTIMATE and COST_CLASS_DERIVED are safe for planning and architecture trade-offs only. |
| ECO-PROP-04 | OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT rows are never release-authoritative or procurement-safe. |
| ECO-PROP-05 | Claude must propagate row-level provenance into downstream recommendations and must not average or collapse rows into a false exactness claim. |

======================================================================
COST_MODEL_TRUST_BOUNDARY_NOTE_v23
======================================================================
Planning economics in this PRD are builder-useful, not procurement-final. Safe use: internal planning, route comparison, capacity preparation, founder trade-off analysis. Unsafe use: vendor commitment, sales pricing, enterprise contract promises, unattended-release business cases that depend on unresolved provider/auth exactness.

======================================================================
DYNAMIC_PRELOAD_DORMANT_UNLOAD_POLICY_MATRIX_v23
======================================================================
| policy_field | structural_rule | implementation implication | source_status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| preload | required_directors, required_skills, required_tools must load for verified mode | mode switch must explicitly warm only the active route set | SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL |
| dormant | non_required_crews, non_required_models, non_required_dashboards remain dormant | do not waste RAM/VRAM/tokens on off-route modules | SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL |
| unload_policy | on_mode_switch true | unload stale route modules after guard pass | SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL |
| hot_cache_retention | keep_hot_cache_for_last_modes=3 | retain recent route cache only | SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL |
| hard_guards_retained | Yama, Kubera, Vayu always remain active | no fast-track mode may bypass hard guards | SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL |

======================================================================
FAST_TRACK_ROUTE_BINDING_RULES_v23
======================================================================
| rule_id | rule | source_status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FT-001 | verified creator_mode_id binds to a verified route_id and module_profile_id |
| FT-002 | fast-track may reduce warm modules but may not unload Yama, Kubera, or Vayu |
| FT-003 | fast-track route cannot claim premium realism if unloaded modules make quality floors unattainable |
| FT-004 | fast-track is an average-hardware optimization path, not a bypass of legality or quality governance |

======================================================================
AGGREGATE_SCALE_VS_EXACT_OPERATIONAL_INVENTORY_NOTE_v23
======================================================================
Authority_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL for estate scale, AGGREGATE_SCALE_ONLY where exact inventory rows are not literal in source

The source corpus proves large structural estate posture - 30 directors, 218 skills, 112 Claude agents, 146 Claude skills, 9000+ plugin ecosystem - but does not prove every one of those as a fully row-final literal runtime inventory. Builders and Claude must distinguish scale posture from exact operational inventory.

======================================================================

======================================================================
| estate_claim | what_is_proved | what_is_not_proved | trust_rule | source_status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 30 directors | structural estate and named council architecture | fully literal row-final runtime exactness for every field | use structural estate; treat per-row finality through director trust matrices | AGGREGATE_SCALE_ONLY |
| 218 skills | structural skill estate and family/factory architecture | every row as literal source-final runtime truth | use family/canonical/generator separation | AGGREGATE_SCALE_ONLY |
| 112 Claude agents | aggregate scale posture in source corpus | exact active operational agent inventory | do not treat as exact runtime list | AGGREGATE_SCALE_ONLY |
| 146 Claude skills | aggregate Claude-native posture | exact per-skill runtime closure | do not collapse into runtime inventory | AGGREGATE_SCALE_ONLY |
| 9000+ plugins | aggregate plugin ecosystem posture | exact approved plugin inventory | treat as scale signal only | AGGREGATE_SCALE_ONLY |

CLAUDE_NATIVE_FREE_PAID_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_MATRIX_v23
======================================================================
Authority_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL from project source corpus and preserved Claude-native appendix

| Claude surface | realistic low-cost / free-start use | paid / stronger automation use | not_the_runtime_for | builder warning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Projects | PRD, DNA, architecture law, blocker ledgers, deck iterations | n/a | heavy render, provider-bound runtime | project brain, not media engine |
| GitHub integration | repo reading, registry/schema review, docs and route maps | n/a | CI/CD or execution runtime by itself | repo-context layer, not execution layer |
| Skills | repeatable audits, route checks, deck critic, blocker checks | stronger with code execution / broader paid usage depending on account posture | avatar render, publish APIs, long async jobs | workflow layer, not execution fabric |
| MCP / connectors | logs, dashboards, docs, analytics, browser/test/tool access | broader connected ops in paid/pro phases | direct replacement for external systems | bridge layer only |
| Claude Code | repo edits, validators, loaders, tests, automation | primary paid/build automation layer | heavy media runtime, provider execution | build engine, not production render engine |
| External providers | not a Claude-native freebie | main production spend path | n/a | avatar, voice, render, publish, analytics stay here |

======================================================================
CLAUDE_NATIVE_SAFE_USAGE_NOTE_v23
======================================================================
| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| low-cost bootstrap != free production | Claude-native layers reduce planning/build cost; they do not remove provider/runtime cost |
| free/cheap applies mainly to project brain layers | PRD, DNA, repo understanding, audits, checklists, deck generation |
| production triggers paid or external layers | Claude Code automation, connector-heavy ops, avatar/voice/render/publish providers |
| Claude must not be treated as the heavy media runtime | use Claude for control, review, QA, and repo intelligence; use external providers/workers for execution |

| section_or_module | current_canonical_YN | historical_retained_YN | inspiration_only_YN | conflict_preserved_YN | generator_dependent_YN | open_packet_dependent_YN | deferred_future_YN | builder_must_follow | Claude_no_guess_rule |

| Generated skill rows and canonicalized actor rows | Partial | Yes | No | No | Yes | No | No | Use validator/factory chains and trust boundaries | Do not treat canonicalized rows as literal source-final rows |

PROMPT_ENGINEER / CREW COMPOSER DOMINANCE NOTE_v24
======================================================================
Authority_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL

Prompt Engineer, PROMPT_SPEC_OBJECT, and Crew Composer are not just listed utilities. They are dominant operator subsystems because they convert human intent into normalized machine-safe route objects, preview legality/cost/resource impact, and persist reusable operational compositions.

======================================================================
CLAUDE_NATIVE_TRUST_AND_COST_BOUNDARY_REINFORCEMENT_v24
======================================================================
Authority_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL

Builder rule:
- free-start Claude layers are valid for project-brain, review, documentation, registry planning, and some structured authoring flows
- paid Claude layers are typically required for serious repo mutation, automation, CI helpers, and stronger integrated build flows
- external providers remain mandatory for heavy avatar, sync, render, publish, and analytics execution
- mislabeling a module as “free Claude runtime” when it is actually heavy execution is a build and budget defect

| --- | --- |

| inspiration-only historical modules stay quarantined | they may inform future work, not current exact build use |
| conflict-retained lines must stay labeled | no silent normalization or implied equivalence |
| deferred future architecture stays visible but non-blocking | preserves roadmap memory without confusing present build authority |

| identity | agent_id, agent_name, agent_class |

| authority | parent-scoped permissions |
| inputs | task packets, route context, tool scope |
| outputs | helper outputs, analyses, or actions |
| reads | accessible registries / dossier fields |
| writes | approved outputs only |
| escalation | parent actor / operator / reviewer |
| placement | local / hybrid / cloud / repo automation |
| runtime_class | operational class vs aggregate posture |
| failure_surfaces | permission fail, stale context, tool fail |
| recovery_role | restart / parent retry / manual review |
| observability_hooks | agent trace / audit |
| archive_relation | optional lineage if artifact-bearing |
| cost_relevance | orchestration overhead / plugin exposure |
| trust_class | trust boundary |
| allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN | no-guess safe or not |

OPS_PRIMARY_SYSTEM_PRECEDENCE_NOTE_v24
======================================================================
| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| Mission Control is the dominant live runtime view | operational truth must be readable through explicit screens, DTOs, and actions |
| Chitragupta is the dominant RCA/replay authority | replay and RCA must bind to archive/evidence, not generic prose summaries |
| Dashboard summaries are secondary | creator-friendly surfaces must not outrank primary ops truth |
| Append packs do not count as dominance | operational systems must have screen/action/query binding |

======================================================================
| engine_name | threshold_status | placement_status | no_guess_rule |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BeatSyncEngine | structural, not fully source-final | local/hybrid safe | do not invent exact millisecond contract beyond declared status |
| VariantMitosis | structural, variant cap needs confirmation | hybrid preferred | do not invent unlimited branch counts |
| VayuRenderSupervisor | partly canonicalized from Vayu thresholds | hybrid/cloud mainline | do not claim per-engine exactness if not source-proven |
| EditDecisionEngine | structural, tuning partly estimated | local/hybrid | do not treat tuning estimates as final retention contract |
| AssetHunter | structural and placement-strong | local/hybrid | do not invent provider-specific asset sources |
| StyleEvolutionEngine | structural, governance-gated | hybrid/cloud | do not auto-promote unreviewed mutations |
| ProxyRenderEngine | structural | local/hybrid | do not treat proxy metrics as final production quality |
| Skill Debate Engine / Memory / Mutation / Governance | structural | local/hybrid/cloud depending queue | do not count restored names as final runtime thresholds |

FAST_TRACK_VERIFIED_ROUTE_RUNTIME_RULES_v24
======================================================================
| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| verified route required | fast-track mode may only use pre-validated route ids |
| hard guards never unload | Yama, Kubera, and Vayu stay active |
| unload is bounded | unload policy removes nonessential modules, not safety/core registry law |
| average hardware first | RAM/VRAM/latency effects must be explicit for 8 GB / 16 GB classes |
| source status | structural concept is source-backed; exact per-mode rows may remain canonicalized |

MODULE_BY_MODULE_CLAUDE_FREE_PAID_EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_MATRIX_v24
======================================================================
Authority_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL

| module_name | free_start_capable_YN | paid_claude_required_YN | external_provider_required_YN | not_safe_to_call_free_YN | project_brain_layer_YN | repo_automation_layer_YN | heavy_execution_layer_YN | runtime_risk_if_misclassified | cost_warning | Claude_no_guess_rule |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PRD Master / DNA Corpus | Yes | No | No | No | Yes | No | No | low | safe low-cost bootstrap layer | treat as planning/governance, not runtime execution |
| Registry Specs / Route Catalog | Yes | Later/Yes for serious automation | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | medium | free-start okay, stronger repo automation may require paid Claude Code | do not confuse registry authoring with execution runtime |
| Director / Skill closure ledgers | Yes | Later/Yes for repo mutation | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | medium | closure review cheap; multi-file patching shifts paid | do not treat review layer as execution fabric |
| Mission Control summaries / RCA drafting | Yes | Optional/Yes for tool-heavy ops | Partial | No | Yes | Yes | No | medium | connectors/tooling can add cost | do not treat summaries as live observability backend |
| Dashboard / Help / Manual / Creator Modes | Yes | Optional/Yes for code generation | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | low | authoring cheap; frontend/runtime hosting separate | do not call UI authoring the UI runtime |
| Prompt Engineer / Crew Composer | Yes | Optional/Yes for repo-integrated build | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | medium | preview/authoring cheap; compiled-route automation may require paid build layer | do not treat as heavy execution engine |
| Sync-on-Ingest / hot reload governance | Partial | Yes for real repo + tool mutation | No | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | high | live mutation automation is not safely “free” in production | do not treat low-cost ideation as production-safe mutation service |
| Heavy media orchestration glue | Partial | Yes for robust automation | Yes | Yes | Partial | Yes | Yes | high | orchestration may be cheap, execution is not | do not treat orchestration as render runtime |
| Avatar Generation | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | No | Yes | high | provider/GPU-heavy production path | Claude must not be treated as avatar runtime |
| Lip-Sync / Emotion-Sync execution | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | No | Yes | high | sync/render path is not free-start runtime | Claude may define QA, not replace execution |
| Editing / Retention render chain | Partial | Yes for automation | Yes | Yes | Partial | Yes | Yes | high | planning can be cheap, render/export is not | do not call editing/render path “free” |
| Publish / Analytics live operations | No | Partial/Yes for connector-heavy automation | Yes | Yes | Partial | Yes | Yes | high | API/provider/runtime costs dominate | do not treat publish/auth flows as free project-brain work |
| Repo automation / CI / tests | No | Yes | No | Yes | No | Yes | No | medium | paid build automation likely required | do not imply free chat features replace Claude Code/CI |
| Logs / dashboards / browser test bridges | Partial | Yes for serious automation | Partial | Yes | Partial | Yes | No | medium | connectors/tool access add real cost and limits | do not treat MCP/connectors as costless or unlimited |

======================================================================
CLAUDE_NATIVE_TRUST_AND_COST_BOUNDARY_NOTE_v24
======================================================================
| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| free-start capable != production-free | low-cost bootstrap is valid for project brain layers only |
| paid Claude layers matter | Claude Code / broader automation / connector-heavy flows are not the same as free-start planning |
| external providers remain main production spend path | avatar, voice, render, publish, analytics stay external/provider-bound |
| misclassification causes budget and runtime failure | the biggest risk is calling a heavy execution path “Claude-free” when it is not |

======================================================================
SAFE_USE_UNSAFE_USE_AND_CONTRACT_TRUST_MATRIX_v24
======================================================================
| economics class | safe use | unsafe use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SOURCE_PROVEN_EXACT | bounded direct use for exact sourced values | extending beyond source scope |
| INTERNAL_ESTIMATE | planning, prioritization, comparative route analysis | contractual pricing, external commitments |
| COST_CLASS_DERIVED | early planning when exact values are absent | procurement, release pricing, hard ROI claims |
| OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT | blocker visibility and contingency planning | release approval or unattended cost certainty |
| NEEDS_CONFIRMATION | placeholder for later benchmark/vendor closure | any exact budgeting decision |



======================================================================
PHYSICAL_EXECUTION_MAINLINE_PRECEDENCE_NOTE_v24
======================================================================
Authority_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL

| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| physical execution stack is mainline runtime plumbing | compositor, async render queue, rejection/rework, adapters, and financial governor are central runtime modules |
| media runtime is incomplete without physical execution | architecture/gov/media logic alone does not produce a trustworthy production machine |
| physical execution must outrank “supplemental appendix” treatment | builders and Claude must treat these as central implementation spine |
| no optionalization by narrative | do not demote compositor/queue/rework/adapters/governor to afterthoughts |

======================================================================
MEDIA_FACTORY_TO_PHYSICAL_EXECUTION_BINDING_MATRIX_v24
======================================================================
| media stage | required physical execution module | why it is central |
| --- | --- | --- |
| avatar / scene composition | ChitraguptaCompositor | turns upstream assets into deterministic composition outputs |
| render scheduling | AsyncRenderQueue | governs queue placement, retry, throughput, and callback wait behavior |
| failed quality path | RejectionReworkLoop | converts rejection into structured rework instead of vague retry prose |
| provider payload normalization | ApiPayloadAdapterLayer | normalizes real provider inputs/outputs and callback packets |
| execution-side cost control | FinancialGovernorRuntime | enforces execution-path spend controls beyond high-level policy narration |



======================================================================
ESTATE_SCALE_NO_GUESS_RULES_v24
======================================================================
Authority_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + AGGREGATE_SCALE_ONLY where applicable

| rule | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| structural estate != exact runtime inventory | 30 directors and 218 skills are structural estate truths, not universal proof of fully literal row-final runtime closure |
| aggregate posture != operational list | 112 Claude agents / 146 Claude skills / 9000+ plugins are scale posture, not exact active runtime inventory |
| generated inventory must stay tagged | factory-generated rows must not be misread as literal source-final rows |
| no-guess applies to scale claims too | Claude must not expand scale posture into exact inventories without explicit source-proven rows |

======================================================================
AGGREGATE_POSTURE_VS_EXACT_RUNTIME_INVENTORY_MATRIX_v24
======================================================================
| inventory_class | meaning | trusted for no-guess build Y/N |
| --- | --- | --- |
| structural_estate | named architecture scale and family structure | Partial, only with trust boundaries |
| aggregate_posture | large ecosystem/count signal | No |
| exact_row_final_inventory | fully source-final row-closed inventory | Yes |
| generated_inventory | factory/validator-emitted runtime rows | Partial, only after generation + validation |
| operational_inventory | currently approved/active runtime subset | Yes when explicitly registered |
| not_yet_proven_inventory | implied or preserved scale without exact rows | No |

ROUTE_RELEASE_ALLOWED_ONLY_IF_ALL_LINKED_ROWS_TRUSTED_NOTE_v25
======================================================================
A route is not machine-final merely because it is structurally present. It becomes no-guess releasable only when linked owner, veto, director, skill, provider, and fallback rows are all trusted to the required execution mode.

======================================================================
CLAUDE_NATIVE_PLAN_ENTITLEMENT_AND_RUNTIME_LIMIT_NOTE_v25
======================================================================
Claude-native layers are excellent for project brain, architecture continuity, repo reasoning, workflow templates, and governed build intelligence. They are not a universal free replacement for provider-bound publishing, premium voice, avatar generation, render/export, or high-throughput background execution. Plan, org, and connector availability can change the exact entitlement surface; therefore module-level caveats must remain explicit.

DIRECTOR_CANONICALIZED_VS_SOURCE_FINAL_NOTE_v26
======================================================================
Current director overlays are useful as canonical working law, not as literal proof that every director runtime row is source-final. Structural truth is strong. Row-final truth remains mixed between preserved source, canonicalized closure, and generator-required continuation.

======================================================================
DIRECTOR_NO_GUESS_RUNTIME_AUTHORITY_RULES_v26
======================================================================
1. Claude must not assume any director row is literal source-final unless source_final_row_YN = Yes.
2. Canonicalized runtime overlays are allowed for planning and governed implementation, but not for unsupported exactness claims.
3. Shadow-pair, jurisdiction, and override details remain non-final where source literal rows do not exist.
4. Any execution-critical route depending on a director row with allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN = No must inherit conditional trust.

======================================================================
| row_class | meaning | allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN |
| --- | --- | --- |
| literal_source_final_row | exact source-backed runtime row | Yes |
| canonical_runtime_row | governed row emitted from merged source truth but not literal per-row source finality | No |
| factory_generated_row | row emitted by validator/factory chain | No until generated + validated |
| unresolved_row | row still waiting on source or artifact closure | No |

======================================================================
1. Claude must not claim the full 218-skill estate is literal source-final runtime truth.
2. Canonical family truth is allowed for structure, routing intent, and generation discipline.
3. Generated rows are not safe for no-guess use until emitted, validated, and registered.
4. Critical-path skills remain release-relevant, but their literal finality must follow factory + validator + registry closure.

======================================================================
ROUTE_TRUST_PROPAGATION_RULES_v26
======================================================================
1. A route is release-allowed only when owner, veto, actor, provider, and fallback dependencies are all trusted for the intended mode.
2. Provider OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT status always downgrades unattended and enterprise release to blocked.
3. Canonicalized actor rows preserve architecture continuity but do not automatically grant no-guess route trust.
4. Internal-only operational routes may proceed under governed trust even when not externally releasable.

======================================================================
ROUTE_RELEASE_ALLOWED_ONLY_IF_ALL_LINKED_ROWS_TRUSTED_NOTE_v26
======================================================================
No route may be represented as fully releasable if it still inherits unresolved lower-layer truth. Structural completeness is not release legality.

======================================================================
PROCUREMENT_SAFE_VS_PLANNING_SAFE_COST_USAGE_RULES_v26
======================================================================
1. Planning-safe means the table can guide architecture and prioritization.
2. Procurement-safe means the table is benchmark-backed, quote-backed, or SOURCE_PROVEN_FINAL. Most Shadow Empire economics tables are not procurement-safe.
3. Release-safe means the economic assumptions do not hide open provider/auth exactness. OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT rows are not release-safe.

======================================================================
COST_TABLE_CONTRACT_SAFETY_NOTE_v26
======================================================================
V26 economics are intentionally more honest, not more exact. Unless a row is benchmark-backed and explicitly procurement_safe_YN = Yes, it must not be treated as contract, quote, or customer-pricing truth.

======================================================================
CLAUDE_NATIVE_PLAN_ENTITLEMENT_AND_RUNTIME_LIMIT_NOTE_v26
======================================================================
Claude-native layers are excellent for project brain, architecture continuity, repo reasoning, workflow templates, and governed build intelligence. They are not a universal free replacement for provider-bound publishing, premium voice, avatar generation, render/export, or high-throughput background execution. Exact plan/org entitlement can vary, so module-level caveats must remain explicit.




======================================================================
DIRECTOR_CANONICALIZED_VS_SOURCE_FINAL_NOTE_v26
======================================================================
Literal source-final director runtime rows are still limited. Canonicalized rows preserve operational continuity but must not be mistaken for no-guess source-final truth.

======================================================================
DIRECTOR_NO_GUESS_RUNTIME_AUTHORITY_RULES_v26
======================================================================
1. A director row is no-guess safe only if source_final_row_YN = Yes and allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN = Yes.
2. Canonicalized rows may be used for governed planning and registry continuation, not unsupported exactness claims.
3. Route legality inherits any director row that remains non-final.

======================================================================
CANONICAL_ROWS_VS_GENERATED_ROWS_VS_UNRESOLVED_ROWS_v26
======================================================================
| row_class | meaning | allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN |
| --- | --- | --- |
| literal_source_final_row | exact source-backed runtime row | Yes |
| canonical_runtime_row | governed row from merged source truth but not literal row proof | No |
| factory_generated_row | row emitted by validator/factory chain | No until emitted + validated |
| unresolved_row | row waiting on source or artifact closure | No |

======================================================================
SKILL_FACTORY_VALIDATOR_NO_GUESS_RULES_v26
======================================================================
Claude must not claim the full 218-skill estate is literal row-final runtime truth. Family truth and generator truth are both real, but they are not the same as universal row-final closure.

======================================================================
ROUTE_TRUST_PROPAGATION_RULES_v26
======================================================================
Route trust equals the weakest linked layer. OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT provider exactness or non-final actor rows automatically downgrade release trust.

======================================================================
No route may be represented as fully releasable if it still inherits unresolved lower-layer truth.

======================================================================
UNIVERSAL_EXECUTION_AND_NO_DEPENDENCY_SURVIVAL_LAW_v27
Truth_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + CANONICAL PROJECT LAW
======================================================================
Purpose: enforce universal execution posture and dependency-minimized survival path across Shadow Empire.
Builder Rule: no major module may rely on a premium external provider as its only existence path unless explicitly marked provider-mandatory and release-blocking.

Core law:
1. Every major module must declare support posture for local without API keys, local with API keys, local or cloud without provider dependency where self-hosted/self-managed alternatives exist, and provider-accelerated operation where premium quality is desired.
2. External providers may improve realism, speed, convenience, or scale, but must not become the only survival path for planning, governance, routing, control-plane, dossier, registry, or builder workflows.
3. When a premium provider is unavailable, the machine must degrade to one of the following instead of becoming undefined:
   - local no-key degraded mode
   - local keyed mode
   - hybrid self-hosted mode
   - manual export / attended publish mode
4. Publish and analytics may fall back to export-package generation and attended/manual upload if live provider APIs are unavailable.
5. Heavy media execution remains provider-accelerated where source-backed realism requires it, but a degraded no-dependency path must still be declared where physically possible.

Canonical fields for all future runtime and deployment tables:
- local_no_key_supported_YN
- local_keyed_supported_YN
- hybrid_no_key_supported_YN
- hybrid_keyed_supported_YN
- cloud_self_hosted_supported_YN
- external_provider_optional_YN
- external_provider_mandatory_YN
- manual_export_fallback_YN
- no_dependency_survival_path
- offline_degraded_mode_description
- quality_delta_vs_premium
- dependency_class

No-dependency survival rule:
If external APIs, provider callbacks, premium voice/avatar services, or cloud workers are absent, the system must degrade into a still-valid local or self-hosted execution path rather than fail as undefined. Provider-backed execution may improve quality, realism, speed, or scale, but must not be the only architectural existence path unless explicitly marked provider-mandatory and release-blocking.


======================================================================

======================================================================

2. Replay is illegal when auth state is stale, callback proof is missing, or lineage cannot prove idempotent re-entry.
3. Poller fallback is mandatory where callback exactness is open, but poller fallback does not convert the route into unattended-release-safe truth.

======================================================================
DIRECTOR_CANONICALIZED_VS_SOURCE_FINAL_NOTE_v27
======================================================================
Literal source-final director runtime rows remain limited. Structural estate truth is strong; full row-final runtime truth remains mixed between preserved source, canonicalized closure, and generator-required continuation. Builders and Claude must treat canonicalized director rows as governed working law, not as literal source-final evidence.

======================================================================
DIRECTOR_NO_GUESS_RUNTIME_AUTHORITY_RULES_v27
======================================================================
1. A director row is no-guess safe only if source_final_row_YN = Yes and allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN = Yes.
2. Canonicalized rows are allowed for governed planning and registry continuation, not unsupported exactness claims.
3. Shadow-pair, jurisdiction, delegation, and override details remain non-final wherever direct literal source rows do not exist.
4. Any execution-critical route depending on a director row with allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN = No must inherit conditional trust.


======================================================================

======================================================================
CANONICAL_ROWS_VS_GENERATED_ROWS_VS_UNRESOLVED_ROWS_v27
======================================================================
| row_class | meaning | allowed_for_claude_no_guess_YN |
| --- | --- | --- |
| literal_source_final_row | exact source-backed runtime row | Yes |
| canonical_runtime_row | governed row from merged source truth but not literal row proof | No |
| factory_generated_row | row emitted by validator/factory chain | No until emitted + validated |
| unresolved_row | row waiting on source or artifact closure | No |

======================================================================
SKILL_FACTORY_VALIDATOR_NO_GUESS_RULES_v27
======================================================================
1. Claude must not claim the full 218-skill estate is literal row-final runtime truth.
2. Family truth and generator truth are both valid architectural laws, but they are not the same as universal row-final closure.
3. Any route depending on generated or unresolved skill rows must inherit conditional trust.

Purpose: make route trust inherit from lower-layer truth instead of looking independently final.
Builder Rule: no route is more trusted than its weakest linked director, skill, provider, or fallback dependency.

======================================================================
ROUTE_TRUST_PROPAGATION_RULES_v27
======================================================================
1. Route trust equals the weakest linked layer.
2. OPEN_PACKET_DEPENDENT provider exactness automatically downgrades unattended and enterprise release trust.
3. CANONICALIZED_FROM_PARTIAL_SOURCE actor rows prohibit no-guess route claims.
4. Generated skill rows require validator output before route exactness can increase.

======================================================================
ROUTE_RELEASE_ALLOWED_ONLY_IF_ALL_LINKED_ROWS_TRUSTED_NOTE_v27
======================================================================
No route may be represented as fully releasable if it still inherits unresolved lower-layer truth.
