Truth_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + CANONICAL CONTEXT OWNERSHIP NORMALIZATION
======================================================================
Why_this_pack_is_needed = Directors already hold decision, approval, and veto gravity, but their row-level Context Engineering ownership was not made explicit in v30.
Row_Fields_Added_For_All_Directors =
- context_domains_owned
- context_enrichment_scope
- context_handoff_authority
- context_lock_policy
- context_escalation_rule
- context_redaction_authority
- context_reopen_authority
- context_default_packet_families
Director_Context_Register =
| Director | Primary_Context_Domains_Owned | Context_Enrichment_Scope | Context_Handoff_Authority | Context_Lock_Policy | Context_Escalation_Rule | Context_Default_Packet_Families |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Krishna | route intent, final sanction, system coherence | full-route synthesis and conflict merge | any phase-1 packet to next lawful route | may freeze final route packets after approval | founder or tribunal on unresolved route conflict | intent_packet, approval_packet, publish_readiness_packet |
| Shiva | strategic reset, destructive simplification, decommission posture | pruning and closure context | architectural closure or reset handoff | freeze on destructive change review | founder review required for destructive reopen | approval_packet, context_compression_packet |
| Brahma | creation planning, scene/world initiation, structural generation seed | creation blueprint enrichment | scene/video planning packets to production paths | lock after creation schema approval | escalate to Vishwakarma or Krishna on structural conflict | generation_packet, scene_prompt_packet |
| Vishnu | preservation, continuity, system stability | continuity and preservation context | continuity-safe handoff across routes | lock continuity-critical context once preserved | escalate on continuity break or regression risk | approval_packet, validation_packet |
| Shakti | empowerment, activation energy, expansion intent | activation and amplification context | empowerment packets to strategic/creative routes | lock only on approved growth posture | escalate on unsafe amplification | intent_packet, generation_packet |
| Chanakya | viability, ROI, strategic filtering | qualification and scoring enrichment | qualification packets to research/script routes | freeze rejected-topic packets after verdict | escalate to Krishna when strategic and narrative goals conflict | qualification_packet |
| Narada | discovery, trend sensing, signal intake | research signal enrichment | research packets to qualification and synthesis | lock raw source snapshots after capture | escalate on source conflict or insufficient evidence | research_context_packet |
| Ravana | adversarial challenge, flaw exposure | challenge-case enrichment | debate and failure packets to review lanes | lock challenge evidence after issuance | escalate to tribunal when risk remains unresolved | debate_packet, validation_packet |
| Durga | defense, resilience, protective gating | security and defensive context | protective packet handoff to governance/system routes | freeze when threat condition active | escalate on attack or prompt-injection risk | validation_packet, approval_packet |
| Yudhishthira | truthfulness, fairness, final integrity | fairness and truth consistency enrichment | integrity packets to final approval | freeze factual verdict packets after pass/reject | escalate on truth conflict or fairness ambiguity | validation_packet, approval_packet |
| Valmiki | narrative structuring, long-form story architecture | narrative framing enrichment | story/context handoff to script generation | lock approved narrative spine before final drafting | escalate on narrative coherence failure | generation_packet |
| Vyasa | knowledge synthesis, archive framing | synthesis and knowledge packing | synthesis packets to script and memory routes | freeze source-backed synthesis bundles after validation | escalate on knowledge conflict | research_context_packet, generation_packet |
| Agastya | compression, disciplined reduction, bounded packaging | compression-safe enrichment | compressed context to downstream routes | lock compressed packet lineage after handoff | escalate when compression risks meaning loss | context_compression_packet |
| Parashara | model wisdom, deep pattern reading, training insight | pattern and training-context enrichment | learning and optimization packets to analytics/evolution | lock training lineage after acceptance | escalate on weak evidence or pattern drift | analytics_feedback_packet |
| Ganesha | route shaping, obstacle removal, skill selection | routing-context enrichment | intent to route and route to skill-chain handoff | freeze route decisions for execution window | escalate when no lawful route exists | intent_packet, qualification_packet |
| Tumburu | sonic framing, music/sound prompt shaping | audio aesthetic enrichment | audio planning packets to later audio routes | lock sound intent after packaging approval | escalate on mismatch with script/context | downstream_resource_packet |
| Arjuna | tactical execution framing, precision shot/action intent | execution precision enrichment | tactical packets to production/editing paths | freeze execution packet on approved shot/sequence plan | escalate when precision conflicts with feasibility | scene_prompt_packet, video_prompt_packet |
| Maya | visual illusion, style and fantasy context | visual style enrichment | visual concept packets to scene/image/video prompt layers | freeze approved style bible for continuity window | escalate on style drift or brand conflict | image_prompt_packet, scene_prompt_packet |
| Vishwakarma | build architecture, avatar/render system framing | technical production enrichment | production packets to provider or hybrid execution surfaces | freeze provider-ready packets once validated | escalate on provider incompatibility or infrastructure constraint | provider_request_packet, video_prompt_packet |
| Agni | editing fire, retention shaping, hook intensity | edit and pacing enrichment | edit planning packets to assembly paths | freeze approved pacing plan before render | escalate on retention-risk conflict | downstream_resource_packet, video_prompt_packet |

| Nataraja | motion rhythm, emotion movement, performance timing | motion and animation enrichment | motion packets to later sync/video routes | freeze motion cadence after sync approval | escalate on motion-quality mismatch | video_prompt_packet |

| Yama | policy legality, semantic gate, non-overridable refusal context | policy and safety enrichment | legal/not-legal packet handoff to route or rejection surface | freeze illegal packet verdicts until audited reopen | escalate automatically on policy conflict | validation_packet, approval_packet |
| Aruna | flow building, stage order, handoff packaging | handoff and workflow packaging enrichment | any phase-1 packet to next stage child-workflow | freeze stage boundary packets during active run | escalate on broken handoff or missing packet field | intent_packet, research_context_packet, qualification_packet, generation_packet, validation_packet, downstream_resource_packet |


======================================================================
MICRO_AGENT_CONTEXT_ENGINEERING_EXTENSION
Truth_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + MACHINE-CONTRACT CLOSURE
======================================================================
Why_this_pack_is_needed = Micro-agent contracts already exist as bounded helper executors, but their Context Engineering fields remained implicit.
Micro_Agent_Added_Fields =
- context_packet_ref
- parent_context_scope
- context_compression_rule
- context_return_schema
- context_replay_policy
- context_drop_conditions
- context_redaction_rule
- context_mutation_rights
- context_handoff_target
Micro_Agent_Context_Law =
- a Micro Agent MUST inherit route lane, policy tier, budget tier, and validation floor from the parent skill or director
- a Micro Agent MUST NOT widen its context scope
- a Micro Agent MUST return structured output only
- a Micro Agent MUST drop context fragments that violate redaction or scope boundary
- a Micro Agent MUST surface failure as packetized result, not hidden console text
Micro_Agent_Context_Row_Template =
| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| context_packet_ref | canonical source packet for the helper task |
| parent_context_scope | bounded parent execution scope inherited by micro-agent |
| context_compression_rule | rule for shrinking or preserving packet size |
| context_return_schema | exact packet/object returned to parent |
| context_replay_policy | replayable, replay-with-review, or non-replayable |
| context_drop_conditions | conditions that force discard instead of handoff |
| context_redaction_rule | fields removed before external or sibling movement |
| context_mutation_rights | read_only or parent_scoped_patch_only |
| context_handoff_target | exact parent node or packet destination |


======================================================================
PLUGIN_AND_CONNECTOR_CONTEXT_ENGINEERING_MANIFEST_EXTENSION
Truth_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + INTEGRATION-EDGE CLOSURE
======================================================================
Why_this_pack_is_needed = Plugin and connector manifests already cover permissions, route bindings, sandbox law, and IO surfaces, but not explicit context-packet discipline.
Added_Manifest_Fields =
- context_inputs
- context_packet_family_supported
- context_redaction_rule
- context_export_scope
- provider_context_boundary
- connector_context_shape
- callback_context_rebind_rule
- context_failure_surface
Plugin_and_Connector_Context_Law =
- no plugin or connector may receive the entire dossier by default
- every plugin and connector MUST declare exact context_inputs and exact context_export_scope
- every provider-bound callback MUST declare callback_context_rebind_rule so async return traffic can be rebound to the right context packet and dossier patch
- external integrations MUST obey redaction before transport
Manifest_Row_Template =
| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| context_inputs | exact packet fields allowed into the integration |
| context_packet_family_supported | packet family families the integration can consume |
| context_redaction_rule | fields removed before export |
| context_export_scope | minimal export boundary |
| provider_context_boundary | exact provider-visible envelope |
| connector_context_shape | schema family for inbound/outbound integration data |
| callback_context_rebind_rule | how async return is remapped to context_packet_ref and dossier patch |
| context_failure_surface | structured error packet or retry class |
Historical_Prompt_Translator_Note = Source corpus preserves a dedicated prompt translation need for downstream image/video/provider dialects. Plugin and connector manifests MUST therefore support prompt-object packets as first-class context families rather than raw string prompts only.


======================================================================
SCENE_GENERATION_PROMPT_OBJECT_CONTRACT
Truth_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + PHASE-1 PACKETIZATION CLOSURE
======================================================================
Why_this_pack_is_needed = Source corpus preserves scene builder roles, prompt translator roles, visual prompt specialist roles, and downstream resource planning, but v30 stopped at planning-level outputs instead of a first-class scene prompt packet.
Object_Name = scene_prompt_packet
Execution_Posture = PHASE_1_PACKETIZATION_READY | DEFERRED_EXECUTION_ALLOWED
Purpose = convert validated final script context into structured scene-generation packets that can be reviewed, stored, translated, and later executed by open or provider-bound visual systems.
Required_Fields =
- scene_packet_id
- parent_generation_packet_ref
- parent_validation_packet_ref
- scene_index
- scene_objective
- script_span_ref
- narrative_function
- continuity_context
- location_context
- character_context
- shot_type
- framing_instructions
- camera_intent
- lighting_intent
- mood_intent
- visual_constraints
- brand_constraints
- safety_constraints
- asset_need_class
- prompt_core
- model_neutral_prompt
- negative_constraints
- validation_checks
- fallback_path
- deferred_execution_target
- next_handoff_target
Validation_Law =
- every scene_prompt_packet MUST reference validated script span
- every scene_prompt_packet MUST declare continuity_context and visual_constraints
- every scene_prompt_packet MUST be model-neutral first and provider-specific second
- every scene_prompt_packet MUST remain usable even when downstream image/video execution is deferred
Fallback_Path_Examples =
- simplify to storyboard sentence pack
- reduce to shot list only
- route to manual creative review
- route to prompt translator skill later
Output_Contract = scene_prompt_packet_schema.json compliant packet

- model_neutral_prompt

- preferred_execution_class

- review_status
- validation_checks

- every image_prompt_packet MUST declare style and safety boundaries explicitly
- every image_prompt_packet MUST support deferred execution without losing lineage
Output_Contract = image_prompt_packet_schema.json compliant packet

======================================================================
NO_API_KEY_PHASE1_MODULE_CLOSURE_MATRIX
Truth_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + HONEST OPEN-FIRST CLOSURE
======================================================================
Why_this_pack_is_needed = Source corpus and preserved open-first laws require explicit no-key truth per immediate module instead of rhetorical open-first posture.
Interpretation_Rule =
- YES = can run without API keys using local/self-hosted/open-first components, subject to builder restriction
- YES_CONDITIONAL = no key path exists, but requires local model/runtime/tooling not guaranteed by PRD alone
- PARTIAL = packetization or rule-based subset can run without keys, but quality or breadth drops materially
- NO = immediate module still depends on external/provider access
| Module | Intended_Phase | No_API_Key_YN | Exact_Local_Open_First_Implementation_Class | External_Dependency_Needed_YN | Degraded_Path | Output_Artifact | Confidence | Builder_Restriction |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Topic Hunt | Phase 1 | YES_CONDITIONAL | n8n workflow using local RSS/html scraping, local file ingest, manual source drops, rule-based source normalizer | No | curated-source mode only; narrower breadth | research_context_packet seed list | Medium | internet scraping quality and legality must be controlled; broad discovery parity NOT_PROVEN_OPEN_FIRST |
| Topic Qualification | Phase 1 | YES | n8n scoring workflow with rule engine, spreadsheet/JSON scoring, local validator nodes | No | manual weighting review if heuristic confidence low | qualification_packet | High | thresholds must be declared in workflow config |
| Topic Scoring | Phase 1 | YES | n8n branch/merge scoring pipeline + local formulas + heuristic nodes | No | founder/creator manual score override review | qualification_packet with score ledger | High | score models remain heuristic unless local ML added |
| Research Synthesis | Phase 1 | YES_CONDITIONAL | n8n synthesis workflow using local summarizer/ollama-compatible LLM or deterministic chunk merge rules | No | extractive summary + manual review | research_context_packet | Medium | generative synthesis quality depends on local model availability |
| Script Debate | Phase 1 | YES_CONDITIONAL | n8n parallel child workflows using local LLM debate or rule-based adversarial rubric packets | No | rubric-only debate without deep generative challenge | debate_packet | Medium | meaningful multi-perspective debate quality BENCHMARK_REQUIRED_BEFORE_RELEASE |
| Script Generation | Phase 1 | YES_CONDITIONAL | n8n generation workflow using local LLM runtime via local HTTP or ollama-compatible node | No | outline-only or templated draft mode | generation_packet / script_draft_packet | Medium | acceptable quality depends on local model strength and hardware |
| Script Analysis | Phase 1 | YES | n8n validator workflow with heuristics, checklist scoring, schema checks, and optional local model critique | No | heuristics-only analysis | validation_packet | High | advanced style critique may degrade without local LLM |
| Script Validation | Phase 1 | YES | n8n rule gates, schema validation, scoring thresholds, conflict checks | No | attended approval queue if gates inconclusive | validation_packet / approval_packet | High | validation rubric must be encoded explicitly |
| Final Script Shaping | Phase 1 | YES_CONDITIONAL | n8n merge/rewrite workflow using local LLM or deterministic templating merge | No | manual editor pass on top of validated draft | final_script_packet | Medium | rewrite fluency depends on local model availability |
| Context Packetization | Phase 1 | YES | n8n structured transform + schema pack + dossier patch workflow | No | stricter schema-only packetization | generation_packet, validation_packet, downstream_resource_packet | High | packet schemas must be frozen before scaling |
| Scene Prompt Packetization | Phase 1 | YES | n8n packet builder from final_script_packet + scene rules + style bible + local templates | No | shot-list-only packet mode | scene_prompt_packet | High | this is packetization only, not image/video execution |
| Resource Preparation Planning | Phase 1 | YES | n8n planning workflow using rule engine + dependency classifier + packet outputs | No | manual asset checklist mode | downstream_resource_packet / resource_plan_packet | High | execution surfaces remain deferred where provider-bound |
No_Key_Closure_Law =
- This matrix closes packetization and planning truth, not premium realism or live platform publish truth.
- Actual image generation, video generation, voice synthesis, avatar rendering, and unattended publish remain later-phase or provider-bound where already declared elsewhere.
- If a builder lacks a local LLM runtime, modules marked YES_CONDITIONAL degrade to template/rubric/manual-review mode rather than pretending parity.


======================================================================
N8N_OWNERSHIP_AND_EXECUTION_BOUNDARY_MATRIX_PATCH
Truth_Tag = SOURCE_PROVEN_STRUCTURAL + PHASE-1 ESTATE EXPANSION
======================================================================
Why_this_pack_is_needed = v30 mapped major classes but not deeper packet-object and helper-estate classes required for actual build decomposition.
| Runtime_Class | Sovereign_Owner | n8n_Shape | Direct_Durable_Write | Context_Packet_Required | Approval_Surface | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Sub Agent | parent agent or parent skill owner | child workflow / reusable sub-workflow / bounded code node | No direct durable write; patch-only if allowed | Yes | inherited from parent route | helper execution persona only |
| SubSkill | parent skill owner director | child workflow / reusable node cluster / transform shard | patch-only under parent skill scope | Yes | inherited from parent skill gate | fine-grained skill reuse layer |
| Scene Prompt Object | parent generation/visual route owner | structured packet object | patch-only packet write | Yes | review or automatic validation gate | phase-1 packetization, deferred execution allowed |
| Image Prompt Object | parent scene/visual route owner | structured packet object | patch-only packet write | Yes | review gate before execution translation | later execution, phase-1 packetization |
| Video Prompt Object | parent production/visual route owner | structured packet object | patch-only packet write | Yes | review gate before later execution | later execution, phase-1 packetization |
| Context Compression Worker | Agastya / parent route owner | child workflow or code node | patch-only compressed packet write | Yes | validation gate | cannot discard required lineage |
| Approval/Remodify Packet | approving director or queue owner | structured packet + wait/resume node | patch-only decision write | Yes | explicit human/director approval | must preserve remodify reason lineage |

CONTEXT_ENGINEERING_UTILITY_CANONICAL_REINTERPRETATION_NOTE
Truth_Tag = CANONICAL OPERATOR-SURFACE INTERPRETATION
======================================================================
Historical_Preservation_Rule = Any inherited reference to Prompt Engineer utility remains visible under zero-loss law.
Operational_Reinterpretation = The historically preserved Prompt Engineer utility shall now be interpreted as the Context Engineering utility surface for current implementation.
Practical_Surface_Meaning = The operator surface is no longer only prompt normalization. It is the bounded utility that creates, validates, edits, compares, compresses, redacts, and hands off context packets and prompt-object packets.
Minimum_Surface_Functions =
- packet family selector
- prompt-to-context object upgrader
- dossier read/write preview
- retrieval policy selector
- redaction rule selector
- validation gate preview
- handoff target selector
- scene/image/video prompt-object formatter
Builder_Instruction = preserve old label where inherited; use Context Engineering utility as the active implementation name in dashboards, APIs, forms, and build artifacts.

ZERO_LOSS_APPEND_ONLY_UPGRADE_AUTHORITY
Truth_Tag = APPEND_ONLY_V32_PATCH_AUTHORITY
======================================================================
Canonical_Baseline = Detailed_PRD_MASTERPIECE_v31_APPEND_ONLY_PATCHED_FOR_N8N.txt
Patch_Mode = APPEND_ONLY_ONLY
Deletion_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Replacement_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Historical_Text_Normalization_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Inherited_Table_Collapse_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Patch_Intent =
- extend the current v31 baseline so it becomes materially ready for downstream n8n-first build-artifact generation
- preserve every inherited word and all earlier patch packs
- harden the PRD for self-hosted n8n + local/open-source first + later premium/provider-bound integration
- stop unresolved implementation defaults from drifting back into questionnaire loops
Baseline_Continuity_Note = v31 already contains the canonical Prompt Engineering → Context Engineering interpretation rule, n8n execution primitives, packet-object contracts, no-API-key module closure matrix, and extended handoff artifacts; this patch does not rewrite those closures and instead adds deployment, artifact-readiness, storage, workflow-generation, and scaffold-boundary closure on top of them.
Builder_Execution_Law =
- treat the current same-project chat as a valid context base for implementation defaults already locked by the founder
- do not re-open settled defaults unless a true machine-blocking contradiction appears
- where exact live-environment values are not yet known, preserve scaffold-only decision packets instead of inventing fake finality
Locked_Defaults_For_This_Patch =
- n8n_version_posture = latest local npm install
- phase_1_reasoning_model_primary = qwen3:4b
- phase_1_reasoning_model_fallback = llama3.2:3b if laptop performance is slow
- workflow_packaging_order = node-by-node docs first, workflow JSON drafts later
- node_family_posture = generic n8n nodes first
- approved_node_family = Manual Trigger/Webhook; Set/Code; HTTP Request; IF/Switch/Merge; Wait; Data Tables; Read/Write Files; Execute Sub-workflow
- windows_root_path = C:\ShadowEmpire\...
- storage_posture = Data Tables for indexes/state + JSON/Markdown files for large artifacts
- runtime_posture = single-instance local n8n first
- queue_mode_posture = disabled on day 1
- architecture_posture = n8n-first phase 1 + local/open-source first + premium/provider/API later + packet-first + Context Engineering everywhere + heavy avatar/voice/render/publish/analytics preserved but deferred

======================================================================
N8N_FIRST_DEPLOYMENT_AUTHORITY_HEADER
Truth_Tag = PHASE_1_EXECUTION_AUTHORITY
======================================================================
Deployment_Direction =
- n8n is the first orchestration surface for the Shadow Empire phase-1 machine
- n8n owns the workflow graph, routing, packetization, approvals, retries, remodify loops, state transitions, and audit visibility
- n8n does not replace the reasoning engine, vector layer, media runtime, or provider callback estate
Phase_1_Mainline =
- topic intake
- topic hunt
- topic discovery
- topic qualification
- topic scoring
- research synthesis
- script debate
- script generation
- script analysis
- script validation
- script quality review
- final script shaping
- final script to Context Engineering packet generation
- dossier/state updates
- approval/remodify routing
- retry/replay/escalation/audit flow
- resource preparation planning
- scene prompt packetization
- image prompt packetization
- video prompt packetization
- later-phase queue-record creation for deferred premium modules
Phase_1_Restriction =
- no claim is made here that n8n itself is the reasoning engine or the media engine
- no claim is made here that phase 1 completes heavy avatar, voice, lip-sync, full cinematic editing, unattended publish, or analytics pull
- packetization readiness must not be misrepresented as heavy-media execution completion
n8n_Primitive_Authority =
- parent orchestration = main workflow
- bounded execution = child workflows / Execute Sub-workflow
- trigger surfaces = Manual Trigger, Webhook, Schedule when needed
- transformation = Set / Code
- conditional logic = IF / Switch / Merge
- external/local calls = HTTP Request
- persistence = Data Tables + local JSON/Markdown file writes
- HITL pause = Wait
- governance stop = explicit fail/reject path + approval/remodify branch
Interpretation_Continuity = this section extends the existing v31 n8n execution primitive law and phase-1 packetization law rather than replacing them.

======================================================================
OPEN_SOURCE_FIRST_PHASE1_IMPLEMENTATION_AUTHORITY
Truth_Tag = OPEN_FIRST_EXECUTION_POSTURE
======================================================================
Core_Law =
- phase 1 must prefer self-hosted n8n + local/open-source helpers before paid APIs
- paid or premium modules remain allowed later, but they must not block the first runnable slice
Local_Open_Source_First_Means =
- local n8n instance on laptop/desktop
- local Ollama-compatible reasoning service
- Data Tables and local file-backed artifacts
- local scripts or local helper services only where actually needed
- no mandatory premium n8n cloud dependency
- no mandatory paid media provider in phase 1
Open_First_Integrity_Rule =
- if a no-key or local path is only conditionally true, mark it conditionally true
- if quality, speed, or breadth depend on local hardware or model choice, preserve the restriction explicitly
- do not label a module "fully local" when only packetization or deterministic governance is local
Registry_First_Builder_Note = schema, registry, validator, route map, and policy table remain the anti-drift implementation spine and must outrank prose-only interpretations in downstream build waves.

======================================================================
PROMPT_ENGINEERING_TO_CONTEXT_ENGINEERING_INTERPRETATION_RULE_v32_EXTENSION
Truth_Tag = CANONICAL_INTERPRETATION_EXTENSION
======================================================================
Historical_Preservation_Rule = inherited Prompt Engineering wording remains visible exactly as preserved.
Operational_Extension =
- all future build artifacts must treat prompt text as one field inside a larger context object
- no serious phase-1 workflow may pass prompt text without declaring the governing context contract when the task materially depends on dossier, route, validation, fallback, or downstream handoff state
Minimum_Context_Object_Fields =
- context_packet_id
- context_family
- dossier_ref
- route_ref
- task_ref
- context_inputs
- dossier_reads
- dossier_writes
- retrieval_policy
- validation_gate
- fallback_policy
- next_handoff_target
- review_state
- audit_stamp
Downstream_Prompt_Object_Law =
- scene/image/video prompt objects are prompt-bearing context packets, not free-floating strings
- local model wrappers, debate prompts, validation prompts, and packet translators all inherit the same packet discipline

======================================================================
CONTEXT_ENGINEERING_CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_HEADER
Truth_Tag = SYSTEM_WIDE_CONTEXT_GOVERNANCE
======================================================================
Canonical_Definition =
Context Engineering is the governing operational layer that packages prompt specification, dossier shaping, retrieval binding, route context, execution framing, dependency context, validation context, handoff-safe packet structures, and downstream resource-conditioning objects into bounded, versionable machine contracts.
System_Purpose =
- prevent drift
- prevent raw-string handoffs
- preserve lineage
- make every serious transition auditable
- support modular n8n sub-workflows without losing global machine coherence
Compression_Law =
- Context Engineering must reduce ambiguity without creating payload bloat
- only the minimum bounded context necessary for the next consumer may be handed off
Patch_Only_Law =
- all context-bearing packet transitions must be patch-only writes against the dossier and packet store
- no blind overwrite of inherited packet families is allowed

======================================================================
CONTEXT_ENGINEERING_INJECTION_ACROSS_ALL_RELEVANT_CLASSES_REGISTER
Truth_Tag = ROW_LEVEL_CONTEXT_COMPLETION
======================================================================
| System_Class | Mandatory_Context_Contract | Required_Context_Output | Phase_1_Posture |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Directors | decision_scope, dossier_slice, budget/policy state, review history, escalation authority | decision_packet, approval_packet, remodify_packet, escalation_packet | ACTIVE |
| Agents | execution_goal, bounded data slice, allowed tools, retry law, next node | structured_result_packet | ACTIVE |
| Skills | typed inputs, validation rule, expected output schema, fallback rule | typed_output + dossier_delta | ACTIVE |
| Micro Agents | parent_context_scope, context_packet_ref, TTL, return schema, mutation rights | micro_result_packet | ACTIVE |
| Sub Agents | parent_workflow_ref, bounded mandate, context inheritance rule, review gate | subagent_result_packet | ACTIVE |
| Sub Skills | parent_skill_ref, skill-class inheritance, local validation gate | subskill_result_packet | ACTIVE |
| Tasks | task_id, route_id, dossier_id, deadline profile, dependency state | task_state_packet | ACTIVE |
| Plugins | credentials scope, request envelope, context redaction rule, provider boundary | normalized_tool_packet | ACTIVE |
| Connectors | ingress/egress mapping, idempotency key, callback rebind rule | connector_event_packet | ACTIVE |
| Workflows | parent objective, child workflow targets, replay policy, failure path | workflow_completion_packet | ACTIVE |
| n8n Nodes | mapped input object, execution_id, dossier_ref, output schema | node_output_item + audit_tag | ACTIVE |
| Dossier Handoffs | prior_version_ref, namespace ownership, mutation scope | dossier_patch_delta | ACTIVE |
| Debate Flows | claim packet, challenge packet, rubric, timeout rule | debate_resolution_packet | ACTIVE |
| Script Flows | research brief, evidence notes, pacing targets, style constraints | draft_script_packet or final_script_packet | ACTIVE |
| Analysis Flows | script packet, critique rubric, validation targets | analysis_packet | ACTIVE |
| Validation Flows | validation rubric, threshold set, fail law, escalation owner | validation_packet or approval_packet | ACTIVE |
| Resource-Prep Flows | final script, downstream consumers, provider boundary, asset need map | voice/visual/edit/metadata plan packets | ACTIVE |
| Scene Prompt Objects | validated script span, continuity context, visual constraints | scene_prompt_packet | ACTIVE_PACKET_ONLY |
| Image Prompt Objects | source scene packet, style rules, safety rules, lineage ref | image_prompt_packet | ACTIVE_PACKET_ONLY |
| Video Prompt Objects | source scene refs, motion rules, pacing, execution target class | video_prompt_packet | ACTIVE_PACKET_ONLY |
| Approval/Remodify Packets | review owner, decision state, remodify cause, replay instructions | approval_or_remodify_packet | ACTIVE |
| Local Model Wrappers | model target, prompt role, packet contract, failure handling | local_model_request_packet + response_packet | ACTIVE |
| Repository/Handoff Layer | workflow_id, artifact path, schema version, validator status | handoff_manifest | ACTIVE |
Row_Level_Closure_Law =
- no class above may remain philosophy-only when a row-level machine contract is required
- all downstream artifact generators must materialize the corresponding schema or registry before runtime claims escalate

======================================================================
INDUSTRY_GRADE_BEST_PRACTICES_INJECTION_REGISTER
Truth_Tag = TASK_AWARE_BEST_PRACTICE_CLOSURE
======================================================================
General_Law =
- best practices must attach to the task class they govern
- generic "use best practices" prose is forbidden as a substitute for row-level task law
Task_Aware_Injection =
- Topic_Intake = idempotent intake, source normalization, explicit origin tagging, dedupe before scoring
- Topic_Hunt_and_Discovery = source whitelist, legality-aware acquisition, provenance fields, bounded scrape depth, curated fallback mode
- Qualification_and_Scoring = explicit rubric versioning, weighted scoring transparency, founder-review override packet instead of hidden override
- Research_Synthesis = provenance-preserving chunking, extractive fallback, model output bounded by evidence references
- Script_Debate = adversarial pair or rubric-driven multi-pass review, disagreement capture, unresolved-risk ledger
- Script_Generation = sectioned generation, style constraints explicit, hook/body/CTA separation, draft versioning
- Script_Analysis = rubric-driven critique, schema check first, subjective critique second
- Script_Validation = pass/fail thresholds explicit, unresolved-risk preservation, no silent auto-pass
- Final_Script_Shaping = merge lineage preserved, rewrite diffs explicit, final approval gate retained
- Context_Packetization = packet family versioning, schema validation before write, downstream handoff target always declared
- Scene_Image_Video_Prompt_Packetization = model-neutral prompt core first, provider-specific translation second, continuity and safety constraints mandatory
- Dossier_Mutations = patch-only writes, namespace ownership, optimistic version check, audit stamp
- Approval_Remodify = wait/resume law, remodify reason lineage, replay instructions, human/director authority preserved
- Retry_Replay_Escalation = bounded retry counts, reason-coded failure classes, escalation owner declared, DLQ-style later-phase queue records allowed
- Data_Tables = indexes/state only, no large binary/blob storage, schema columns frozen per phase
- File_Storage = JSON/Markdown for large artifacts, deterministic folder law, no silent overwrite, version-suffixed writes
- Local_Model_Use = explicit model routing rule, prompt wrapper contract, timeout/fallback path, performance benchmarking before production promises
- External_Boundaries = provider-specific values must remain scaffold-only until live environment truth exists
Hardware_Reality_Note = older source corpus preserves average-PC compatibility in the 8–16 GB RAM class and hardware-protection strategies such as model quantization, lazy loading, proxy rendering, and frame tiling; phase-1 n8n-first posture must inherit that realism instead of assuming server-grade local execution.

======================================================================
N8N_FREE_SELF_HOSTED_CAPABILITY_AND_ELIGIBILITY_MATRIX
Truth_Tag = HONEST_SELF_HOSTED_N8N_CLASSIFICATION
======================================================================
| Capability_Class | Supported_In_Free_Self_Hosted_n8n_YN | Phase_1_Use | Boundary_Truth |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Workflow orchestration | YES | parent/child workflow control | core n8n strength |
| Manual/webhook/scheduled triggers | YES | intake and routing | core n8n strength |
| Set/Code transforms | YES | normalization, scoring, packet shaping | core n8n strength |
| HTTP requests | YES | local model calls, external research calls, future provider calls | orchestration surface only |
| IF/Switch/Merge routing | YES | scoring gates, debate branching, approval routing | core n8n strength |
| Wait/Resume | YES | approval, remodify, callback-safe pause, retry delay | core n8n strength |
| Data Tables | YES | indexes/state/lookup tables | use for moderate structured state, not large artifacts |
| Read/Write Files | YES in self-hosted posture | dossier/packet/script JSON and Markdown writes | local path discipline required |
| Execute Sub-workflow | YES | director/agent/skill decomposition | preferred modularization surface |
| Forms / simple HITL | YES if used | approval/remodify intake | bounded HITL only |
| Native reasoning engine | NO | none | n8n is not the reasoning engine |
| Native local LLM inference | NO | none | requires local helper runtime |
| Native media rendering engine | NO | none | requires local/open media tools or provider runtime |
| Native publish-platform ownership | NO | later-phase only | platform APIs/oauth required |
| Enterprise-grade callback truth by itself | NO | later-phase only | provider-specific live details required |
Eligibility_Law =
- a phase-1 module may be marked self-hosted-n8n-eligible only when orchestration plus deterministic transformation is enough
- any module whose core value depends on reasoning, media generation, or external platform ownership must be classified as n8n + local helper, external, or later-phase

======================================================================
NO_API_KEY_PHASE1_FEASIBILITY_MATRIX
Truth_Tag = HONEST_NO_KEY_CLASSIFICATION_EXTENSION
======================================================================
| Phase_1_Module | n8n_Solo | n8n_Plus_Local_Open_Source | External_API_Needed | Later_Phase_Only | Why |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Topic Hunt | NO | YES_CONDITIONAL | NO | NO | needs local source collection / scraping / RSS or manual source drops |
| Topic Discovery | NO | YES_CONDITIONAL | NO | NO | needs external content sources or curated local feeds, but not necessarily paid APIs |
| Topic Qualification | YES | YES | NO | NO | rules-first scoring works locally |
| Topic Scoring | YES | YES | NO | NO | deterministic weighted scoring works locally |
| Research Synthesis | NO | YES_CONDITIONAL | NO | NO | synthesis needs local model or extractive fallback |
| Script Debate | NO | YES_CONDITIONAL | NO | NO | quality debate needs local model or rubric-only degraded mode |
| Script Generation | NO | YES_CONDITIONAL | NO | NO | meaningful generation needs local model |
| Script Analysis | PARTIAL | YES | NO | NO | rule checks solo; semantic critique improved by local model |
| Script Validation | YES | YES | NO | NO | rubric + thresholds fully local |
| Script Quality Review | PARTIAL | YES | NO | NO | richer quality review improved by local model |
| Final Script Shaping | PARTIAL | YES_CONDITIONAL | NO | NO | final fluency benefits from local model |
| Context Packetization | YES | YES | NO | NO | deterministic packet building |
| Scene Prompt Packetization | YES | YES | NO | NO | packetization only, not generation |
| Image Prompt Packetization | YES | YES | NO | NO | packetization only, not generation |
| Video Prompt Packetization | YES | YES | NO | NO | packetization only, not generation |
| Resource Preparation Planning | YES | YES | NO | NO | rules-first planning works locally |
| Dossier/State Updates | YES | YES | NO | NO | local state discipline only |
| Approval/Remodify | YES | YES | NO | NO | n8n wait/resume + local state |
No_Key_Honesty_Law =
- YES_CONDITIONAL means the no-key path exists but depends on local runtime quality, performance, or legal source access
- PARTIAL means a subset is local, but quality or breadth drops materially without local model help
Continuity_Note = this section extends the earlier v31 no-key closure matrix into direct artifact-generation guidance rather than replacing it.

======================================================================
N8N_SOLO_VS_N8N_PLUS_LOCAL_OPEN_SOURCE_VS_EXTERNAL_MATRIX
Truth_Tag = IMPLEMENTATION_BOUNDARY_NORMALIZATION
======================================================================
| Module_Family | n8n_Solo | n8n_Plus_Local_Open_Source | External_API | Later_Hybrid |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Intake / Dossier shell | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | NO |
| Topic qualification / scoring | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | NO |
| Research synthesis | NO | PRIMARY | OPTIONAL | NO |
| Script debate / generation / analysis | NO | PRIMARY | OPTIONAL | NO |
| Validation / approval / remodify | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | NO |
| Context packetization | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | NO |
| Scene/Image/Video prompt packetization | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | NO |
| Resource planning | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | NO |
| Voice generation execution | NO | NO | OPTIONAL_OR_LOCAL_TOOL_LATER | YES |
| Avatar generation execution | NO | NO | PROVIDER_OR_HEAVY_LOCAL_LATER | YES |
| Heavy render/edit execution | NO | NO | OPTIONAL_OR_HEAVY_LOCAL_LATER | YES |
| Publish platform execution | NO | NO | YES | YES |
| Analytics pull / learning loop execution | NO | NO | YES | YES |
Builder_Interpretation =
- n8n solo owns orchestration and deterministic transformation
- n8n + local/open-source owns local reasoning and helper execution
- external APIs own provider/platform-bound execution
- later hybrid owns heavy media, publish, and analytics loops not required for day-1 phase 1

======================================================================
LOCAL_DEPLOYMENT_AND_RUNTIME_PREPARATION_PACK
Truth_Tag = LAPTOP_FIRST_RUNTIME_LOCK
======================================================================
Deployment_Posture =
- laptop-first
- Windows-first for the immediate local operator surface
- self-hosted n8n first
- single-instance first
- no queue mode on day 1
- local/open-source helper posture before premium escalation
Implementation_Default_Lock =
- n8n install posture = latest local npm install
- local model posture = qwen3:4b primary; llama3.2:3b fallback when performance is slow
- path posture = C:\ShadowEmpire\...
- storage posture = Data Tables for indexes/state + JSON/Markdown files for large artifacts
- workflow packaging posture = node-by-node docs first; workflow JSON drafts later
- node posture = generic n8n nodes first
Laptop_Reality_Law =
- phase-1 design must remain viable on a 16 GB RAM / 4 CPU / 256 GB SSD machine
- disk-heavy vector stacks, queue clusters, heavy GPU runtimes, or media-first dependencies must not be made prerequisite to starting phase 1
Preserved_Hardware_Reality = older source corpus preserves 8–16 GB machine realism and hardware-protection logic, while the larger consolidated empire stack also preserves Vayu-style hardware observability for later heavy modules.
Root_Folder_Law =
- C:\ShadowEmpire\data\dossiers
- C:\ShadowEmpire\data\packets
- C:\ShadowEmpire\data\scripts
- C:\ShadowEmpire\data\approvals
- C:\ShadowEmpire\logs
- C:\ShadowEmpire\exports
Escalation_Pack_Note =
- Vayu hardware admission control remains preserved for any later local heavy-media or helper escalations
- it must not be made a blocker for the first n8n-only intake/packetization steps
Preserved_Vayu_Guard_Reference = Vayu remains the later heavy-runtime safety standard with explicit PRECHECK, LOCK, MONITOR, CHECKPOINT, and RELEASE semantics in the wider architecture.

======================================================================
DATA_TABLE_AND_FILE_STORAGE_ARCHITECTURE_PACK
Truth_Tag = STORAGE_DISCIPLINE_CLOSURE
======================================================================
Storage_Principle =
- Data Tables are for indexes, routing state, approval state, packet indexes, and lightweight lookups
- JSON/Markdown files are for large artifacts and human-readable payloads
Data_Table_Families =
1. dossiers
- dossier_id
- job_id
- creator_id
- channel_id
- route_id
- selected_topic
- approval_state
- dossier_version
- last_mutation_owner
- updated_at

2. packets
- packet_id
- dossier_id
- packet_type
- file_path
- version
- created_at

3. approvals
- approval_id
- dossier_id
- review_state
- decision
- comment
- updated_at

4. routes
- route_id
- dossier_id
- route_lane
- workflow_id
- result
- updated_at
File_Backup_Families =
- dossier snapshots as JSON
- large packets as JSON
- human-readable scripts as Markdown + JSON metadata
- approval notes as Markdown or JSON
- export packs as versioned folders
Storage_Best_Practice_Law =
- Data Tables must not become a dumping ground for all long artifact text
- file paths must be deterministic and version-aware
- writes must be append-safe or version-safe; no blind overwrite
- packet files must remain schema-valid even when manually inspected later

FIRST_WAVE_WORKFLOW_GENERATION_PACK

Authorized_First_Wave_Workflows =
- WF_00_Intake_Dossier_Create
- WF_01_Topic_Discovery_Qualification_Scoring
- WF_02_Research_Synthesis
- WF_03_Script_Debate_Generation
- WF_04_Script_Analysis_Validation
- WF_05_Final_Script_Shaping
- WF_06_Context_Engineering_Packet_Generation
- WF_07_Approval_Remodify
- WF_08_Resource_Preparation_Planning
Workflow_Generation_Law =
- each workflow must be generated first as a node-by-node implementation sheet before import-ready JSON is treated as canonical
- each workflow must declare input packet families, output packet families, dossier reads/writes, approval gates, replay points, and error branches
Workflow_Decomposition_Rule =
- parent workflow owns route selection, high-level state, and final handoff
- child workflows own bounded tasks only
- one giant empire workflow is prohibited
Immediate_Priority_Order =
1. WF_00_Intake_Dossier_Create
2. WF_01_Topic_Discovery_Qualification_Scoring
3. WF_02_Research_Synthesis
4. WF_03_Script_Debate_Generation
5. WF_04_Script_Analysis_Validation

7. WF_06_Context_Engineering_Packet_Generation
8. WF_07_Approval_Remodify

NODE_BY_NODE_BUILD_SPEC_SUPPORT_PACK
Truth_Tag = NODE_LEVEL_BUILD_READINESS
======================================================================
Node_Level_Families_To_Specify_For_Each_Workflow =
- trigger node
- initial normalization Set/Code nodes
- dossier read node
- packet read/write nodes
- HTTP Request nodes
- IF/Switch scoring gates
- Merge reconcile nodes
- Wait node where approval/resume is needed
- final file write nodes
- final Data Table update nodes
- error branch nodes
Node_By_Node_Law =
- each node spec must declare purpose, inputs, outputs, item shape, failure mode, retry law, and dossier impact
- each HTTP node spec must declare local-helper vs external-provider posture
- each Code node spec must declare whether it is deterministic transform logic or conditional helper logic
- each Wait node spec must declare resume trigger and resume output contract
JSON_Generation_Precursor_Law =
- import-ready JSON drafts may only be produced after the node-by-node spec exists and the target n8n version posture is locked for that draft wave

======================================================================
JSON_IMPORT_READY_WORKFLOW_SUPPORT_PACK
Truth_Tag = JSON_DRAFT_BOUNDARY_CLOSURE
======================================================================
Packaging_Posture =
- workflow JSON support is allowed
- workflow JSON is not the first authoring surface
- semi-importable draft JSON is acceptable before final import-ready polish
JSON_Support_Modes =
- node skeleton JSON
- semi-importable draft JSON
- version-cautioned import-ready JSON
Version_Caution_Law =
- workflow JSON drafts must preserve a version-caution note because import quirks can vary by target n8n version and node shape
- no JSON draft may be treated as the source of truth over the node-by-node workflow spec
Import_Export_Guidance_Requirement =
- every JSON support artifact family must include import/export cautions, credential placeholders, and environment-bound fields marked scaffold-only

======================================================================
OLLAMA_AND_LOCAL_MODEL_INTEGRATION_PACK
Truth_Tag = LOCAL_REASONING_HELPER_AUTHORITY
======================================================================
Local_Model_Role =
- local reasoning helper for synthesis, debate, generation, analysis, validation, and final shaping
- not a replacement for n8n orchestration
Locked_Default_Model_Rule =
- qwen3:4b = primary phase-1 local reasoning model
- llama3.2:3b = fallback when laptop performance is slow
Integration_Surfaces =
- HTTP Request to local model service
- later optional Ollama-specific wrappers
- model-neutral packet wrappers first
Required_Local_Model_Artifacts =
- OLLAMA_HTTP_NODE_STANDARD.md
- OLLAMA_PROMPT_WRAPPERS.yaml
- LOCAL_MODEL_ROUTING_RULES.yaml
- OLLAMA_FAILURE_HANDLING.md
Local_Model_Best_Practices =
- explicit timeout rules
- deterministic packet in / packet out wrappers
- degraded-mode fallback for no model response
- no raw dossier dump into the model without compression/redaction
- benchmark before production-grade promise
Reasoning_Boundary_Law =
- if the local model is unavailable or too weak, the workflow must degrade gracefully to rubric-only, template-only, or extractive-only mode rather than silently failing

======================================================================
GOVERNANCE_SCORING_APPROVAL_AND_VALIDATION_PACK
Truth_Tag = GOVERNANCE_RUNTIME_CLOSURE
======================================================================
Required_Governance_Artifacts =
- topic_scoring_rules.yaml
- script_quality_rubric.yaml
- validation_thresholds.yaml
- approval_decision_matrix.yaml
- retry_remodify_escalation_codes.yaml
- governance_rules_phase1.yaml
Governance_Law =
- topic scoring must be versioned and visible
- script quality review must separate structural pass/fail from stylistic critique
- approval must preserve the approving owner, decision time, decision reason, and remodify instructions
- retries must be bounded and coded by reason class
- escalation owners must be explicit
Approval_Remodify_Runtime =
- approval may return PASS, REMODIFY, ESCALATE, or HOLD
- remodify must emit a reason-coded packet with exact correction targets
- replay must resume from the nearest valid packet checkpoint, not regenerate the entire machine blindly
Wait_And_Resume_Continuity = the patch-only dossier and approval packet law remains aligned with the earlier preserved wait/resume doctrine and must continue from the last approved dossier version where possible.

- intake creates dossier and packet index correctly
- topic scoring path produces deterministic qualification output
- local-model unavailable path degrades safely
- debate failure produces explicit remodify or downgrade packet
- approval pause resumes correctly
- packet files and Data Table indexes stay consistent
- resource planning produces scene/image/video packet families without claiming execution
Handoff_Law =
- every handoff artifact must point back to schema families, workflow IDs, and packet families defined in this hardened PRD
- no handoff artifact may invent new core packet families outside the packet authority pack without an append-only PRD patch

HEAVY_MODULE_LATER_PHASE_BOUNDARY_LEDGER

======================================================================
Preserved_But_Deferred_Families =
- full avatar generation execution
- premium voice synthesis execution
- lip sync runtime
- cinematic FFmpeg/render pipeline
- advanced editing runtime
- publish-platform execution
- analytics pull and learning loop execution
Boundary_Reason =
- these families remain part of the architecture
- they are preserved as later-phase/hybrid/provider-bound modules
- they must not block phase-1 n8n-first implementation
Preserved_Heavy_Spine = older source corpus clearly preserves the avatar/voice/emotion/render/publish/analytics production spine as a separate heavier system; this patch keeps that estate intact while refusing to force it into day-1 n8n-first claims.
Later_Phase_Queue_Record_Law =
- phase 1 may create deferred execution records for heavy modules
- those records must reference the validated upstream packets and later execution target class
- creating the deferred record is not the same as executing the heavy module

SCAFFOLD_ONLY_ENVIRONMENT_BOUNDARY_LEDGER

======================================================================
Scaffold_Only_Until_Real_Env_Truth_Exists =
- exact callback URIs
- exact OAuth values
- exact provider secret rotation details
- exact enterprise budget thresholds
- exact benchmark-backed hardware thresholds
- exact provider SLA commitments
- exact provider-specific webhook verification details
Scaffold_Builder_Rule =
- these fields may be represented as decision packets, placeholder config entries, scaffold schemas, or environment variable templates
- they must not be hallucinated as final in the PRD or downstream artifacts
Safe_Current_Posture =
- provider-bound modules may be packetized and stubbed
- live integration truth remains environment-bound and must be finalized only when the real integration surface is chosen

APPEND_ONLY_VALIDATION_AND_MERGE_PROTOCOL

======================================================================
Merge_Mode = APPEND_TO_V31_BASELINE_ONLY
Validation_Steps =
1. preserve the current v31 file exactly
2. append this v32 patch pack after the existing v31 closure ledger
3. do not edit inherited wording
4. do not rename inherited section headers
5. if an overlapping concept exists, treat this patch as additive implementation closure, not replacement
6. if a future artifact generator needs a concrete schema, generate the schema from the newest additive authority section rather than from older prose
7. if a future environment-specific value is unknown, emit a scaffold artifact or decision packet instead of fake finality
Patch_Success_Criteria =
- no inherited text deleted
- phase-1 n8n-first/open-first/local-first posture materially clearer
- downstream artifact families explicitly authorized
- packet, dossier, workflow, storage, governance, and scaffold boundaries all explicit
Residual_Honesty_Law =
- this patch makes the PRD materially more artifact-generatable and build-ready
- this patch does not claim that the entire full merged PRD should be emitted in one giant response block
- this patch does not claim that later premium/provider-heavy execution families are complete today
- this patch is the safe append-only bridge between the current hardened PRD and the later modular build-artifact waves

APPEND_ONLY_V33_ZERO_LOSS_EXECUTION_AUTHORITY
Truth_Tag = APPEND_ONLY_V33_PATCH_AUTHORITY

Canonical_Baseline = Detailed_PRD_MASTERPIECE_v32_ZERO_LOSS_MERGED.txt
Patch_Mode = APPEND_ONLY_ONLY
Deletion_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Replacement_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Historical_Text_Normalization_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Inherited_Table_Collapse_Prohibition = ABSOLUTE
Patch_Intent =
- extend the current v32 baseline so it becomes materially ready for estate-level n8n deployment rather than only phase-1 workflow-level readiness
- preserve every inherited word and all earlier patch packs exactly
- harden the PRD for self-hosted n8n + local/open-source first + later premium/provider-bound integration
- bind Directors, Skills, Agents, Sub Agents, Sub Skills, Plugins, Connectors, storage planes, and operator surfaces into actual n8n-runtime interpretation
- stop unresolved estate-level deployment defaults from drifting back into questionnaire loops

Baseline_Continuity_Note =
v32 already contains the canonical Prompt Engineering -> Context Engineering interpretation rule, n8n execution primitives, packet-object contracts, no-API-key module closure matrix, free/self-hosted n8n capability matrix, workflow-generation authority, storage discipline, local-model posture, and heavy-module deferral law; this v33 patch does not rewrite those closures and instead adds estate-level binding, state-plane reconciliation, day-1 runtime reconciliation, operator-surface classification, and residual blocker normalization on top of them.

Builder_Execution_Law =
- treat the current same-project chat as a valid context base for implementation defaults already locked by the founder
- do not re-open settled defaults unless a true machine-blocking contradiction appears
- where exact live-environment values are not yet known, preserve scaffold-only decision packets instead of inventing fake finality
- no downstream builder may interpret this patch as authorization to rewrite inherited v32 wording
- this patch may only inject and update the PRD; it may not delete or replace inherited text

Locked_Defaults_For_This_Patch =
- n8n_version_posture = latest local npm install
- phase_1_reasoning_model_primary = qwen3:4b
- phase_1_reasoning_model_fallback = llama3.2:3b if laptop performance is slow
- workflow_packaging_order = node-by-node docs first, workflow JSON drafts later
- node_family_posture = generic n8n nodes first
- approved_node_family = Manual Trigger/Webhook; Set/Code; HTTP Request; IF/Switch/Merge; Wait; Data Tables; Read/Write Files; Execute Sub-workflow
- windows_root_path = C:\ShadowEmpire...
- storage_posture = Data Tables for indexes/state + JSON/Markdown files for large artifacts
- runtime_posture = single-instance local n8n first
- queue_mode_posture = disabled on day 1
- architecture_posture = n8n-first phase 1 + local/open-source first + premium/provider/API later + packet-first + Context Engineering everywhere + heavy avatar/voice/render/publish/analytics preserved but deferred

Patch_Success_Criteria =
- no inherited text deleted
- n8n is materially injected into estate-level runtime interpretation rather than only workflow-level authority
- Directors, Skills, Agents/Sub Agents/Sub Skills, Plugins, Connectors, storage, and operator surfaces all become deployment-readable
- free/self-hosted eligibility becomes estate-readable, not just capability-readable
- day-1 single-instance laptop posture becomes explicit and non-drifting


======================================================================
DIRECTOR_TO_N8N_WORKFLOW_BINDING_REGISTER_v33
Truth_Tag = ESTATE_LEVEL_N8N_DIRECTOR_BINDING
======================================================================

Why_this_pack_is_needed =
v32 preserves Director runtime, cost, jurisdiction, and execution truth, but does not yet bind the Director estate to actual n8n workflow surfaces. This register makes Director rows deployment-readable for the first n8n build wave.

| Director_ID | Director_Name | Primary_Workflow_ID | Child_Workflow_Refs | Input_Packet_Families | Output_Packet_Families | Dossier_Reads | Dossier_Writes | Approval_Gate | Retry_Policy | Data_Table_Touchpoints | File_Write_Surfaces | n8n_Node_Family | Free_Self_Hosted_Eligible_YN | Phase_1_Status | Notes |

Director_Binding_Law =
- every Director that materially participates in phase 1 MUST bind to at least one workflow_id
- every ACTIVE or ACTIVE_PACKET_ONLY Director row MUST declare dossier reads/writes and packet ownership
- no Director may remain architecture-only where workflow ownership is required for phase 1
- preserved later-phase Directors remain intact, but SHALL NOT be misrepresented as phase-1 n8n runtime owners

Why_this_pack_is_needed =
older logical state planes remain preserved, while v32 later authorizes Data Tables and JSON/Markdown files; this register reconciles them for day-1 n8n deployment.

| Logical_State_Plane | Phase_1_Storage_Surface | Data_Table_Family | File_Family | Optional_Local_DB_YN | Writer_Workflow | Reader_Workflows | Mutation_Law | Backup_Rule | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

The inherited PRD contains strong truth but remains historically accreted across many additive waves. This section defines the canonical architecture-zone interpretation so future builders can read the machine in a cleaner order without altering inherited wording.

- inherited blocks MAY be logically reclassified by additive authority even when their original text remains in its historical location
- future downstream exports MAY physically reorder preserved blocks only if every moved block remains byte-preserved and relocation is fully recorded in a relocation ledger
- when in doubt, preserve the original block and classify it into the correct canonical zone rather than rewriting it
- any component that cannot be placed confidently into a primary zone MUST be preserved in a named appendix class and not deleted

   - tools
   - memory
   - guardrails

8. Skills / Sub Skills / Tasks
9. Plugins / Connectors / Tools

12. Governance / validation / approvals / remodify / escalation
13. Local open-source execution layer
14. External / outsourced / premium later layer
15. Handoff / build-artifact readiness
16. Preserved unmapped / legacy / cross-domain appendices

- older historical section names remain preserved exactly
- the current build interpretation must follow the v34 zone model whenever older ordering would confuse the builder

Why_this_pack_is_needed =

| Canonical_Zone | Inherited_Section_Families_or_Truth_Classes | Current_Interpretation_Rule | Relocation_Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
