| Canonical terminology / Context Engineering authority | Prompt Engineering interpretation rules, Context Engineering authority, packet object contracts, context injection registers | govern all prompt-bearing runtime surfaces | RECLASSIFIED |
| Global runtime posture | founder/creator/runtime modes, platform targets, runtime posture, day-1 runtime collapse, queue disable law | governs deployment interpretation before module reading | RECLASSIFIED |
| Harness architecture | newly injected v34 system prompt, tool, memory, guardrail packs + inherited policy/tool/state sections | read as mandatory runtime scaffold across all classes | NEW_CANONICAL_ZONE |
| Modes and execution modes | founder mode, creator mode, local/hybrid/cloud/untrusted_local, no-key truth, self-hosted eligibility | read together under mode-aware harness behavior | RECLASSIFIED |
| Directors | Director Population Register, Director Execution Matrix, Director-to-n8n binding, director decision packets | read as governance / routing / ownership estate | RECLASSIFIED |
| Agents / Sub Agents / Micro Agents | agent estate registers, micro-agent rules, helper bindings, runtime TTL rules | read as bounded helper runtime estate | RECLASSIFIED |
| Skills / Sub Skills / Tasks | skill population, skill runtime, task distribution, subskill bindings | read as execution estate, not as free-floating taxonomy only | RECLASSIFIED |
| Plugins / Connectors / Tools | plugin lifecycle, provider / connector rows, tool surfaces, node families | read as tool/runtime surfaces with explicit harness binding | RECLASSIFIED |
| Workflows / n8n execution | workflow runtime registers, workflow packs, child workflow maps, queue/runtime collapse, operator flow routes | read as canonical execution plane | RECLASSIFIED |
| Dossier / packet / storage / memory | dossier law, packet families, Data Tables, file storage, state-plane reconciliation, audit/replay memory | read as memory and persistence plane | RECLASSIFIED |
| Governance / validation / approvals / escalation | policy gates, budget gates, validation packets, approval/remodify loops, retry ceilings | read as guardrail and governance plane | RECLASSIFIED |
| Local open-source execution layer | n8n-first authority, local model integration, local source acquisition, self-hosted matrices, laptop-first runtime | read as immediate deployment plane | RECLASSIFIED |
| External / outsourced / premium later layer | heavy media ledger, provider stubs, callback/oAuth scaffolds, publish/analytics later execution | read as deferred execution plane | RECLASSIFIED |
| Handoff / build-artifact readiness | artifact readiness registers, runbook/test families, workflow spec packs, registry artifact lists | read as builder export plane | RECLASSIFIED |
| Preserved appendices | unresolved or cross-domain historical material, older platform posture, duplicated but preserved truth | preserve under named appendix classes when primary placement is uncertain | APPENDIX_REQUIRED_WHEN_UNCERTAIN |

Relocation_Law =
- future structured exports may group inherited blocks under the zones above only if the original block text is preserved exactly
- the existence of a relocation ledger does not authorize silent rewriting or omission
- if a block belongs to multiple zones, the primary zone MUST be declared and the secondary dependency noted rather than duplicating and mutating the text


======================================================================
HARNESS_ARCHITECTURE_CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_v34
Truth_Tag = HARNESS_ARCHITECTURE_CORE
======================================================================

Harness_Definition =
The Shadow Empire harness is the runtime-control layer that makes the orchestrator, local helpers, context packets, and later provider bridges behave as one governed machine. It has four non-optional pillars:
- System Prompts
- Tools
- Memory
- Guardrails

Harness_Law =
- no serious phase-1 or later-phase execution may claim completeness without explicit harness treatment
- system prompts without tools, memory, and guardrails are incomplete
- tools without system prompts, memory discipline, and guardrails are dangerous
- memory without prompt discipline and guardrails becomes drift
- guardrails without runtime integration become decorative prose

Harness_Deployment_Posture =
- phase 1 harness is n8n-first and packet-first
- local/open-source helpers are allowed and expected where orchestration alone is insufficient
- later premium/provider modules remain preserved but deferred; they MUST still obey harness law when activated

Harness_Objectives =
- preserve mode-aware execution discipline
- preserve context-safe prompting
- make tool invocation explicit
- stratify memory correctly
- harden validation and approval behavior
- prevent state and prompt drift
- preserve provider-bound stub honesty until live environment truth exists

Harness_Scope =
- Modes
- Founder mode
- Creator mode
- n8n-first phase-1 mode
- local-only / no-key mode
- later premium / hybrid mode
- Directors
- Agents / Sub Agents / Micro Agents
- Skills / Sub Skills / Tasks
- Plugins / Connectors
- Workflows / n8n nodes / child workflows
- Dossier handoffs / packet families / storage and state planes
- Topic / Research / Debate / Script / Validation / Context / Resource-prep workflows
- Scene / image / video prompt packets
- Local model wrappers
- Deferred provider bridges
- Operator/control surfaces


======================================================================
SYSTEM_PROMPT_RUNTIME_LAYER_v34
Truth_Tag = SYSTEM_PROMPT_HARNESS_LAYER
======================================================================

System_Prompt_Law =
- system prompts are a runtime-control layer, not decorative instruction text
- system prompts MUST exist at the correct control granularity: global, mode, class, and workflow/task level
- no downstream builder may reduce the system-prompt layer to a single monolithic text blob when mode or class separation is required

System_Prompt_Tiers =
1. Global_System_Authority
- governs the whole machine
- defines packet-first, Context Engineering, zero-loss, policy, audit, and deployment honesty rules

2. Mode_Aware_System_Instructions
- Founder mode
- Creator mode
- n8n-first phase-1 mode
- local-only / no-key mode
- later premium/hybrid mode

3. Director_Level_System_Instructions
- governance, routing, escalation, decision ownership, approval boundaries, policy/budget legality, packet ownership

4. Agent_SubAgent_MicroAgent_Role_Prompts
- bounded role prompt, scope limit, allowed tools, memory read/write policy, packet IO, retry/degrade rules

5. Skill_Task_Execution_Instructions
- execution-specific task framing, input/output schemas, validation targets, fallback behavior, tool-use constraints

6. Provider_Bridge_System_Stubs
- scaffold-only behavior, no false claims, no fake callback truth, no fake live credentials

Packet_Safe_Prompting_Law =
- any prompt-bearing runtime step MUST receive a bounded packet object or context object, not a free-floating untracked string
- prompts MUST reference packet lineage and validation targets where task-critical
- provider translation prompts MUST derive from model-neutral prompt packets where possible

Fallback_Degrade_Prompt_Law =
- every local-model-dependent prompt path MUST have a fallback prompt behavior
- degraded prompt paths MUST remain packet-safe and audit-visible
- no workflow may silently substitute a weaker prompt path without recording the degrade reason


======================================================================
MODE_AWARE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_REGISTER_v34
Truth_Tag = MODE_PROMPT_BINDING
======================================================================

| Mode | Global_System_Authority | Mode_Aware_System_Instructions | Tool_Use_Constraints | Memory_Read_Write_Policy | Guardrail_Invocation_Policy | Fallback_Degrade_Rule | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Founder_Mode | full governance authority | release, override, cost legality, incident response, approval ownership | full visibility; premium escalation allowed only under preserved policy/budget law | read_all / write_scoped_and_audited | Yama, Kubera, approval gates, release gates always active | hold_or_escalate_not_silent_bypass | founder controls do not bypass non-overridable policy law |
| Creator_Mode | safe production authority | daily content production, review, limited route control | no unsafe premium override; limited route and cost controls | read_creator_scope / write_creator_scope | policy, packet validation, approval/remodify gates active | degrade_to_creator_safe_path | creator mode must not mutate core registry truth |
| n8n_First_Phase_1_Mode | orchestration-first authority | packet-first workflow execution, Data Table and file discipline, child-workflow decomposition | generic n8n nodes first; local helper calls explicit | workflow/session/dossier memory active | schema, packet, retry, approval, no-key honesty all active | local_helper_degrade_or_hold | primary immediate build posture |
| Local_Only_No_Key_Mode | local survivability authority | no-key honesty, bounded discovery, local helper or deterministic fallback only | no premium/provider live calls | local/session/dossier memory only | legality, no-key honesty, packet validation, degrade rules active | rubric_only / extractive_only / hold | no-key does not imply full parity with premium execution |
| Later_Premium_Hybrid_Mode | hybrid escalation authority | deferred provider bridges, callback/resume, premium execution by explicit decision path | external/provider tools allowed only under stub-to-live transition law | packet lineage + provider bridge memory | callback, auth, signature, approval, environment-bound truth rules active | stub_only_until_live_truth | preserved but deferred in current posture |

Mode_Prompt_Law =
- every mode MUST bind to a distinct system-instruction envelope
- no mode may inherit unsafe tool or memory rights implicitly
- mode switching MUST preserve packet lineage and guardrail continuity


======================================================================
DIRECTOR_AGENT_SKILL_TASK_SYSTEM_PROMPT_BINDING_REGISTER_v34
Truth_Tag = CLASS_PROMPT_BINDING
======================================================================

| Runtime_Class | Required_System_Prompt_Content | Tool_Use_Rule | Memory_Rule | Guardrail_Rule | Packet_Rule | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Directors | decision ownership, escalation law, route legality, budget/policy posture, approval logic | only invoke workflow/packet-safe tools allowed to the Director class | read bounded dossier slices; write decision packets and approved deltas only | Yama/Kubera/Vayu/validation gates respected | must emit decision/approval/remodify/escalation packets | no Director prompt may drift into unbounded worker execution |
| Agents | bounded mandate, allowed tools, expected output packet, retry/degrade logic | tool list explicit; no hidden tool use | read bounded packet set; write structured result packets only | packet validation and task guardrails mandatory | packet in / packet out | agent prompts must remain task-bounded |
| Sub Agents | child helper scope, parent ref, context inheritance rule, TTL/task bound | only parent-approved tool surfaces | inherit minimal required memory only | strict error and replay behavior | bounded packet contract | no Sub Agent may become an unbounded general agent |
| Micro Agents | ultra-bounded helper behavior, TTL, fixed output schema | minimal helper tools only | ephemeral workflow memory only | no blind retry; no privilege escalation | micro packet only | micro agents remain utility helpers |
| Skills | input schema, output schema, validator target, fallback mode, dependency notes | only declared tool surface | read only declared input packets and dossier fields | validator, threshold, and failure law mandatory | must obey skill packet IO | skill prompts are execution instructions, not personality text |
| Sub Skills | parent skill ref, bounded operation, structural fallback | tool use inherited and narrowed | minimal inherited memory | same validator family as parent unless declared otherwise | bounded subskill packet IO | structural helpers only |
| Tasks | goal, route id, deadline, dependency, success criteria | no hidden tools | task scope only | task validation and escalation owner mandatory | task packet required | tasks are never free-form prompts |

Class_Prompt_Law =
- every runtime class MUST have a packet-safe, mode-aware system-prompt contract
- task and skill prompting MUST be execution-aware and validator-aware
- Director prompts MUST remain governance/routing oriented and not mutate into generic worker prompts


======================================================================
TOOL_RUNTIME_LAYER_v34
Truth_Tag = TOOL_HARNESS_LAYER
======================================================================

Tool_Law =
- tools are not generic capability labels; they are explicit runtime surfaces with phase, ownership, and guardrail posture
- every tool used in the PRD MUST be classifiable as phase-1 self-hosted now, local-helper now, external/provider later, or premium/provider later
- no runtime class may invoke undeclared tools where packet lineage or guardrail surfaces are required

Tool_Surface_Families =
1. n8n_Native_Tools
- Manual Trigger / Webhook
- Set / Code
- HTTP Request
- IF / Switch / Merge
- Wait
- Data Tables
- Read/Write Files
- Execute Sub-workflow

2. Local_Open_Source_Tools
- local model runtimes
- local source acquisition helpers
- local file system and export helpers
- local validation / transform scripts where later enabled

3. Human_Review_Tools
- approval forms
- wait/resume interfaces
- operator Data Table views
- file export / review bundles

4. Deferred_Provider_Tools
- live provider HTTP surfaces
- callback/webhook verifier tools
- OAuth / secret rotation / signature verification surfaces
- publish and analytics adapters

Tool_Decomposition_Law =
- tool definitions must distinguish orchestration surfaces from reasoning surfaces from media/runtime surfaces
- n8n MUST remain the orchestration surface, not be mislabeled as the reasoning engine
- local models MUST remain reasoning helpers, not be mislabeled as workflow control planes
- provider-bound tools MUST remain deferred or scaffold-only until live truth exists


======================================================================
TOOL_PHASE_AND_BOUNDARY_REGISTER_v34
Truth_Tag = TOOL_BOUNDARY_MATRIX
======================================================================

| Tool_Surface | Category | Phase_1_Now | Local_Helper_Now | External_Later | Premium_Later | Primary_Use | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| n8n core nodes | orchestration | YES | NO | NO | NO | workflow graph, routing, packetization, approvals, state writes | primary workflow spine |
| local model HTTP surface | reasoning helper | NO | YES | NO | NO | synthesis, debate, generation, analysis, shaping | conditional on local model performance |
| local RSS/manual/HTML/file ingest | source acquisition | PARTIAL | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | topic discovery and research intake | bounded and legality-aware only |
| Data Tables | storage/index tool | YES | NO | NO | NO | indexes, state, approvals, routes, packet registry | not long artifact blob store |
| Read/Write Files | storage/artifact tool | YES | NO | NO | NO | dossier snapshots, packets, scripts, exports | deterministic path law applies |
| approval forms / wait surfaces | human review tool | YES | NO | NO | NO | approval, remodify, replay gating | operator/control surface |
| Qdrant_or_other_external_memory | retrieval layer | NO | OPTIONAL_LATER | OPTIONAL_LATER | NO | later semantic memory | not day-1 requirement |
| browser automation helper | acquisition/integration | OPTIONAL | YES | OPTIONAL | NO | later bounded research or provider bridge work | not mainline phase-1 dependency |
| provider HTTP bridge | external/provider tool | NO | NO | YES | YES | later publish / analytics / media / callbacks | scaffold-only until live truth |
| heavy media runtime | media helper | NO | OPTIONAL_LATER | OPTIONAL_LATER | YES | voice/avatar/render execution | preserved but deferred |

Tool_Boundary_Law =
- every tool surface MUST declare which side of the current deployment boundary it lives on
- no tool may be misclassified as phase-1 active if it is only preserved as deferred or scaffold-only


======================================================================
MEMORY_RUNTIME_LAYER_v34
Truth_Tag = MEMORY_HARNESS_LAYER
======================================================================

Memory_Definition =
Memory is the structured runtime layer that preserves bounded context, lineage, state, and replay truth across workflows, dossiers, approvals, packets, routes, and later provider bridges.

Memory_Law =
- memory is not assumed just because some state exists
- memory types MUST be explicitly classified by scope, write owner, retention posture, and replay value
- no workflow may read or write memory implicitly where packet lineage or approval state can be affected

Memory_Tiers =
1. Workflow_Short_Term_Memory
- execution-local item state
- child workflow temporary context
- per-run derived values

2. Session_Memory
- current run or operator session state
- approval and remodify session context
- creator/founder surface interaction state

3. Dossier_Memory
- canonical longitudinal state for a job / creator / channel route
- mutable only via patch-safe updates

4. Packet_Lineage_Memory
- source-to-packet-to-packet lineage
- validation lineage
- translation lineage for scene/image/video packets

5. Route_Memory
- selected lane, retries, degrade reasons, replay checkpoints, deferred execution records

6. Approval_And_Remodify_Memory
- prior decisions, reasons, correction targets, resumption checkpoints

7. Incident_And_Replay_Memory
- failure class, retry count, degrade class, replay target, audit event trail

8. Long_Term_Persistent_Memory
- creator/channel preferences, rubric versions, historical approvals, future analytics seeds, later optional retrieval layers

Memory_Posture =
- local-first in phase 1
- dossier and packet memory are primary
- optional external retrieval memory remains later and non-blocking


======================================================================
MEMORY_CLASSIFICATION_AND_WRITE_AUTHORITY_REGISTER_v34
Truth_Tag = MEMORY_CLASSIFICATION_MATRIX
======================================================================

| Memory_Class | Primary_Surface | Owner_Class | Write_Posture | Read_Posture | Replay_Value | Phase_1_Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Workflow_Short_Term_Memory | in-flight workflow items | workflow / task runtime | ephemeral | workflow-bound | low_to_medium | ACTIVE | short-lived execution values |
| Session_Memory | operator/workflow session | workflow + operator surface | bounded | session-bound | medium | ACTIVE | approval and remodify interaction state |
| Dossier_Memory | dossiers Data Table + dossier snapshot files | Chitragupta / workflow owners | patch-only | broad phase-1 reads by authorized workflows | high | ACTIVE | canonical longitudinal state |
| Packet_Lineage_Memory | packets Data Table + packet files | packet-producing workflow | append-safe + version-safe | downstream workflows and audits | high | ACTIVE | source and lineage truth |
| Route_Memory | routes Data Table + route logs | Ganesha / Aruna / workflow layer | patch-only | route-aware workflows | high | ACTIVE | lane and degrade state |
| Approval_Memory | approvals Data Table + notes | approval workflow / governance owners | append-safe | validation and resumption flows | high | ACTIVE | approval/remodify lineage |
| Incident_And_Replay_Memory | audit logs + checkpoint files | Chitragupta / workflow layer | append-only | replay and RCA surfaces | high | ACTIVE | no blind reset allowed |
| User_Channel_Creator_Memory | dossier/meta plane | founder/builder-controlled workflow surfaces | bounded and audited | creator/founder surfaces and workflow selectors | medium | ACTIVE | preference and ownership memory |
| Long_Term_External_Memory | later optional retrieval layer | later selected memory subsystem | later-defined | later-defined | medium_to_high | DEFERRED | not day-1 mandatory |

Memory_Write_Law =
- every memory class MUST declare an owner and a write posture
- dossier and packet lineage memory SHALL remain patch-safe and audit-visible
- no local model wrapper may dump arbitrary memory back into the dossier without packet validation


======================================================================
GUARDRAIL_RUNTIME_ENFORCEMENT_LAYER_v34
Truth_Tag = GUARDRAIL_HARNESS_LAYER
======================================================================

Guardrail_Definition =
Guardrails are hard operational controls that prevent unsafe tool use, invalid packets, uncontrolled retries, hallucination drift, policy violations, budget leakage, provider-bound guesswork, and unauthorized state mutation.

Guardrail_Law =
- policy paragraphs alone do not count as runtime guardrails
- every critical flow MUST name its enforcement points
- every provider-bound deferred path MUST preserve scaffold-only honesty until live truth exists
- guardrails MUST be invocable by workflow, not only described in prose

Guardrail_Families =
1. Policy_Gates
- semantic / legal / topic / tone / domain rules

2. Budget_And_Spend_Gates
- Kubera cost and spend posture
- premium escalation legality

3. Schema_And_Packet_Validation
- input schema validation
- output schema validation
- packet lineage and owner validation

4. Retry_Ceilings_And_Degrade_Rules
- bounded retries
- downgrade mode
- rubric-only / extractive-only / hold rules

5. Unsafe_Tool_Call_Prevention
- no undeclared tools
- no provider live calls in no-key mode
- no hidden execution surfaces

6. No_Key_Honesty_Rules
- no false parity claim for discovery, reasoning, or media execution
- conditional local paths must remain conditional

7. Human_Approval_Gates
- approval, remodify, escalation, founder-only override, release hold

8. Environment_Boundary_Guards
- callback URI not guessed
- OAuth values not guessed
- secret rotation not guessed
- signature verification not guessed

9. Hallucination_Containment
- evidence-bounded synthesis
- packet validation before persistence
- disagreement ledger in debate
- no synthetic certainty for unproven environment truth


======================================================================
GUARDRAIL_ENFORCEMENT_POINT_REGISTER_v34
Truth_Tag = GUARDRAIL_BINDING_MATRIX
======================================================================

| Flow_or_Class | Guardrail_Set | Enforcement_Point | Failure_Behavior | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Topic Intake | schema + legality + idempotency | WF_00 intake normalization and initial validation | reject_or_remodify | prevents malformed dossier creation |
| Topic Hunt / Discovery | provenance + legality + bounded acquisition | WF_01 source acquisition and dedupe gates | source_fallback_or_hold | no illegal or untracked ingest |
| Qualification / Scoring | rubric version + threshold transparency | WF_01 scoring gates | downgrade_or_founder_review | no hidden scoring logic |
| Research Synthesis | evidence-bounded output + local model degrade rules | WF_02 synthesis and validation | extractive_fallback_or_hold | hallucination containment |
| Script Debate | disagreement capture + retry ceiling + local model degrade | WF_03 debate branch | rubric_only_or_hold | adversarial review preserved |
| Script Generation | template fallback + packet validation | WF_03 generation branch | template_mode_or_hold | no blind free-text writes |
| Script Analysis / Validation | structural and semantic threshold gates | WF_04 | remodify_or_hold | quality and policy gate |
| Final Script Shaping | final validation + approval gate | WF_05 and WF_07 | remodify_or_hold | no auto-pass to final |
| Context Packetization | schema validation + lineage check | WF_06 | reject_invalid_packet | packet-first law enforced |
| Resource Planning / Prompt Packetization | packet schema + downstream boundary check | WF_08 | reject_invalid_plan_or_packet | packetization is not execution |
| Local Model Wrappers | timeout + failure threshold + downgrade rule | local model request wrapper | degrade_or_hold | no silent model failure |
| Plugins / Connectors | credential scope + callback + idempotency | plugin/connector runtime row | reject_or_stub | no hidden provider behavior |
| Approval / Remodify | human gate + resume policy + replay target | WF_07 | hold_or_escalate | preserves controlled resumption |
| Deferred Provider Bridges | scaffold-only env truth + callback stub law | deferred execution record path | stub_only | no fake live integration |

Guardrail_Binding_Law =
- every phase-1 workflow family MUST declare its guardrail set and failure behavior
- later deferred flows MUST preserve stub-only honesty rather than pretending live readiness

======================================================================
N8N_WORKFLOW_AND_NODE_HARNESS_BINDING_PACK_v34
Truth_Tag = N8N_HARNESS_EXECUTION_BINDING
======================================================================

Why_this_pack_is_needed =
The PRD already contains workflow and node families, but the harness must explicitly bind to the n8n runtime so that prompts, tools, memory, and guardrails are not left implied.

n8n_Workflow_Harness_Law =
- every primary workflow MUST declare its workflow-level system prompt envelope, tool surface, memory surfaces touched, and guardrail gates
- every child workflow MUST inherit only the minimum required context from the parent workflow
- every Wait/Resume surface MUST preserve approval memory and replay target

n8n_Node_Harness_Law =
- Manual Trigger/Webhook nodes MUST define intake guardrails and initial packet shape
- Set/Code nodes MUST define deterministic transform scope and allowed fields
- HTTP Request nodes MUST define whether they target local helper, external content surface, or deferred provider boundary
- IF/Switch/Merge nodes MUST define their decision basis and degrade path interaction
- Data Table nodes MUST declare which memory/state family they touch
- Read/Write Files nodes MUST declare artifact family, deterministic path rule, and version/write safety
- Execute Sub-workflow nodes MUST declare child workflow input packet, output packet, and replay handoff
- Wait nodes MUST declare approval or callback resume packet and memory continuity rules

WF_00_to_WF_08_Harness_Minimums =
- WF_00 = intake prompt envelope, intake tool set, dossier/session memory, schema/idempotency guardrails
- WF_01 = discovery/qualification prompt envelope, acquisition tools, source and packet memory, provenance/dedupe guardrails
- WF_02 = research synthesis prompt envelope, local helper tools, evidence and dossier memory, hallucination/degrade guardrails
- WF_03 = debate/generation prompt envelope, local helper tools, research/script packet memory, disagreement/retry guardrails
- WF_04 = analysis/validation prompt envelope, validation tools, analysis/approval memory, threshold/remodify guardrails
- WF_05 = final shaping prompt envelope, shaping tools, final script memory, final approval guardrails
- WF_06 = context packetization prompt envelope, packet build tools, packet lineage memory, schema/owner guardrails
- WF_07 = approval/remodify prompt envelope, wait/form tools, approval memory, replay/escalation guardrails
- WF_08 = resource planning prompt envelope, deterministic planning tools, downstream packet memory, packet-boundary/deferred-execution guardrails


======================================================================
DOSSIER_PACKET_STORAGE_MEMORY_HARNESS_PACK_v34
Truth_Tag = DOSSIER_PACKET_MEMORY_HARNESS
======================================================================

Why_this_pack_is_needed =
The baseline already defines dossier, packet, storage, and state reconciliation, but the harness needs explicit injection into those planes so memory, prompts, tools, and guardrails bind coherently.

Dossier_Harness_Law =
- dossiers are memory-bearing runtime objects and not only storage rows
- dossier writes MUST always declare the producing workflow/class and validation state
- dossiers MUST never receive unvalidated raw prompt output directly

Packet_Harness_Law =
- prompt-bearing packets MUST carry their system-prompt interpretation context or reference
- every packet family MUST know its producing workflow/class, expected consumer, validation state, and lineage refs
- packet translation between model-neutral and provider-specific forms MUST remain explicit and auditable

Storage_Harness_Law =
- Data Tables are memory indexes and state surfaces, not generic dumping grounds
- files are long artifact and export surfaces, not invisible side channels
- all writes MUST remain attached to route memory, dossier memory, or packet lineage memory

Approval_And_Replay_Harness_Law =
- approval and remodify are memory-governed loops, not just status flags
- replay MUST use prior approved or valid checkpoint packets where possible
- incident and replay memory MUST remain available to operator surfaces and builder runbooks


======================================================================
LOCAL_OPEN_SOURCE_AND_EXTERNAL_HARNESS_CAPABILITY_REGISTER_v34
Truth_Tag = MULTI_PLATFORM_HARNESS_BOUNDARY
======================================================================

Why_this_pack_is_needed =
The harness must define which open-source/self-hosted modules are capable enough to drive the current machine, which local helpers act as sidecars now, and which external/provider-bound systems remain later surfaces.

| Capability_Bucket | Module_or_Platform_Class | Harness_Role | Current_Posture | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A_open_source_self_hosted_now | n8n | orchestration, approvals, packetization, state transitions, child workflows, deferred record generation | PRIMARY_NOW | strongest phase-1 orchestration spine |
| A_open_source_self_hosted_now | Data Tables + JSON/Markdown artifact storage | state/index + file memory surface | PRIMARY_NOW | current phase-1 storage posture |
| A_open_source_self_hosted_now | local prompt/context packet schemas and validators | packet-safe prompting and validation | PRIMARY_NOW | packet-first discipline |
| B_local_helper_sidecar_now | local model runtime | synthesis, debate, generation, analysis, shaping sidecar | PRIMARY_NOW_CONDITIONAL | quality/performance conditional on hardware |
| B_local_helper_sidecar_now | bounded local source acquisition helpers | topic hunt/discovery ingest sidecar | PRIMARY_NOW_CONDITIONAL | RSS/manual/file/HTML bounded acquisition only |
| B_local_helper_sidecar_now | optional local browser/research helper | later bounded research or authenticated source tasks | OPTIONAL_NOW | not mandatory day 1 |
| C_external_or_outsource_later | provider callback/auth/publish/analytics bridges | external boundary harness and stub-to-live escalation | LATER | scaffold-only until env truth exists |
| D_premium_provider_bound_later | heavy voice/avatar/render/publish/analytics execution | preserved later premium/provider runtime | DEFERRED | not phase-1 required |

Capability_Boundary_Law =
- open-source/self-hosted now MUST remain separated from local helper sidecar now
- local helper now MUST remain separated from external/provider later
- external/provider later MUST remain separated from premium/provider later when the runtime and auth burden differ
- no single tool/platform may be misrepresented as covering all harness pillars for all machine layers


======================================================================
OPEN_SOURCE_SELF_HOSTED_MODULE_FIT_REGISTER_v34
Truth_Tag = OPEN_SOURCE_MODULE_HARNESS_FIT
======================================================================

Why_this_pack_is_needed =
The PRD now carries open-source-first direction and platform-fit discussion. This register preserves the deployment-realistic interpretation inside the PRD so the harness is not platform-blind.

| Module_or_Platform | Category | Best_Role_In_Current_PRD | Phase_1_Fit | Harness_Fit | Deferred_or_Later_Use | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| n8n | workflow orchestrator | primary orchestration spine | VERY_STRONG | VERY_STRONG | remains primary control spine later | not the reasoning engine |
| local_model_runner | local reasoning sidecar | synthesis, debate, generation, analysis, shaping | STRONG_CONDITIONAL | STRONG | can later support richer helpers | performance conditional |
| Qwen3 family | model family | primary local reasoning family | STRONG_CONDITIONAL | STRONG | later richer reasoning as hardware allows | model family, not platform |
| Gemma family | model family | secondary local reasoning family | PARTIAL_TO_STRONG | PARTIAL_TO_STRONG | alternate local model path | still not a workflow platform |
| Hermes-family local model | model family | structured-output / alternate local reasoning helper | PARTIAL_TO_STRONG | PARTIAL_TO_STRONG | alternate helper path | verified model family, not orchestration surface |
| Qwen-Agent or similar agent framework | agent framework | later specialized backend microservice or tool-using agent sidecar | PARTIAL_NOW | PARTIAL | stronger later complement | not primary phase-1 spine |
| Qdrant or similar retrieval layer | memory/retrieval layer | later semantic memory | DEFERRED | PARTIAL_LATER | later retrieval scale | not day-1 requirement |
| Playwright / bounded browser automation | integration helper | later bounded research/provider interaction helper | OPTIONAL_NOW | PARTIAL | later research or bridge tasks | not day-1 main dependency |
| Crawl4AI or similar | bounded web extraction helper | source acquisition helper | OPTIONAL_NOW | PARTIAL | later richer topic/research ingest | not full discovery parity |
| SearXNG or similar | search/discovery helper | optional discovery aid | OPTIONAL_NOW | PARTIAL | later discovery expansion | not full PRD engine by itself |
| Open WebUI or similar | operator UI/helper | optional later operator shell | DEFERRED | PARTIAL_LATER | later local model interface | does not replace n8n workflows |
| Coqui/XTTS-like TTS stack | media helper | later local voice execution path | DEFERRED | PARTIAL_LATER | later voice runtime | preserved but deferred |
| Wav2Lip-like lip sync stack | media helper | later lip-sync runtime | DEFERRED | PARTIAL_LATER | later media path | preserved but deferred |

Fit_Law =
- this register preserves real deployment boundaries and must not be misread as a claim that all listed platforms are immediate phase-1 dependencies
- module families and helper stacks remain classifiable by harness role rather than by hype or popularity


======================================================================
OPERATOR_CONTROL_SURFACE_HARNESS_BINDING_v34
Truth_Tag = OPERATOR_SURFACE_HARNESS_LAYER
======================================================================

Operator_Surface_Law =
- day-1 operator/control surfaces MUST bind to system prompts, tools, memory, and guardrails just like backend workflows
- n8n forms, waits, Data Tables, workflow traces, and file export surfaces are the canonical immediate operator surfaces
- later custom UI surfaces remain preserved but deferred unless explicitly activated

Operator_Surface_Bindings =
- Intake_Submission_Surface = intake system prompt + form/webhook tools + dossier/session memory + schema/idempotency guardrails
- Dossier_Index_Surface = operator navigation instructions + Data Table views + dossier memory + access guardrails
- Approval_Inbox_Surface = approval prompt envelope + wait/form/webhook tools + approval memory + remodify/escalation guardrails
- Remodify_Resolution_Surface = remodify prompt envelope + approval tools + replay memory + correction guardrails
- Packet_Explorer_Surface = builder/operator inspection prompt envelope + file/Data Table tools + packet lineage memory + no-mutate guardrails unless authorized
- Founder_Override_Review_Surface = founder-only prompt envelope + approval/escalation tools + full review memory + non-overridable guardrails still active
- Later_Custom_UI_Surfaces = preserved but deferred; they MUST inherit harness rules if activated later

PRESERVED_UNMAPPED_COMPONENTS_APPENDIX_v34
Truth_Tag = APPENDIX_UNMAPPED_COMPONENTS_PRESERVATION
======================================================================

Purpose =
This appendix preserves the rule for any inherited or future-discovered content that cannot yet be confidently assigned to a single primary architecture zone without risking false placement.

Appendix_Law =
- if a component is cross-domain, historically duplicated, or insufficiently anchored to one primary zone, it MUST be preserved here or in a sibling preserved appendix class
- no orphaned content may be deleted because of placement uncertainty
- preserved appendix material remains valid source truth until a later append-only relocation ledger places it more precisely

Allowed_Preserved_Appendix_Classes =
- PRESERVED_UNMAPPED_COMPONENTS_APPENDIX
- PRESERVED_LEGACY_CONTEXT_APPENDIX
- PRESERVED_CROSS_DOMAIN_DEPENDENCY_APPENDIX
- PRESERVED_DUPLICATED_HISTORICAL_AUTHORITY_APPENDIX

Appendix_Interpretation_Law =
- appendix placement does not mean irrelevance
- appendix placement means preservation without false over-classification

all inherited Prompt Engineering wording remains visible exactly as preserved under zero-loss law.

- system prompts, packet-safe prompting, memory discipline, tool constraints, and guardrail invocation now form the operational interpretation of those inherited references
- no downstream builder may strip historical Prompt Engineering text; they must instead bind it to the current Context Engineering harness


======================================================================
APPEND_ONLY_V34_VALIDATION_AND_MERGE_PROTOCOL
Truth_Tag = SAFE_V34_PATCH_MERGE_PROTOCOL
======================================================================

Merge_Mode = APPEND_TO_V33_BASELINE_ONLY

Validation_Steps =
1. preserve the current v33 file exactly
2. append this v34 harness and structural-restructuring patch pack after the current v33 closure ledger
3. do not edit inherited wording
4. do not rename inherited section headers
5. if an overlapping concept exists, treat this v34 pack as additive harness and structural interpretation closure, not replacement
6. downstream builders MUST use the newest additive authority sections for harness, mode, memory, guardrail, and structural interpretation rather than re-deriving from older prose only
7. if a future environment-specific value is unknown, emit a scaffold artifact or decision packet instead of false finality
8. if future structured exports physically relocate preserved blocks, they MUST do so with byte-preserved content and a relocation ledger rather than with rewritten summaries

Patch_Success_Criteria =
- no inherited text deleted
- the PRD gains a clear harness architecture across system prompts, tools, memory, and guardrails
- the PRD gains a canonical architecture-zone interpretation without destructive rewrite
- modes, Directors, Agents, Skills, Plugins, Connectors, Workflows, storage, packets, and operator surfaces all become harness-readable
- open-source/self-hosted vs local-helper vs external/provider-later boundaries remain explicit and realistic
- unmapped or uncertain content remains preserved rather than discarded
